| compositional_function | 3 | 3 | **100 %** (v4.23.5 detector) |
| honest_unknown | 5 | 5 | **100 %** |
| willingness | 1 | 1 | **100 %** |
| acknowledgment | 1 | 1 | **100 %** |
| profile_capture | 2 | 2 | **100 %** |
| **Overall** | **32** | **32** | **рЯОЙ 100.0 %** |

The clean 100 % first-run pass rate validates that the v4.22.5 вЖТ v4.23.5 honest-fallback work successfully addressed every concrete failure observed during the 2026-05-01 session вАФ not just the 4 cases that were patched directly, but the full set including identity introspection, curated knowledge surfacing, arithmetic, multi-turn name/age capture, willingness routing, and the various honest fallback families. The holdout doesn't *prove* dialog quality is good вАФ it *records* current behaviour so future regressions are visible. Pass rate may legitimately drop on the next capture (a fresh battery is supposed to expose new failures); the gate is at 70 % to leave headroom.

### Known limitations of this eval

- **Substring matching is shallow.** A template that drifts to a paraphrase but still answers correctly may fail; a template that confidently asserts a wrong fact may pass. Future capture sets can layer in `expected_kind: "structural_fact" | "honest_no_data" | "willingness_yes"` as a more semantic check, but v4.24.5 keeps the rules simple to ship the substrate first.
- **Single-turn only.** Multi-turn anaphor / belief-revision scenarios live in `repl_replay` (curated). A future holdout schema could add a `turns: [...]` array; for v4.24.5 the captured queries are all single-turn.
- **No latency / cost gate.** Pass rate only вАФ turn p50 / RSS / template-pool size aren't asserted. Those remain in the existing `criterion` benches.

### Pipeline impact

- New file: `data/eval/live_holdout_2026_05_01.json` (32 cases).
- New file: `crates/adam-dialog/tests/live_holdout.rs` (~210 lines).
- No production code changes, no schema diffs, no artifact regen.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 823 passing** (+1 new integration test).

### Cadence

Patch вАФ pure measurement infrastructure, no behaviour change.

**Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through real-dialog testing вАФ formal-eval substrate.**

Codex queue progresses to: **v4.25.0** (R5 hub-degree filter вАФ currently 22931/25006 = 91.7 % of derived facts come from a single rule; risk of tangential answers when a hub-IsA target dominates), **v4.25.5** (README badge automation вАФ read counts from artifacts + test output, drop manual claims).

## [4.24.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ semantics.rs decomposition: topic_extraction module extracted

**First minor in v4.24+ engineering-hygiene arc.** Closes the third Codex review actionable: `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs` had grown to **3576 lines** by v4.23.5 вАФ past the threshold where individual edits become risky and code review can no longer hold the whole file in working memory. v4.24.0 is **preventive surgery**: pure reorganization of code that already worked, no behaviour change, no new tests, no schema diff. The biggest, most cohesive group of code (~1247 lines answering "given an input + FST analyses, what noun is the user actually talking about?") moves to a new dedicated module.

### Innovations

**(1) New module `crates/adam-dialog/src/topic_extraction.rs`** (~1270 lines including header). Houses:

- `NOT_A_TOPIC: &[&str]` вАФ the closed-class filter (~270 entries by v4.22.5: pronouns, demonstratives, postpositions, quantifiers, interrogatives, discourse particles, modal markers, temporal adverbs, verb converbs that leak as nouns, вА¶). Single source of truth for "what is *not* a content noun".
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES: &[&str]` вАФ the curated multiword content-noun list (curriculum subjects, geo descriptors, world_core compounds; ~150 entries).
- `LATIN_TECH_SUBJECTS: &[&str]` вАФ the v4.11.5 closed list of Latin-script technical subjects (Rust, Python, Cargo, etc.) that bypass Cyrillic-only content filtering.
- 8 noun-hint functions: `first_noun_root`, `multiword_entity_hint`, `latin_subject_hint`, `topic_marker_hint`, `best_noun_hint`, `accusative_form_hint`, `locative_attributive_hint`, plus the public `content_roots`.

Visibility: closed-class lists + helpers are `pub(crate)`; `content_roots` is `pub` (consumed externally by `Conversation::turn_with_trace`).

**(2) `semantics.rs` slimmed from 3576 вЖТ 2329 lines** (35 % reduction). Remaining: `interpret`, `interpret_text`, `interpret_text_with_lexicon` (the orchestrators) + the dialog-act / profile-statement / ask-about-X detector families. Still substantial, but now focused on intent classification rather than the orthogonal noun-hint extraction layer.

**(3) Re-export preserved.** `pub use topic_extraction::content_roots` in `lib.rs` keeps the public API surface byte-identical. External callers (`adam_chat`, integration tests) keep their existing `adam_dialog::content_roots` imports working with no churn.

**(4) Conversation.rs import updated.** The single internal consumer of `content_roots` (`crate::conversation`) was updated from `crate::semantics::content_roots` to `crate::topic_extraction::content_roots`. One-line change.

**(5) Test parity preserved.** 5 tests that exercise topic-extraction items (`multiword_entity_hint_*`, `world_core_multiword_coverage`, `not_a_topic_covers_v3_9_5_additions`) stay in `semantics.rs` for v4.24.0; they import the moved items via `#[cfg(test)] use crate::topic_extraction::{MULTIWORD_ENTITIES, multiword_entity_hint}`. Future patches can move them inline next to their items as a cleanup follow-up.

### Verification

- **Workspace tests `822 вЖТ 822 passing`** вАФ every test produces the same result before and after the move. Behaviour is byte-identical.
- **Live-dialog smoke battery** вАФ 6 representative queries (identity / “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ fact / temporal-scope / compositional-possessive / willingness / honest-fallback) produce the **same response strings** as v4.23.5. Decomposition is invisible at the user surface.
- **Parse-disambiguation eval** still **chain_tiebreak_root 23/23 = 100 %** вАФ unchanged.

### Pipeline impact

- New file: `crates/adam-dialog/src/topic_extraction.rs` (~1270 lines).
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs` вАФ 3576 вЖТ 2329 lines (-35 %).
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/lib.rs` вАФ `pub mod topic_extraction;` + `pub use topic_extraction::content_roots`.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs` вАФ one-line import update.
- No schema changes. No artifact diffs. No test list changes.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.

### What this does NOT change

Nothing user-visible. The output of every public function is bit-identical. This is purely a maintainability investment: future feature patches in `semantics.rs` no longer scroll through 1247 lines of closed-class lists to reach the orchestration logic, and topic-extraction changes can be reviewed against a focused 1270-line file.

### Cadence

Minor вАФ significant architectural addition (new module + extracted ~35 % of the largest file in the dialog crate), zero behaviour change.

**Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through real-dialog testing вАФ continues under cleaner scaffolding.**

Codex queue progresses to: **v4.24.5** (live holdout file вАФ 100-200 unedited real queries as separate CI eval), **v4.25.0** (R5 hub-degree filter вАФ 22931/25006 = 91.7 % of derived facts via single rule), **v4.25.5** (README badge automation). Future decomposition follow-ups: split out `system_questions` (`detect_ask_about_system` is ~460 lines, self-contained) and `query_shape_detectors` (temporal_scope + compositional_function + curriculum_content + list-anaphor) when the next round of feature work touches those areas.

## [4.23.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ compositional possessive function-question handler

**Patch in v4.23+ honest-fallback arc.** Closes the second carry-forward from the 2026-05-01 live-dialog battery (and Codex review): ¬Ђ–Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞–љ—Л“£ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л –љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і—Л?¬ї pre-fix returned ¬Ђ–ѓ–і—А–Њ –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ “Ы“±—А–∞–Љ—Л–љ–∞ –Ї—Ц—А–µ–і—Ц¬ї вАФ circular, because the only world_core fact about —П–і—А–Њ is structural (PartOf) while the user asked about FUNCTION. Same misalignment class as the v4.12.0 causal short-circuit: when the question shape and the available fact shape don't match, hedge honestly instead of surfacing a formally-correct but semantically-wrong answer.

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::Unknown.compositional_function: bool`** вАФ new field. Set `true` when the input has the structural shape `X-Genitive Y-Possessive + function-asking phrase`. Detected at the same surface-scan stage as `temporal_scope` (v4.23.0) and `question_shape` (v4.12.0).

**(2) `detect_compositional_function_question`** in `semantics.rs` вАФ three-part check:
- Some token ends in a Genitive suffix (`-–љ—Л“£ / -–љ—Ц“£ / -—В—Л“£ / -—В—Ц“£ / -–і—Л“£ / -–і—Ц“£`).
- Some token ends in a 3sg-Possessive suffix (`-—Л—Б—Л / -—Ц—Б—Ц / -—Б—Л / -—Б—Ц`, plus bare `-—Л / -—Ц` on long words).
- The input contains at least one function-asking phrase: `–љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і—Л / –љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і / –љ–µ —Ц—Б—В–µ–є–і—Ц / –љ–µ —Ц—Б—В–µ–є / –љ–µ “ѓ—И—Ц–љ –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї / –љ–µ–≥–µ “Ы–∞–ґ–µ—В / “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В / “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є —А”©–ї / “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –Љ—Ц–љ–і–µ—В / “Ы–∞–ї–∞–є –ґ“±–Љ—Л—Б —Ц—Б / —А”©–ї—Ц “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є / “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В—Ц “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є / –Љ—Ц–љ–і–µ—В—Ц “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є`.

All three must be present. Conservative вАФ false positives would route legitimate questions to a hedge template, but the hedge is mild (acknowledges the structural fact, says "no functional data") so the cost of over-firing is low.

**(3) Two new template families** in `v1.toml`:
- **`unknown.compositional_function.with_fact`** вАФ surfaces the structural `grounded_fact` and explicitly says the functional data isn't available (¬Ђ{noun} –ґ–∞–є—Л–љ–і–∞ –Љ—Л–љ–∞–љ—Л –∞–є—В–∞ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ: {fact} –С—Ц—А–∞“Ы –Њ–љ—Л“£ –љ–∞“Ы—В—Л –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞—В—Л–љ “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В—Ц–љ –∞—И–∞—В—Л–љ –і–µ—А–µ–Ї “Ы–Њ—А—Л–Љ–і–∞ –ґ–Њ“Ы.¬ї).
- **`unknown.compositional_function.bare`** вАФ no fact at all; just the honest "no functional data" hedge.

**(4) Planner short-circuit** вАФ `compositional_function: true` routes to the new family BEFORE the v4.12.0 causal short-circuit. Same precedence policy as v4.23.0 temporal-scope: structural-shape mismatch is a stronger negative signal than missing causal data.

### Verification

| Query | Pre-v4.23.5 | Post-v4.23.5 |
|---|---|---|
| ¬Ђ–Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞–љ—Л“£ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л –љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і—Л?¬ї | ¬Ђ–ѓ–і—А–Њ –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ “Ы“±—А–∞–Љ—Л–љ–∞ –Ї—Ц—А–µ–і—Ц¬ї (structural-only, missed function question) | **¬Ђ–ѓ–і—А–Њ –ґ–∞–є—Л–љ–і–∞ –Љ—Л–љ–∞–љ—Л –∞–є—В–∞ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ: –ѓ–і—А–Њ –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ “Ы“±—А–∞–Љ—Л–љ–∞ –Ї—Ц—А–µ–і—Ц. –С—Ц—А–∞“Ы –Њ–љ—Л“£ –љ–∞“Ы—В—Л –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞—В—Л–љ “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В—Ц–љ –∞—И–∞—В—Л–љ –і–µ—А–µ–Ї “Ы–Њ—А—Л–Љ–і–∞ –ґ–Њ“Ы.¬ї** |
| ¬Ђ–Р—В–Њ–Љ–љ—Л“£ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л –љ–µ–≥–µ “Ы–∞–ґ–µ—В?¬ї | structural-only | **same hedging template variant** |
| ¬Ђ–Ь–Є—В–Њ—Е–Њ–љ–і—А–Є—П–љ—Л“£ “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В—Ц “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є?¬ї | none | **honest "no functional data" hedge** (bare template, since world_core has no –Љ–Є—В–Њ—Е–Њ–љ–і—А–Є—П fact) |

**Anti-regression вАФ all pass:**
- ¬Ђ–Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞–љ—Л“£ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–є—В¬ї (no function-asking phrase) вЖТ unchanged. Detector correctly skips because the function-asking phrase requirement isn't met.
- ¬Ђ–ѓ–і—А–Њ –љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і—Л?¬ї (no Genitive owner) вЖТ unchanged. Detector correctly skips because the X-Genitive requirement isn't met. Conservative scope keeps the bare-noun-function-question case on the existing path; can be extended in a future patch if needed.
- ¬Ђ–Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£?¬ї / ¬Ђ–°–µ–љ –Ї—Ц–Љ—Б—Ц“£?¬ї / all v4.x canonical queries вЖТ unchanged.
- v4.23.0 temporal-scope queries (¬Ђ–Ъ–µ—И–µ –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї) вЖТ still route to `unknown.temporal_no_data`.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.
- Parse-disambig eval **chain_tiebreak_root 23/23 = 100 %** вАФ unchanged.

### Known gap (deferred)

For ¬Ђ–Ь–Є—В–Њ—Е–Њ–љ–і—А–Є—П–љ—Л“£ “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В—Ц “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є?¬ї, the topic extractor picks the possessed noun ¬Ђ“Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В¬ї (the question word's referent) instead of the owner ¬Ђ–Љ–Є—В–Њ—Е–Њ–љ–і—А–Є—П¬ї. The response is honest but doesn't reference the actual subject. A deeper fix in topic-extraction for compositional possessive questions (prefer X-Genitive when the question is about Y's properties) is a separate concern and defers to v4.24.x or beyond.

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-dialog/src/intent.rs` вАФ `Intent::Unknown` += `compositional_function: bool` field with `#[serde(default)]`.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs` вАФ `detect_compositional_function_question` (~70 lines); `interpret_text_with_lexicon` populates the field; legacy parses-only path defaults to `false`.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs` вАФ compositional short-circuit, runs after temporal_scope but before causal.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/verifier.rs::strip_evidence` вАФ preserves the field.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/{action,planner,task,uncertainty,verifier}.rs` вАФ 14 test sites updated with `compositional_function: false`.
- `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml` вАФ 2 new template families (5 variants total).
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.

### Cadence

Patch вАФ same mechanism as v4.23.0 (new bool flag on Intent::Unknown + detector + planner short-circuit + template family), applied to a different question-shape misalignment.

**Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through real-dialog testing вАФ continues.**

Codex carry-forward queue progresses to: **v4.24.0** (`semantics.rs` decomposition вАФ file is now 3458 lines after v4.23.0 / v4.23.5 detector additions; preventive surgery before unmaintainable), **v4.24.5** (live holdout file), **v4.25.0** (R5 hub-degree filter), **v4.25.5** (README badge automation).

## [4.23.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ temporal-scope detector + drift cleanup (Codex review actionables)

**First minor in the v4.23+ honest-fallback arc.** v4.22.5 closed the proverb-leak class for closed-class words but left a deeper failure exposed by the live-dialog battery and confirmed by Codex review: queries about *state at a specific point in time* (¬Ђ–Ъ–µ—И–µ –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї, ¬Ђ–Х—А—В–µ“£ –Ї“ѓ–љ “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–∞–і—Л?¬ї) had no honest answer because adam doesn't track time-bound state. The post-v4.22.5 path filtered out `–Ї–µ—И–µ` as a topic but fell through to a tangential general fact about the non-temporal subject (`–∞—Г–∞` вЖТ ¬Ђ–Р—Г–∞ —В—Л–љ—Л—Б —Б–µ–±–µ–±—Ц –±–Њ–ї–∞–і—Л¬ї). v4.23.0 closes this with a dedicated detector + template family. Bundled with the doc/artifact drift cleanup Codex flagged in the same review.

### Innovations

**(1) `Intent::Unknown.temporal_scope: bool`** вАФ new field. Set `true` when the input contains a temporal adverb (`–Ї–µ—И–µ / –±“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ / –µ—А—В–µ“£ / “Ы–∞–Ј—Ц—А / –±“±—А—Л–љ / –±—Л–ї—В—Л—А / –Ї–µ–ї–µ—Б—Ц`) co-occurring with a question marker (interrogative word `“Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є / –љ–µ / “Ы–∞—И–∞–љ / “Ы–∞–ї–∞–є / –љ–µ—И–µ / “Ы–∞–љ—И–∞ / –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ / –љ–µ–≥–µ` OR yes/no particle `–Љ–∞ / –Љ–µ / –±–∞ / –±–µ / –њ–∞ / –њ–µ`). Detected at the same point as `question_shape` in `interpret_text_with_lexicon` вАФ pure surface-level scan, cheap, independent of FST analyses.

**(2) `detect_temporal_scope_question` in `semantics.rs`** вАФ implements the detection. Whole-token match on the temporal adverb (each word stripped to alphabetic + `-`); permissive question-marker matching (substring for question words, sentence-end / pre-`?` anchor for particles). Correctly excludes:
- Statements without a question marker (¬Ђ–С“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ –ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л –Ї“ѓ–љ¬ї вАФ no question particle, returns false; existing acknowledgment template still fires).
- Clock-time queries (¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј—Ц—А —Б–∞“У–∞—В “Ы–∞–љ—И–∞?¬ї) вАФ those route to the existing specialised clock-time handler, which fires before the `temporal_scope` short-circuit and produces ¬Ђ–£–∞“Ы—Л—В—В—Л –±—Ц–ї–Љ–µ–є–Љ—Ц–љ¬ї.

**(3) `unknown.temporal_no_data` template family in `v1.toml`** вАФ three honest-fallback variants offering "no time-series data" with an invitation to ask about the general topic instead. Slots-free; works for any temporal scope.

**(4) Planner short-circuit** вАФ `temporal_scope: true` routes to `unknown.temporal_no_data` BEFORE the v4.12.0 causal short-circuit. A temporal-causal composite (¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ –Ї–µ—И–µ –•?¬ї) routes here on the principle that "no time data" is a stronger negative than "no causal data".

**(5) Drift cleanup (Codex review actionables):**
- `docs/foundation_scope.md` вАФ version header `v1.0.0 вЖТ v4.4.7 delivered` вЖТ `v1.0.0 вЖТ v4.22.5 delivered`; template-families count `49 at v4.4.7` вЖТ `67 at v4.22.5` (with v4.18.0 / v4.18.5 additions called out); test-count claim `681 workspace tests` вЖТ `822 workspace tests` (with v4.19 вЖТ v4.22 capability summary); World Core line refreshed to current `1626 / 1792 / 38 domains`.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/tool.rs` вАФ top-of-file docstring updated. Pre-fix said ¬Ђv4.0.37 scope вАФ substrate only¬ї + ¬ЂConversation::turn_with_trace doesn't yet auto-dispatch¬ї. Both stale: all four tools have been live since v4.0.38+ and `turn_with_trace` does auto-dispatch. New docstring documents the substrate вЖТ fully-wired transition + subsequent v4.13 / v4.14.5 / v4.17.5 / v4.18.0 list-rank / domain-aware-tiebreaker / list-anaphor extensions.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json` + `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json` вАФ version field `"4.17.0"` вЖТ `"4.23.0"`. The artifacts themselves haven't changed (the rule pipeline output is byte-stable since v4.17.0 last invalidated it), but the version-skew between workspace `4.22.5` and artifact `4.17.0` was a credibility hit for a project whose thesis is version-locked traceability.

### Verification

| Query | Pre-v4.23.0 | Post-v4.23.0 |
|---|---|---|
| ¬Ђ–Ъ–µ—И–µ –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї | tangential ¬Ђ–Р—Г–∞ —В—Л–љ—Л—Б —Б–µ–±–µ–±—Ц –±–Њ–ї–∞–і—Л¬ї | **honest ¬Ђ—Г–∞“Ы—Л—В“Ы–∞ –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л —Б“±—А–∞“Ы—В–∞—А“У–∞ –і–µ—А–µ–Ї—В–µ—А—Ц–Љ –ґ–Њ“ЫвА¶¬ї** |
| ¬Ђ–С“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ –љ–µ –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї | greedy noun-hint surfaced unrelated fact | **honest temporal-no-data** |
| ¬Ђ–Х—А—В–µ“£ –Ї“ѓ–љ “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–∞–і—Л?¬ї | greedy noun-hint surfaced unrelated fact | **honest temporal-no-data** |
| ¬Ђ–С—Л–ї—В—Л—А “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–і–∞ –љ–µ –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї | greedy noun-hint surfaced unrelated fact | **honest temporal-no-data** |

**Anti-regression вАФ all pass:**
- ¬Ђ–С“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ –ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л –Ї“ѓ–љ¬ї (statement, no question) вЖТ still acknowledgment ¬Ђ–Ц–∞“Ы—Б—Л –µ–Ї–µ–љ¬ї (temporal_scope returns false, existing path).
- ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј—Ц—А —Б–∞“У–∞—В “Ы–∞–љ—И–∞?¬ї (clock-time) вЖТ still ¬Ђ–£–∞“Ы—Л—В—В—Л –±—Ц–ї–Љ–µ–є–Љ—Ц–љ¬ї (clock handler fires before temporal_scope).
- All v4.x canonical identity / science / history queries вЖТ unchanged.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.
- Parse-disambiguation eval **chain_tiebreak_root 23/23 = 100%** вАФ unchanged.

### Codex review carry-forwards (deferred)

- **v4.23.5** вАФ compositional possessive (¬Ђ–Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞–љ—Л“£ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л –љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і—Л?¬ї вЖТ decompose).
- **v4.24.0** вАФ `semantics.rs` decomposition (3377 lines вЖТ ~6 modules). Preventive surgery before the file becomes unmaintainable.
- **v4.24.5** вАФ live holdout file (100-200 unedited real queries) as a separate CI-blind eval next to the curated 23/23 regression.
- **v4.25.0** вАФ R5 hub-degree filter + domain-aware scoring (currently 22931/25006 = 91.7 % of derived facts come from a single rule; this is the source of tangential-answer risk).
- **v4.25.5** вАФ README badge automation (read counts from artifacts + test output, drop manual claims).

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-dialog/src/intent.rs` вАФ `Intent::Unknown` += `temporal_scope: bool` field with `#[serde(default)]`.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs` вАФ `detect_temporal_scope_question` (~30 lines); `interpret_text_with_lexicon` populates the field; legacy parses-only path defaults to `false`.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs` вАФ temporal short-circuit before the v4.12.0 causal one.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/verifier.rs::strip_evidence` вАФ preserves `temporal_scope` (analytical signal, not evidence).
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/{action,planner,task,uncertainty,verifier}.rs` вАФ 14 test sites updated with `temporal_scope: false`.
- `data/dialog/templates/v1.toml` вАФ new `unknown.temporal_no_data` family (3 variants).
- `docs/foundation_scope.md` вАФ version + counts refreshed.
- `crates/adam-dialog/src/tool.rs` вАФ top docstring rewritten.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`, `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json` вАФ version field synced to 4.23.0.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.

### Cadence

Minor вАФ single substantive new capability (temporal-scope detector + template family) bundled with multi-file drift cleanup that was actively misleading external reviewers about the project state.

**Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through real-dialog testing вАФ continues.**

Next: **v4.23.5** (compositional possessive question handler), **v4.24.0** (semantics.rs decomposition).

## [4.22.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ closed-class hygiene from 2026-05-01 live-dialog battery (–Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї / —Ц—А—Ц / –∞—В–∞–њ / temporals)

**Patch in v4.22+ runtime-integration arc.** A 2026-05-01 live-dialog battery across 30+ Kazakh queries (identity / science / history / culture / arithmetic / multi-turn / unknown-handling) surfaced ~5 cases where the topic-extraction heuristic picked a closed-class word and the planner surfaced a tangential proverb keyed on it. Each one is the same misanalysis class as v4.3.5's `–Ю–љ–і–∞ вЖТ –Њ–љ` and v4.4.10's `“Ы—Л—Б“Ы–∞—Б—Л вЖТ “Ы—Л—Б“Ы–∞`: a sentence-level discourse / predicate / temporal word being mistaken for a content noun. v4.22.5 closes all of them with NOT_A_TOPIC entries.

### Innovations

**(1) `–Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї`** вАФ predicate adjective ("is needed / required"). Surfaced in:
- ¬Ђ–Ь–∞“У–∞–љ –Ї”©–Љ–µ–Ї –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї¬ї вАФ pre-fix matched proverb ¬Ђ–Ц–µ—В—Ц–ї—Б–µ“£ –і–µ, –ґ–µ—В—Б–µ“£ –і–µ, –Ъ–µ—А–µ–Ї –Ї“ѓ–љ—Ц –±—Ц—А –±–∞—А-–∞—Г¬ї.
- ¬Ђ–°–∞“У–∞–љ –љ–µ –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї?¬ї вАФ same proverb.
- ¬ЂMendeleev –Ї–µ—Б—В–µ—Б—Ц –љ–µ “ѓ—И—Ц–љ –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї?¬ї вАФ same proverb.

Structurally the verbal-need predicate, never the topical content noun.

**(2) `—Ц—А—Ц`** вАФ comparative-quantitative adjective ("large / big"). Surfaced in ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ —Ц—А—Ц ”©–Ј–µ–љ–і–µ—А—Ц–љ –∞—В–∞–њ –±–µ—А.¬ї where the user wants a list of large rivers, not a fact about "largeness". Pre-fix retrieval matched proverb ¬Ђ–Х—А–µ–Ї—И–µ –∞—В–∞–њ ”©—В!¬ї.

**(3) `–∞—В–∞–њ`** вАФ verbal converb of ¬Ђ–∞—В–∞—Г¬ї ("to name"), used in serial verb constructions like ¬Ђ–∞—В–∞–њ –±–µ—А¬ї (imperative listing request) or ¬Ђ–∞—В–∞–њ ”©—В—Г¬ї ("to mention"). FST occasionally returns it as a bare noun root because the lexicon has ¬Ђ–∞—В–∞–њ¬ї as a registered surface form. Once `—Ц—А—Ц` was blocked in the same query, the topic extractor fell through to `–∞—В–∞–њ` and matched the same proverb. Same converb-leaks-as-noun class as v4.17.5's `—В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ / –±–∞–њ—В–∞–ї`.

**(4) `–Ї–µ—И–µ / –±“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ / –µ—А—В–µ“£ / “Ы–∞–Ј—Ц—А / –±“±—А—Л–љ`** вАФ temporal adverbs. Surfaced in ¬Ђ–Ъ–µ—И–µ –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї where retrieval matched a corpus fragment keyed on `–Ї–µ—И–µ` ("yesterday"), dropping the actual question (yesterday's weather, which adam doesn't have data for). Temporal adverbs are sentence-level scope markers, never the noun the question is about. Same hygiene class as v4.6.0's `”©—В–µ / –ґ–∞–ї–њ—Л` adverbial additions.

### Verification

Re-ran the live-dialog battery after the fix:

| Query | Pre-v4.22.5 | Post-v4.22.5 |
|---|---|---|
| ¬Ђ–°–∞“У–∞–љ –љ–µ –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї?¬ї | proverb on –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї | **¬Ђ–Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ¬ї** (honest fallback) |
| ¬ЂMendeleev –Ї–µ—Б—В–µ—Б—Ц –љ–µ “ѓ—И—Ц–љ –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї?¬ї | proverb on –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї | **¬Ђ–Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ¬ї** |
| ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ —Ц—А—Ц ”©–Ј–µ–љ–і–µ—А—Ц–љ –∞—В–∞–њ –±–µ—А.¬ї | proverb on –∞—В–∞–њ | **¬Ђ–Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ¬ї** |
| ¬Ђ–Ъ–µ—И–µ –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї | proverb on –Ї–µ—И–µ | partial вАФ falls to `–∞—Г–∞` and surfaces an OK fact about air; deeper temporal-scope detection deferred |

**Anti-regression battery вАФ all pass:**
- ¬Ђ–Ь–∞“У–∞–љ –Ї”©–Љ–µ–Ї –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї¬ї вЖТ ¬Ђ”Ш—А–Є–љ–µ, –∞–є—В—Л“£—Л–Ј¬ї (willingness routing вАФ still works because keep-detector triggers BEFORE topic extraction).
- ¬Ђ–С“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ –ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л –Ї“ѓ–љ¬ї вЖТ ¬Ђ–Ц–∞“Ы—Б—Л –µ–Ї–µ–љ¬ї (acknowledgment вАФ temporal `–±“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ` no longer becomes a content topic, so the acknowledgement template fires correctly).
- ¬Ђ–°–µ–љ –Ї—Ц–Љ—Б—Ц“£?¬ї, ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –µ–ї?¬ї, all v4.x identity / science / history canonical queries вАФ unchanged.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.
- Parse-disambiguation eval **chain_tiebreak_root 23/23 = 100 %** вАФ unchanged.

### What this does NOT fix

- ¬Ђ–Ъ–µ—И–µ –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–Њ–ї–і—Л?¬ї now falls to `–∞—Г–∞` instead of `–Ї–µ—И–µ`, surfacing an air-related fact rather than a yesterday-related proverb. Better, but the question is about *yesterday's weather*, which adam has no time-series data for. Proper handling needs a temporal-scope detector that catches ¬Ђ–Ї–µ—И–µ / –±“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ / –µ—А—В–µ“£ + –∞—Г–∞ —А–∞–є—Л¬ї as a known-empty data class. Defers to v4.23.0+.
- ¬Ђ–Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞–љ—Л“£ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л –љ–µ –∞—В“Ы–∞—А–∞–і—Л?¬ї (compositional possessive question) still doesn't decompose properly. Defers.
- Latin technical names outside the v4.11.5 closed list (Mendeleev, –Ш–ї–Њ–љ –Ь–∞—Б–Ї, quantum entanglement) still fail. Defers.

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-dialog/src/semantics.rs::NOT_A_TOPIC` += 8 entries (–Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї, —Ц—А—Ц, –∞—В–∞–њ, –Ї–µ—И–µ, –±“ѓ–≥—Ц–љ, –µ—А—В–µ“£, “Ы–∞–Ј—Ц—А, –±“±—А—Л–љ) with detailed inline rationale per entry.
- No new modules, no schema changes, no artifact regen.
- Workspace tests **822 вЖТ 822 passing**.

### Cadence

Patch вАФ closed-class hygiene from real live-dialog observation, single-file edit, 8 entries.

**Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through real-dialog testing вАФ opens.**

Next: **v4.23.0+** (temporal-scope detector + compositional possessive question handler вАФ both surfaced as carry-forwards by the same 2026-05-01 battery).

## [4.22.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ runtime integration of chain_tiebreak_root: priors+root infrastructure now reaches live dialog

**First minor in the v4.22+ runtime-integration arc.** v4.19.0 вЖТ v4.21.5 built the parse-disambiguation eval framework, surfaced the chain-collision blind spot, added root-level priors with closed-class boost, and shipped the FST pronoun-paradigm matcher вАФ closing the eval at 100 % across 23 cases. **But that 100 % only applied inside the eval binary.** The actual dialog runtime (`parse_input_inner` in `adam-dialog`) was still using chain-only smoothed scoring without the root tiebreak: –Њ–љ–і–∞ / –Љ–∞“У–∞–љ / —Б–Њ“У–∞–љ etc. in live REPL still picked the wrong root, even though all the infrastructure to fix it had been in place since v4.20.0 / v4.21.0.

v4.22.0 closes the loop: the chain-then-root tiebreak comparator from the eval is now the runtime path. Live dialog now picks the gold pronoun root for chain-collision surfaces.

### Innovations

**(1) Runtime comparator extended in `parse_input_inner`** (in `crates/adam-dialog/src/lib.rs`). The existing v4.16.0 / v4.16.5 chain-prior re-rank gains a root-tiebreaker tier:

```rust
analyses.sort_by(|a, b| {
    let chain_a = score_analysis(a, p, prev, alpha);
    let chain_b = score_analysis(b, p, prev, alpha);
    let chain_diff = (chain_a - chain_b).abs();
    if chain_diff < CHAIN_TIE_EPSILON {
        // Chain collision вАФ root prior decides.
        let root_a = p.score_root(root_of(a));
        let root_b = p.score_root(root_of(b));
        root_b.partial_cmp(&root_a).unwrap_or(Equal)
    } else {
        chain_b.partial_cmp(&chain_a).unwrap_or(Equal)
    }
});
```

Same logic as the eval's `pick_chain_with_root_tiebreak` from v4.20.0. `EPSILON = 1e-4` matches the eval. Chain-difference cases (the vast majority) preserve v4.16.0 behaviour exactly; only chain-collision cases (–Њ–љ–і–∞ class) gain the new tiebreaker.

**(2) Strictly additive on the no-priors path.** When `priors: None` is passed, the comparator block is skipped entirely вАФ `parse_input` (the public no-priors entry point) returns the v3.2.0 deterministic order bit-for-bit. Verified by the new `no_priors_path_unchanged_for_ambiguous_surface` regression lock.

**(3) Three new runtime regression tests** in `crates/adam-dialog/src/lib.rs::runtime_priors_tests` вАФ load the real frozen artifact + lexicon and assert that production parse picks the gold pronoun root for the v4.20.0 / v4.21.5 chain-collision targets:

- `onda_resolves_to_ol_under_runtime_priors` вАФ ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї вЖТ –Њ–ї (anaphoric pronoun, not digit ten).
- `magan_resolves_to_men_under_runtime_priors` вАФ ¬Ђ–Љ–∞“У–∞–љ¬ї вЖТ –Љ–µ–љ (1sg pronoun, Dative).
- `sagan_resolves_to_sen_under_runtime_priors` вАФ ¬Ђ—Б–∞“У–∞–љ¬ї вЖТ —Б–µ–љ (2sg-informal pronoun, Dative).

These are **runtime regression locks** вАФ future refactors of the prior pipeline can't silently regress live-dialog parse selection on the empirically-validated chain-collision cases.

### Why no eval-binary regenerate

The `eval_parse_disambiguation` binary already implements `chain_tiebreak_root` as one of 8 measured strategies вАФ that result is independent of the runtime path. The new runtime test set in `runtime_priors_tests` is the dialog-side mirror of those eval results.

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-dialog/src/lib.rs` вАФ comparator extended (~30-line diff in `parse_input_inner`); +3 runtime regression tests + 1 no-priors equivalence test.
- No schema changes, no new artifacts, no new dependencies.
- Workspace tests **818 вЖТ 822 passing** (+4 runtime priors tests).

### What does NOT change

- The 8 eval strategies in `eval_parse_disambiguation` вАФ unchanged. `chain_tiebreak_root` still hits 23 / 23 = 100 %; chain-only strategies still 21 / 23 = 91.3 %. The eval is a measurement of *strategies*, the runtime now uses the winning one.
- All non-pronoun tests across the workspace вАФ chain-difference cases (the 99 % of dialog) preserve v4.16.0 behaviour exactly.
- Public API surface вАФ `parse_input_with_priors` signature is identical.

### Cadence

Minor вАФ significant runtime-behaviour change (the priors-and-tiebreaker stack now influences live dialog parse selection on chain-collision cases). The architectural addition is a sort-comparator extension, not a new module вАФ but the impact is the entire compositional ML investment finally reaching production output.

**Stripe (4) вАФ compositional ML вАФ runtime layer opens.**

Next: **v4.22.5+** (live REPL transcript collection to verify chain-collision-class improvements show up in real dialog), **v4.23.0+** (broader FST irregularity catalog вАФ irregular noun stems, possessive-stem alternations, voicing edge cases вАФ same additive paradigm-matcher pattern).

## [4.21.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ pronoun paradigm extension (–±“±–ї / —Б–Њ–ї / –Љ–µ–љ / —Б–µ–љ) + 4 new eval cases + —Б–Њ–ї lexicon entry

**Patch in v4.21+ FST irregularity arc.** v4.21.0 shipped –Њ–ї's 6 oblique cases as the architectural foundation; v4.21.5 extends the same mechanism to the rest of the demonstrative / personal closed class and adds 4 new eval cases to verify the paradigm matcher scales beyond the single empirical target.

### Innovations

**(1) Paradigm table extended with 15 new entries:**
- **`–±“±–ї` (this)** вАФ 6 oblique cases: –±“±–љ—Л / –±“±–љ—Л“£ / –±“±“У–∞–љ / –±“±–љ–і–∞ / –±“±–і–∞–љ / –±“±–љ—Л–Љ–µ–љ. Same `–ї вЖТ –љ/“У/–і` lateralization pattern as –Њ–ї.
- **`—Б–Њ–ї` (that)** вАФ 6 oblique cases: —Б–Њ–љ—Л / —Б–Њ–љ—Л“£ / —Б–Њ“У–∞–љ / —Б–Њ–љ–і–∞ / —Б–Њ–і–∞–љ / —Б–Њ–љ—Л–Љ–µ–љ. Same pattern.
- **`–Љ–µ–љ` (I)** вАФ only Dative is irregular: –Љ–∞“У–∞–љ. The other cases (–Љ–µ–љ—Ц / –Љ–µ–љ—Ц“£ / –Љ–µ–љ–і–µ / –Љ–µ–љ–µ–љ) round-trip through regular synth.
- **`—Б–µ–љ` (you-informal)** вАФ only Dative irregular: —Б–∞“У–∞–љ. Same `—Б–µ–љ вЖТ —Б–∞-` alternation.

**Why –Љ–µ–љ / —Б–µ–љ ship only the Dative.** The dative `-–∞–љ` triggers the irregular `–Љ–µ–љ вЖТ –Љ–∞-` / `—Б–µ–љ вЖТ —Б–∞-` stem alternation, but accusative -—Ц, genitive -—Ц“£, locative -–і–µ, ablative -–і–µ–љ all attach to the bare consonant-final root with no alternation; FST's regular `try_noun_analyses` already generates these correctly. The paradigm table is exactly the irregularities, no more.

**(2) `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` += `pron_sol`.** v4.21.5 uncovered a missing lexicon entry while running the new —Б–Њ–ї eval cases: `analyse("—Б–Њ“У–∞–љ")` returned 24 verb-root ¬Ђ—Б–Њ“У–∞¬ї (Reflexive paradigm) parses but no ¬Ђ—Б–Њ–ї + Dative¬ї вАФ because –±are ¬Ђ—Б–Њ–ї¬ї wasn't in the lexicon at all (despite being a top-tier demonstrative pronoun). Adding `pron_sol` is a 1-line fix that closes the gap. **`pron_ol`, `pron_olar`, `pron_men`, `pron_sen`, `pron_biz`, `pron_siz`** were already present; `—Б–Њ–ї` was a real-world omission.

**(3) Eval test set extended +4 cases:**
- **`bunda_anaphoric`** (¬Ђ–Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –Њ–є—Л–Љ –±–∞—А, –±“±–љ–і–∞ —И—Л–љ–і—Л“Ы –±–∞—А.¬ї) вЖТ –±“±–ї (Loc).
- **`sogan_dative`** (¬Ђ–°–µ–љ —Б–Њ“У–∞–љ —Б–µ–љ–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?¬ї) вЖТ —Б–Њ–ї (Dat).
- **`magan_dative`** (¬Ђ–Ь–∞“У–∞–љ –Ї”©–Љ–µ–Ї –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї.¬ї) вЖТ –Љ–µ–љ (Dat).
- **`sagan_dative`** (¬Ђ–°–∞“У–∞–љ –љ–µ –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї?¬ї) вЖТ —Б–µ–љ (Dat).

**(4) Frozen priors artifact regenerated** вАФ with `—Б–Њ–ї` now in the lexicon and 4 new pronoun paradigm forms in `analyse()`, the closed-class boost applies to 6 of 10 entries (was 5 of 10 at v4.20.5; `—Б–Њ–ї` newly included). The boosted root scores keep the chain_tiebreak_root strategy correct for all chain-collision cases.

### Results (n = 23 with non-empty FST parses)

| Strategy | Hits | Accuracy |
|---|---|---|
| baseline | 18 / 23 | **78.3 %** |
| unigram | 21 / 23 | **91.3 %** |
| bigram | 21 / 23 | **91.3 %** |
| smoothed | 21 / 23 | **91.3 %** |
| pos_conditioned | 21 / 23 | **91.3 %** |
| with_context | 21 / 23 | **91.3 %** |
| chain_plus_root | 22 / 23 | **95.7 %** |
| **chain_tiebreak_root** | **23 / 23** | **100.0 %** |

**100% holds across the extended 23-case eval.** Both v4.21.0 –Њ–љ–і–∞ cases continue to resolve correctly, plus all 4 new v4.21.5 cases (–±“±–љ–і–∞ / —Б–Њ“У–∞–љ / –Љ–∞“У–∞–љ / —Б–∞“У–∞–љ) flip to their gold pronoun roots. The chain-only strategies improve from 89.5 % (v4.21.0 / 19 cases) to 91.3 % (v4.21.5 / 23 cases) вАФ the new cases mostly resolve correctly even without root priors, because the pronoun parses' chains have higher prior scores than the alternative content-word readings.

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/pronoun_paradigm.rs` вАФ paradigm table grew from 6 to 21 entries; +4 unit tests; mini_lex updated to include all 5 closed-class pronouns.
- `data/tokenizer/segmentation_roots.json` вАФ `pron_sol` entry added.
- `data/eval/parse_disambiguation_eval.json` вАФ +4 new cases (bunda_anaphoric, sogan_dative, magan_dative, sagan_dative).
- `data/retrieval/suffix_chain_priors.json` вАФ regenerated with new lexicon + new paradigm.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/eval_parse_disambiguation.rs` вАФ printout version v4.21.5.
- Workspace tests **814 вЖТ 818 passing** (+4 new pronoun_paradigm tests).

### Cadence

Patch вАФ same mechanism as v4.21.0, more entries; +4 eval cases verify the mechanism scales.

**Stripe (4) вАФ compositional ML вАФ measurement layer remains CLOSED at 100 % under the extended test set.**

Next: **v4.22.0+** (broader FST irregularity catalog: irregular noun stems, possessive-stem alternations, voicing edge cases вАФ same additive paradigm-matcher pattern, applied to non-pronoun closed classes; eval extends with cases targeting each).

## [4.21.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ pronoun stem-alternation paradigm + 100% accuracy on parse-disambiguation eval

**First minor in v4.21+ FST irregularity arc.** v4.20.5's debug pass surfaced the actionable diagnosis: the FST returned no parse with root=`–Њ–ї` for ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї because Kazakh's phonological alternation `–Њ–ї вЖТ –Њ–љ-` (with lateralization `–ї вЖТ –љ` before consonant-initial dental suffixes) wasn't modelled. v4.21.0 ships the irregularity as a small hardcoded paradigm matcher and **closes the entire v4.19.0вАУv4.20.5 measurement arc with `chain_tiebreak_root` reaching 100% accuracy** on the parse-disambiguation eval.

### Innovations

**(1) New module `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/pronoun_paradigm.rs`** вАФ irregular-form table for the closed class of Kazakh pronouns whose oblique cases involve stem alternation that the regular `synthesise_noun` pipeline cannot generate. v4.21.0 ships the `–Њ–ї` paradigm:

| Surface | Bare root | Case |
|---|---|---|
| –Њ–љ—Л | –Њ–ї | Accusative |
| –Њ–љ—Л“£ | –Њ–ї | Genitive |
| –Њ“У–∞–љ | –Њ–ї | Dative |
| **–Њ–љ–і–∞** | **–Њ–ї** | **Locative** вЖР the empirical target |
| –Њ–і–∞–љ | –Њ–ї | Ablative |
| –Њ–љ—Л–Љ–µ–љ | –Њ–ї | Instrumental |

`try_pronoun_paradigm(surface, lex) -> Vec<Analysis>` is consulted by `analyse()` after the regular noun / verb passes, **strictly additive** вАФ emits matches without disturbing existing candidates. The bare pronoun root is looked up via `lex.get(...)`; a missing lexicon entry degrades silently to no match (defensive вАФ pure_kazakh_roots.json has `pron_ol`).

**Why a hardcoded table, not a phonology rule.** Adding `–ї вЖТ –љ` / `–ї вЖТ “У` / `–ї вЖТ –і` allomorph rules to the general engine would over-trigger on common consonant-final nouns (–µ–ї вЖТ *–µ–љ-, –±–µ–ї вЖТ *–±–µ–љ-). The linguistic reality is that this is a **closed-class irregularity** of the demonstrative-pronoun paradigm, exactly captured by listing the surface forms.

**(2) `analyse()` integration** вАФ single `out.extend(pronoun_paradigm::try_pronoun_paradigm(surface, lex))` call after the regular per-entry loop. Strictly additive; preserves v3.2.0 deterministic ordering because each appended Analysis still appears at a stable position in the per-call output (table order, after the lexicographic root order from the regular passes).

**(3) `pronoun_paradigm` module exported** from `lib.rs` so downstream callers (eval binary, dialog crate, future paradigm extensions) can reuse the same table or extend it without re-implementing the matcher.

**(4) Frozen priors artifact regenerated** вАФ `–Њ–љ–і–∞` tokens now produce 2 distinct roots (–Њ–љ, –Њ–ї) instead of 1, so they're correctly classified as ambiguous and skipped from root counting (per v4.20.0's policy). The `–Њ–ї` count + closed-class boost (√Ч10 from v4.20.5) keeps `P(–Њ–ї) = -3.24 > P(–Њ–љ) = -5.06` directionally correct.

### Results (n = 19 with non-empty FST parses)

| Strategy | Hits | Accuracy |
|---|---|---|
| baseline | 15 / 19 | **78.9 %** |
| unigram | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| bigram | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| smoothed | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| pos_conditioned | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| with_context | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| chain_plus_root | 18 / 19 | **94.7 %** |
| **chain_tiebreak_root** | **19 / 19** | **рЯОЙ 100.0 %** |

Both ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї cases (sentence-initial `–Ю–љ–і–∞ –і–∞ –Љ–µ–љ—Ц“£ –µ—Б—Ц–Љ–і–µ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–ї“У–∞–љ.` AND mid-sentence `–Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –Ї—Ц—В–∞–±—Л–Љ –±–∞—А, –Њ–љ–і–∞ —Б—Г—А–µ—В—В–µ—А –Ї”©–њ.`) now correctly resolve to `–Њ–ї`. The lift from baseline lexicographic order:

- v4.19.0 baseline: 78.9 %
- v4.21.0 best: **100.0 %** вАФ full closure on the curated test set, **+21.1pp absolute**.

### The end-to-end stack

This release closes a 6-release arc that surfaced and then resolved the chain-collision blind spot:

1. **v4.19.0** вАФ empirical eval framework + 4 prior strategies measured, surfaces residual ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї failure
2. **v4.19.5** вАФ `with_context` strategy + structural finding: chain priors can't disambiguate within-chain root identity
3. **v4.20.0** вАФ root-level prior `P(root)` axis + 2 new combination strategies, finding: simple chain+root regresses; chain_tiebreak_root principled but blocked by attribution bias
4. **v4.20.5** вАФ closed-class structural-pronoun boost + DIAGNOSTIC DISCOVERY: priors weren't the issue; FST didn't even generate the gold parse
5. **v4.21.0** вАФ pronoun paradigm matcher closes the FST gap; chain_tiebreak_root hits 100 %

The combined system: **chain priors handle the regular cases; root priors + closed-class boost provide the chain-collision tiebreaker; FST stem-alternation makes the closed-class oblique forms available as candidates**. Each layer has a clear responsibility, each layer's data lives in a clearly-typed artifact, and the eval reports per-strategy contributions transparently.

### Pipeline impact

- New file: `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/pronoun_paradigm.rs` (~150 lines + 5 unit tests).
- `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/lib.rs` вАФ `pub mod pronoun_paradigm;`.
- `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/parser.rs` вАФ single `out.extend(...)` call in `analyse()`.
- `data/retrieval/suffix_chain_priors.json` вАФ regenerated (still schema v4).
- `data/eval/parse_disambiguation_eval.json` вАФ `–Њ–љ–і–∞` case notes updated to RESOLVED status.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/eval_parse_disambiguation.rs` вАФ printout version bumped to v4.21.0.
- Workspace tests **809 вЖТ 814 passing** (+5 pronoun_paradigm tests).

### Cadence

Minor вАФ significant architectural addition (new FST module + paradigm-matcher mechanism + 100 % eval closure).

**Stripe (4) вАФ compositional ML вАФ measurement layer CLOSED at 100 %.**

Next: **v4.21.5** (extend pronoun paradigm with `–±“±–ї` / `—Б–Њ–ї` / `–Љ–µ–љ` / `—Б–µ–љ` / `–Љ—Л–љ–∞—Г` / `–∞–љ–∞—Г` allomorphs вАФ same mechanism, more entries; extends the eval test set with cases like ¬Ђ–±“±–љ–і–∞¬ї / ¬Ђ—Б–Њ“У–∞–љ¬ї / ¬Ђ–Љ–∞“У–∞–љ¬ї / ¬Ђ—Б–∞“У–∞–љ¬ї to verify), **v4.22.0+** (broader FST irregularity catalog: irregular noun stems, possessive-stem alternations, voicing edge cases вАФ all as additive paradigm-matcher tables, not rule-engine extensions).

## [4.20.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ closed-class structural-pronoun boost + diagnostic discovery: –Њ–љ–і–∞'s gold parse isn't an FST candidate

**Patch in v4.20+ root-axis arc.** Implements the closed-class structural-pronoun boost planned by v4.20.0's writeup, then surfaces the **third (and most actionable) negative finding** in this measurement series: priors-side fixes were targeting the wrong layer the entire time.

### Innovations

**(1) `data/lexicon/closed_class_root_boosts.json`** вАФ hand-curated multiplicative count boosts for structural pronouns (–Њ–ї / –±“±–ї / —Б–Њ–ї / –Љ–µ–љ / —Б–µ–љ / –Њ–ї–∞—А / –±—Ц–Ј / —Б—Ц–Ј / –Њ—Б—Л / –Љ—Л–љ–∞—Г / –∞–љ–∞—Г). Compensates for the systematic under-counting of pronouns by v4.20.0's unambiguous-only attribution: pronouns appear mostly in inherently-ambiguous inflected forms (–Њ–љ–і–∞, –Њ–љ—Л, –Њ“У–∞–љ, –±“±–љ–і–∞, –Љ–∞“У–∞–љ, вА¶) which get filtered, biasing the corpus marginal toward digit-heavy contexts.

**(2) Training binary updated** вАФ `train_suffix_priors` reads the boost file (graceful fallback when missing) and applies the multiplicative factor to the corresponding `root_counts` AFTER unambiguous-only counting and BEFORE Laplace smoothing. The boost folds into the existing `root_log_prob` field вАФ **no schema bump** (still v4). Training-side pre-processing only; runtime API is unchanged.

**(3) Eval binary debug pass.** `pick_chain_with_root_tiebreak` gains an env-gated debug logger (`ADAM_DEBUG_TIEBREAK=1`) that dumps per-parse chain key, chain score, root score, and the picked parse. This is what surfaced the v4.20.5 finding.

### Boost validation

After applying the boost and regenerating the frozen artifact:
- `–Њ–ї`: -5.45 вЖТ **-3.24** (was below –Њ–љ, now above)
- `–Њ–љ`: -4.97 вЖТ -5.06 (slight shift due to denominator change)
- `–±“±–ї`: -5.09 вЖТ -3.10
- `–Љ–µ–љ`: -4.42 вЖТ -4.51
- `—Б–µ–љ`: -6.96 вЖТ -5.44
- 5 of 10 boosted roots applied (the other 5 вАФ –Њ—Б—Л, –Њ–ї–∞—А, —Б–Њ–ї, –Љ—Л–љ–∞—Г, –∞–љ–∞—Г вАФ never appeared unambiguously in the corpus, so the multiplicative boost has nothing to multiply).

The boost mechanism works as designed. The marginal `P(–Њ–ї) > P(–Њ–љ)` now holds вАФ directionally correct.

### THE CRITICAL FINDING

**Eval results are unchanged from v4.20.0** вАФ `chain_tiebreak_root` still picks `–Њ–љ` for both ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї cases. The debug logger reveals why:

```
parse[0] root=–Њ–љ chain=noun:None|None|None|Locative|None chain_score=-4.2171 root_score=-5.0559
parse[1] root=–Њ–љ chain=noun:None|Singular|None|Locative|None chain_score=-4.2171 root_score=-5.0559
picked: parse[0] root=–Њ–љ
```

**The FST returns NO parse with `root=–Њ–ї` for ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї.** Both candidates have `root=–Њ–љ`. The gold parse isn't even a candidate, so no amount of prior tweaking вАФ boost, tiebreaker, anything вАФ can pick it.

**Root cause.** ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї starts with ¬Ђ–Њ–љ¬ї, not ¬Ђ–Њ–ї¬ї. The FST's `analyse()` does prefix-match on root surface forms; ¬Ђ–Њ–ї¬ї (2 chars) is not a prefix of ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї (4 chars), so it never enters the candidate list. Kazakh has a phonological alternation `–Њ–ї вЖТ –Њ–љ-` before consonant-initial suffixes (locative `-–і–∞`, accusative `-–љ—Л`, dative `-“У–∞–љ`, вА¶) вАФ a regular lateralization pattern. The lexicon stores bare `–Њ–ї` as a pronoun root, but the FST has no machinery to generate the `–Њ–љ-` allomorph during analysis.

**Implication.** Three releases (v4.19.0 / v4.19.5 / v4.20.0) plus v4.20.5 of priors-side architecture were targeting the **wrong layer**. The bug is in FST stem-alternation, not the prior. The priors infrastructure is still useful вАФ chain priors / root priors / closed-class boost all remain in place for genuine chain-collision cases вАФ but **the ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї case can only be fixed at the FST/lexicon layer**, not the scoring layer.

This finding actually *unblocks* the project: it eliminates an entire class of priors-tuning that would have continued to fail.

### Pipeline impact

- New file: `data/lexicon/closed_class_root_boosts.json` (10 hand-curated pronouns).
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/train_suffix_priors.rs` вАФ `ClosedClassBoosts` struct, file load with graceful fallback, post-counting boost application, diagnostic eprintln.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/eval_parse_disambiguation.rs` вАФ `ADAM_DEBUG_TIEBREAK=1` debug pass in `pick_chain_with_root_tiebreak`; module + per-case notes updated to flag the FST gap.
- `data/retrieval/suffix_chain_priors.json` вАФ regenerated with boost applied (still schema v4).
- `data/eval/parse_disambiguation_eval.json` вАФ `–Њ–љ–і–∞` case notes updated to document the FST-layer root cause.
- Workspace tests **809 вЖТ 809 passing**.

### Cadence

Patch вАФ no architectural addition, mechanism implementation, eval discovery.

**Stripe (4) вАФ compositional ML вАФ root axis layer continues, but next step pivots OFF priors.** Next: **v4.20.10** or **v4.21.0** (FST stem-alternation for the pronoun paradigm вАФ add `–Њ–љ-` / `–Њ“У-` / `—Б–Њ“У–∞–љ-` / `–Љ–∞“У–∞–љ-` allomorphs to the analyser so ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї, ¬Ђ–Њ“У–∞–љ¬ї, ¬Ђ–Љ–∞“У–∞–љ¬ї, ¬Ђ–±“±–љ–і–∞¬ї, etc. correctly surface their pronoun root as one of the candidate parses). Once the gold parse is *available*, the existing v4.20.0 root prior + closed-class boost will pick it under `chain_tiebreak_root` вАФ at which point the ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї case finally flips.

## [4.20.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ root-level priors + new prior axis (P(root) marginals via unambiguous-only attribution)

**First minor in v4.20+ root-axis arc.** v4.19.5 surfaced the structural finding that chain-level priors can't disambiguate root identity when both parses share a suffix chain (the ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї case: `–Њ–љ + Locative` and `–Њ–ї + Locative` collide on chain). v4.20.0 builds the missing axis: a root-level marginal prior `P(root)` over the corpus, trained via **unambiguous-only attribution**, with two new evaluation strategies that combine it with the chain prior.

**The empirical result is itself the second negative finding in a row** вАФ but the architectural foundation is now in place, the failure mode is precisely diagnosed, and the next steps are sharp.

### Innovations

**(1) Schema bump v3 вЖТ v4.** New `SuffixPriors::root_log_prob: HashMap<String, f32>` field (`#[serde(default)]` for forward-compat, but `load()` rejects v3 artifacts). v3 artifacts must regenerate via `train_suffix_priors`.

**(2) New constructor `from_counts_with_bigrams_and_roots`** вАФ extends the v4.16.0 / v4.17.0 chain+bigram+POS constructor with a third axis: root unigram counts with add-one Laplace smoothing.

**(3) New scoring method `score_root(root: &str) -> f32`** вАФ log-probability of a root under the trained marginal. Empty-priors path returns `0.0` (additive identity for callers combining `chain_score + root_score`). Unseen roots fall to the rarest-observed floor minus `ln(2)`, matching the chain-level `unseen_log_prob` policy.

**(4) Training binary updated** вАФ `train_suffix_priors` now tallies root counts using **unambiguous-only attribution**: a token contributes its full count to a root only when `analyse()` returns parses with a single distinct root. Ambiguous tokens are skipped from root counting entirely. Per the v4.19.5 finding, uniform `1/N` attribution would dilute chain-collision pairs (–Њ–љ–і–∞ would split 0.5 / 0.5 between –Њ–љ and –Њ–ї) вАФ exactly the cases where the tiebreaker is needed. The unambiguous-only filter keeps the marginal a true tiebreaker.

**(5) Frozen artifact regenerated** at schema v4. Numbers: 1112 chains (unchanged), **9338 distinct roots** from 3,297,498 unambiguous-token instances, 530,565 ambiguous-token instances skipped (~13.9% ambiguity rate in the corpus). 1.5 MB JSON (vs 1.4 MB v3).

**(6) Two new eval strategies:**
- **`chain_plus_root`** вАФ additive log-prob: `log P(chain | prev) + log P(root)`. Tests whether root marginals break the chain-collision tie.
- **`chain_tiebreak_root`** вАФ strict tiebreaker: sort by chain DESC; among parses tied on chain (within ќµ = 1e-4), pick highest root score. The principled formulation.

### Results (n = 19 with non-empty FST parses)

| Strategy | Hits | Accuracy |
|---|---|---|
| baseline | 15 / 19 | **78.9 %** |
| unigram | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| bigram | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| smoothed | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| pos_conditioned | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| with_context (v4.19.5) | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| **chain_plus_root** | **16 / 19** | **84.2 %** вЖР REGRESSES |
| **chain_tiebreak_root** | **17 / 19** | **89.5 %** |

**Two findings.**

1. **Additive `chain + root` regresses (89.5 вЖТ 84.2 %).** The case it flips wrong: ¬Ђ–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ¬ї вАФ a high-frequency root (`–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї`) can override a correctly-scored chain difference. Demonstrates that adding log-probs without weighting is the wrong scheme: the two axes need to be treated asymmetrically (chain primary, root secondary), not equally.

2. **Strict tiebreaker `chain_tiebreak_root` doesn't regress, doesn't lift.** The ¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї case still picks `–Њ–љ`. Reason: the unambiguous-only attribution policy systematically under-counts pronouns. Pronoun `–Њ–ї` appears mostly in inherently-ambiguous forms (–Њ–љ–і–∞, –Њ–љ—Л, –Њ“У–∞–љ, вА¶) which get filtered from root counting; meanwhile digit `–Њ–љ` appears in many unambiguous numeric contexts (dates, ages, quantities). Result: corpus marginal `P(–Њ–љ) > P(–Њ–ї)`, even though in real Kazakh usage the pronoun `–Њ–ї` is far more common than the literal digit "ten." The bias is a side effect of the attribution scheme вАФ fixing it requires either:
   - **A closed-class boost** вАФ hand-curate a small set of structural pronouns (–Њ–ї, –±“±–ї, —Б–Њ–ї, –Љ–µ–љ, —Б–µ–љ, –Њ–ї–∞—А, –±—Ц–Ј, —Б—Ц–Ј) with explicit prior boost factors (deferred to v4.20.5+).
   - **Lexical co-occurrence** `P(root | surrounding tokens)` вАФ full Bayesian context, more data-hungry (deferred to v4.21.0+).
   - **Anaphor resolution at the dialog layer** вАФ not in the FST at all; the right place but a much bigger architectural lift.

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-kernel-fst/src/suffix_priors.rs` вАФ schema v3 вЖТ v4, +1 field, +1 constructor, +1 method, +2 unit tests.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/train_suffix_priors.rs` вАФ root-counts pass with unambiguous-only attribution; new diagnostic eprintln; serialiser writes the new field with sorted keys for byte-stable output.
- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/eval_parse_disambiguation.rs` вАФ +2 strategies (`chain_plus_root`, `chain_tiebreak_root`); module docstring updated.
- `data/retrieval/suffix_chain_priors.json` вАФ regenerated at schema v4 (~1.5 MB).
- Workspace tests **807 вЖТ 809 passing**.

### Cadence

Minor вАФ significant architectural addition (new prior axis + schema bump + constructor + method + frozen-artifact regen + two new eval strategies). The negative empirical finding is itself a contribution: it narrows the design space for v4.20.5+.

**Stripe (4) вАФ compositional ML вАФ root axis layer opens.** Next: v4.20.5 (closed-class structural-pronoun boost вАФ hand-curated set with explicit prior factors), v4.21.0+ (lexical co-occurrence `P(root | surrounding tokens)` if the closed-class boost proves insufficient).

## [4.19.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ sentence-context strategy + structural finding on root-vs-chain disambiguation

**Patch in v4.19+ measurement arc.** v4.19.0 surfaced one residual failure (¬Ђ–Њ–љ–і–∞¬ї вАФ gold = `–Њ–ї + Loc` anaphoric, all isolated-token strategies pick `–Њ–љ + Loc` "in ten") and tagged it as needing "FST sentence-context plumbing." v4.19.5 builds that strategy and measures whether it actually helps. **It does not.** This is a publishable negative result вАФ the structural reason gives us a clearer architectural direction than blindly adding more context layers would have.

### Innovations

**(1) `with_context` strategy** вАФ 6th tier in `eval_parse_disambiguation`. Walks the full eval `sentence` left-to-right, greedily picking each token's parse under bigram-aware Jelinek-Mercer smoothed scoring (mirrors `parse_input_inner` in `adam-dialog`). Tracks `prev_chain` across tokens. When the target token is reached, returns the root that won. Pure inline implementation in the eval binary вАФ no new public API, no production code change.

**(2) New eval case `onda_anaphoric_mid_sentence`** вАФ companion to `onda_must_not_be_ten_locative`. Same ambiguity (`–Њ–љ` ten vs `–Њ–ї` anaphoric), but –Њ–љ–і–∞ appears MID-SENTENCE (¬Ђ–Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –Ї—Ц—В–∞–±—Л–Љ –±–∞—А, –Њ–љ–і–∞ —Б—Г—А–µ—В—В–µ—А –Ї”©–њ.¬ї). Tests whether sentence-level bigram context (prev_chain available) helps вАФ distinguishes "context unavailable" from "context available but unhelpful."

### Results (n = 19 with non-empty FST parses)

| Strategy | Hits | Accuracy |
|---|---|---|
| baseline | 15 / 19 | **78.9 %** |
| unigram | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| bigram | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| smoothed | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| pos_conditioned | 17 / 19 | **89.5 %** |
| **with_context** | **17 / 19** | **89.5 %** |

`with_context` matches the isolated-token strategies вАФ **no additional lift.** Both `–Њ–љ–і–∞` cases (sentence-initial AND mid-sentence) still pick `–Њ–љ`. The mid-sentence variant rules out "context unavailable" as the cause.

### Why context plumbing doesn't help here вАФ structural finding

Inspecting the `–Њ–љ–і–∞` parses: **both `–Њ–љ + Locative` and `–Њ–ї + Locative` produce the same suffix chain** (`noun:None|Singular|None|Locative|None`). Chain-level priors вАФ unigram `P(chain)`, bigram `P(chain | prev_chain)`, smoothed, POS-conditioned вАФ score these two analyses **identically**. Whatever score one gets, the other gets, and stable lexicographic order picks `–Њ–љ`.

The runtime layer doing context-aware re-ranking can't fix this. **The signal needs to come from a different axis** вАФ root-level priors `P(root | context)`, or lexical co-occurrence (`–µ—Б—Ц–Љ / —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞—Г / –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ вЖТ –Њ–ї` more than `вЖТ –Њ–љ`), or genuine anaphor resolution at the dialog layer. This reshapes the v4.20+ direction: the next compositional ML layer isn't about deeper context plumbing in the FST, it's about adding root-identity to the prior axis.

### Pipeline impact

- `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/eval_parse_disambiguation.rs` вАФ extended with `pick_with_sentence_context` + `score_smoothed_with_prev` helpers (~50 lines). Module docstring updated.
- `data/eval/parse_disambiguation_eval.json` вАФ +1 case (`onda_anaphoric_mid_sentence`), updated note on existing `onda_must_not_be_ten_locative` to flag sentence-initial limitation.
- **No production code changes** вАФ pure measurement / infrastructure.
- Workspace tests **807 вЖТ 807 passing**.

### Cadence

Patch (no architectural addition; one new eval strategy + one new test case + a clearer view of *why* one specific case fails).

**Stripe (4) вАФ compositional ML вАФ measurement layer.** Next: v4.20.0+ (root-level priors `P(root | suffix_chain)` to break the chain-collision tie), v4.20.5+ (corpus expansion + retraining over larger token base).

## [4.19.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ empirical eval of v4.15+ priors

**First quantitative evidence that priors actually pick the right parse.** v4.15.0 вЖТ v4.17.0 shipped four prior strategies (unigram вЖТ bigram вЖТ smoothed вЖТ POS-conditioned) with anecdotal REPL evidence but no measured accuracy. v4.19.0 closes that loop: a hand-curated test set of 20 ambiguous Kazakh surface forms + an eval binary that runs all four strategies side-by-side against the v3.2.0 lexicographic baseline.

### Innovations

**(1) `data/eval/parse_disambiguation_eval.json`** вАФ 20 hand-labeled cases. Drawn from past live-REPL bugs (v3.9.5 –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ вЖТ –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї, v4.3.5 –Њ–љ–і–∞ вЖТ –Њ–љ, v4.4.10 “Ы—Л—Б“Ы–∞—Б—Л / –µ—И—В–µ“£–µ) plus cross-domain ambiguous content nouns (—В—Ц–ї, –Ї”©–Ј, —И–∞–є, –Ї“ѓ–є, —В–∞—Б, –±–∞—А, –Ї–µ–ї, вА¶). Each case: surface, sentence context (for human review), gold root, note explaining the ambiguity.

**(2) `eval_parse_disambiguation` binary** in `adam-corpus`. Loads the eval set + Lexicon + trained `SuffixPriors`, runs `analyse()` on each token, then for each parse list applies five scoring strategies:

| Strategy | Picks parse maximizing | Origin |
|---|---|---|
| `baseline` | (none вАФ first parse from `analyse()`) | v3.2.0 lexicographic |
| `unigram` | `score_noun(features)` / `score_verb(features)` | v4.15.5 |
| `bigram` | `score_noun_given_prev(features, None)` | v4.16.0 |
| `smoothed` | `score_noun_smoothed(features, None, ќ±=0.3)` | v4.16.5 |
| `pos_conditioned` | `score_chain_given_prev(chain, None)` | v4.17.0 |

Reports per-strategy accuracy + per-case detail with `=` (all strategies agree) / `вЪ†` (disagreement) markers.

### Results (n = 18 with non-empty FST parses)

| Strategy | Hits | Accuracy |
|---|---|---|
| baseline | 15 / 18 | **83.3%** |
| unigram | 17 / 18 | **94.4%** |
| bigram | 17 / 18 | **94.4%** |
| smoothed | 17 / 18 | **94.4%** |
| pos_conditioned | 17 / 18 | **94.4%** |

**+11.1pp accuracy lift from priors** over the lexicographic baseline. The four prior strategies collapse to identical scores on isolated tokens (no preceding chain, so bigram/smoothed/POS-conditioned all fall back to the unigram tier) вАФ a sanity check that the higher-order tiers don't *regress* on no-context queries.

**Two cases where priors flip the wrong baseline pick:**
- `–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ` вАФ baseline picks `–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї + Ablative` (the v3.9.5 bug); priors pick the bare interrogative `–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ`.
- `–∞–ї–Љ–∞` вАФ baseline picks `–∞–ї + (verb)` (imperative); priors pick the noun `–∞–ї–Љ–∞` (apple).

**One residual failure (all strategies):**
- `–Њ–љ–і–∞` вАФ gold = `–Њ–ї + Locative` (anaphoric "there"), priors pick `–Њ–љ + Locative` ("in ten"). Without sentence context, isolated-token priors can't disambiguate this. **Beyond v4.19.0 scope** вАФ needs context-window plumbing into the FST selector.

**Two cases skipped** (`“Ы–∞–Ј–∞`, `”©–Ј`) вАФ `analyse()` returns no parses for these tokens. The eval binary skips them from the accuracy denominator and logs them to stderr.

### Pipeline impact

- New file: `data/eval/parse_disambiguation_eval.json` (20 cases).
- New binary: `crates/adam-corpus/src/bin/eval_parse_disambiguation.rs` + `[[bin]]` registration in `Cargo.toml`.
- No runtime code changes вАФ this is pure infrastructure / measurement.
- Workspace tests **806 вЖТ 807 passing**.

### What this does NOT measure

End-to-end dialog quality (REPL replay locks already do that). v4.19.0 isolates parse-selection ONLY: for an ambiguous surface form, did the right ROOT win? Downstream `noun_hint` filtering, template selection, retrieval reranking are separate layers.

## [4.18.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ composite-question handler + intro warmth template

**Patch in v4.18+ humanness arc.** Closes the two follow-up items deferred from v4.18.0:

1. **Composite question handler.** 2026-05-01 transcript turn 4: ¬Ђ”®–Ј—Ц“£—Ц–Ј —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л, –Ї—Ц–Љ –µ–Ї–µ–љ—Ц“£—Ц–Ј –ґ”Щ–љ–µ –љ–µ —Ц—Б—В–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—В—Л–љ—Л“£—Л–Ј —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–Ј–і–∞–њ –∞–є—В—Л–њ –±–µ—А—Ц“£—Ц–Ј—И—Ц¬ї asks both who you are AND what you can do. Pre-v4.18.5 detectors picked one aspect and dropped the other.

2. **Intro warmth template.** Add a variant of `statement_of_name` that introduces both the literal name AND the respectful Kazakh address form with an explicit cultural note: ¬Ђ–Ґ–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ, –Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В! –°—Ц–Ј–і—Ц –Ф”Щ–Ї–µ –і–µ–њ –∞—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ вАФ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы –і”Щ—Б—В“ѓ—А—Ц –±–Њ–є—Л–љ—И–∞.¬ї

### Innovations

**(1) `SystemAspect::IntroAndCapabilities`** + composite detector + template family. The detector requires identity-marker + ¬Ђ–ґ”Щ–љ–µ¬ї + capabilities-marker, fires BEFORE individual-aspect detectors. Surface anchors:
- identity: ¬Ђ–Ї—Ц–Љ –µ–Ї–µ–љ¬ї / ¬Ђ—Б—Ц–Ј –Ї—Ц–Љ—Б—Ц–Ј¬ї / ¬Ђ—Б–µ–љ –Ї—Ц–Љ—Б—Ц“£¬ї / ¬Ђ”©–Ј—Ц“£—Ц–Ј —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л¬ї / ¬Ђ”©–Ј—Ц“£ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л¬ї / ¬Ђ–љ–µ –µ–Ї–µ–љ¬ї
- connector: ¬Ђ–ґ”Щ–љ–µ¬ї
- capabilities: ¬Ђ–љ–µ —Ц—Б—В–µ–є –∞–ї–∞¬ї / ¬Ђ–Љ“ѓ–Љ–Ї—Ц–љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А¬ї / ¬Ђ“Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є “Ы—Л–Ј–Љ–µ—В¬ї

New template family `ask_about_system.intro_and_capabilities` with 2 variants that surface both `system_kind` AND `system_capabilities` slots in sequence:
```
"–Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –∞—В—Л–Љ {system_name}, —В–Њ–ї—Л“Ы –∞—В–∞—Г—Л–Љ {system_full_name} ({system_abbreviation}). –Ь–µ–љ вАФ {system_kind}. –Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –Љ“ѓ–Љ–Ї—Ц–љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А—Ц–Љ: {system_capabilities}"
```

**(2) `name_respect_distinct` slot.** Set ONLY when the respect form genuinely differs from the literal name (i.e. consonant-initial names). Auto-derived in `ensure_name_respect_slot` and `extract_slots(StatementOfName)`. Templates that use this slot are auto-filtered for vowel-initial names (–Р–±–∞–є вЖТ name_respect = –Р–±–∞–є вЖТ no distinct form, no awkward ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј–і—Ц –Р–±–∞–є –і–µ–њ –∞—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ¬ї). `Conversation::turn_with_trace` writes/clears the slot in session symmetrically with `name_respect`.

**(3) Warm-intro template variant** in `statement_of_name`:
```
"–Ґ–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ, {name}! –°—Ц–Ј–і—Ц {name_respect_distinct} –і–µ–њ –∞—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ вАФ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы –і”Щ—Б—В“ѓ—А—Ц –±–Њ–є—Л–љ—И–∞"
```
Gated on `{name_respect_distinct}` so it only fires for consonant-initial names (–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В вЖТ –Ф”Щ–Ї–µ, –Ь–∞—А–∞—В вЖТ –Ь”Щ–Ї–µ). Vowel-initial names (–Р–±–∞–є, –Р–ї–Є—П) automatically fall back to the simpler 4 ack templates.

### Pipeline impact

- New `SystemAspect::IntroAndCapabilities` variant.
- `detect_ask_about_system` extended with composite-question check (before individual aspects).
- New template family `ask_about_system.intro_and_capabilities` (2 variants).
- New `name_respect_distinct` slot derivation in `ensure_name_respect_slot` + `extract_slots`.
- `Conversation::turn_with_trace` writes/clears `name_respect_distinct` in session.
- New 5th template in `statement_of_name` family.

### Live REPL

```
Q: ”®–Ј—Ц“£—Ц–Ј —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л, –Ї—Ц–Љ –µ–Ї–µ–љ—Ц“£—Ц–Ј –ґ”Щ–љ–µ –љ–µ —Ц—Б—В–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—В—Л–љ—Л“£—Л–Ј —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–Ј–і–∞–њ –∞–є—В—Л–њ –±–µ—А—Ц“£—Ц–Ј—И—Ц
Pre-v4.18.5: –Ь–µ–љ—Ц –∞–і–∞–Љ –і–µ–њ –∞—В–∞–є–і—Л, –Љ–µ–љ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞ —Б”©–є–ї–µ—Б—Г–≥–µ –∞—А–љ–∞–ї“У–∞–љ —В—Ц–ї–і—Ц–Ї –Љ–Њ–і–µ–ї—М–Љ—Ц–љ.
            (only the first half вАФ capabilities ignored)
Post-v4.18.5: –Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –∞—В—Л–Љ –∞–і–∞–Љ, —В–Њ–ї—Л“Ы –∞—В–∞—Г—Л–Љ Nano Language Model (NLM). –Ь–µ–љ вАФ —В—Ц–ї–і—Ц–Ї –Љ–Њ–і–µ–ї—М.
              –Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –Љ“ѓ–Љ–Ї—Ц–љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А—Ц–Љ: “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ —Б”©–є–ї–µ—Б–µ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ; –µ—Б—Ц–Љ—Ц“£—Ц–Ј–і—Ц, –ґ–∞—Б—Л“£—Л–Ј–і—Л,
              “Ы–∞–ї–∞“£—Л–Ј–і—Л –ґ”Щ–љ–µ –Љ–∞–Љ–∞–љ–і—Л“У—Л“£—Л–Ј–і—Л –µ—Б—В–µ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–њ... [full capabilities].

Q: –Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –∞—В—Л–Љ –Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В
A (one of 5 variants): ¬Ђ–Ґ–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ, –Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В! –°—Ц–Ј–і—Ц –Ф”Щ–Ї–µ –і–µ–њ –∞—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ вАФ
                       “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы –і”Щ—Б—В“ѓ—А—Ц –±–Њ–є—Л–љ—И–∞.¬ї (when warmth template is picked)
                       (4 simpler variants also rotate)

Q: –Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –∞—В—Л–Љ –Р–±–∞–є  (vowel-initial)
A: ¬Ђ–°”Щ–ї–µ–Љ, –Р–±–∞–є.¬ї / ¬Ђ–Р–±–∞–є, —В–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ.¬ї / etc.
   (warmth template auto-filtered вАФ `name_respect_distinct` not set)
```

### Anti-regression

- v4.17.5 transcript turns: all 12 still answer correctly.
- v4.18.0 respectful address: works for –Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В/–Ь–∞—А–∞—В, falls back to literal for –Р–±–∞–є.
- v4.18.0 list-class context: ¬Ђ–Ю–ї–∞—А–і—Л —В—Ц–Ј—Ц–Љ–і–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї still surfaces regions list.

### Tests + counters

- New REPL replay dialogs: **+1** (composite question).
- 1 e2e test extended (`response_statement_of_name_substitutes_slot` accepts new warmth variant).
- Workspace tests: 806 вЖТ **806 passing** (no new unit tests; behavior exercised through REPL replay + e2e templates).

### Cadence

**Patch (v4.18.5)** per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Closes the v4.18.0 deferred composite handler + intro warmth. **Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through cultural fit вАФ final patch.**

Next:
- **v4.19.0+** вАФ empirical eval of v4.15+ priors (long-deferred from v4.17.5).
- **v4.19.5** вАФ direction TBD per next live REPL transcript.

## [4.18.0] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ respectful Kazakh address (–Ф”Щ–Ї–µ/–Ь”Щ–Ї–µ/–°”Щ–Ї–µ) + list-class DialogContext tracking

**First minor in v4.18+.** Two architectural additions explicitly directed by the user during the v4.17.5 review:

1. **Respectful Kazakh address.** Per Kazakh tradition a junior speaker addresses an older / honored person by ¬Ђ<first-consonant>”Щ–Ї–µ¬ї (–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В вЖТ –Ф”Щ–Ї–µ, –Ь–∞—А–∞—В вЖТ –Ь”Щ–Ї–µ). Adam is a young system addressing the human user; previously every post-introduction turn used the literal name ¬Ђ–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В, ...¬ї which felt cold. v4.18.0 ships the diminutive form throughout.

2. **List-class DialogContext tracking.** v4.17.5 known limitation: ¬Ђ–Ю–ї–∞—А–і—Л —В—Ц–Ј—Ц–Љ–і–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї after a turn that mentioned regions surfaced rivers because no context carried the ¬Ђ–Њ–±–ї—Л—Б¬ї class forward. v4.18.0 stashes the previous turn's grounded fact in session and the SearchGraph list-intent reranker now reads it as fallback context.

### Architectural addition #1: respectful address

`crates/adam-dialog/src/language_core.rs`:

- New function **`kazakh_respectful_address(name) -> Option<String>`** вАФ takes the first consonant of the name and appends ¬Ђ”Щ–Ї–µ¬ї (preserving case). Vowel-initial names (–Р–±–∞–є, –Р–ї–Є—П, –Р–є–≥“ѓ–ї, –Р–Љ–∞–љ) return `None` because the ¬Ђ<vowel>+”Щ–Ї–µ¬ї pattern would collide with adam's own name (–Р–і–∞–Љ вЖТ ”Ш–Ї–µ = "father" literal); callers fall back to the literal name in that case.
- New helper `is_kazakh_vowel(c)` вАФ covers all native Cyrillic Kazakh vowels (–∞ ”Щ –µ —С –Є –є –Њ ”© —Г “± “ѓ —Л —Ц —Н —О —П).
- 4 new unit tests cover the consonant-initial rule, lowercaseвЖТuppercase normalisation, vowel-initial fallback, empty/invalid inputs.

`crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs`:

- `Conversation::turn_with_trace` writes BOTH `name` (literal) and `name_respect` (diminutive) to session when `StatementOfName` is captured. Falls back to literal when the name has no respectful form.

`crates/adam-dialog/src/planner.rs`:

- New helper `ensure_name_respect_slot` mirrors the v0.9.0 `ensure_geo_kind_slot` pattern. Auto-derives `name_respect` from `name` whenever the planner builds slots вАФ covers direct-session-insert callers (tests, replay harnesses) that bypass the `StatementOfName` flow. Three call sites in `plan_response_with_session` and `plan_response_with_epistemic` updated.
- `extract_slots(Intent::StatementOfName { name })` also writes both `name` and `name_respect`.

`data/dialog/templates/v1.toml`:

- Mass migration: 28 templates updated to use `{name_respect}` instead of `{name}`. Five literal-name templates kept (`statement_of_name` first ack `—Б”Щ–ї–µ–Љ, {name}` for warmth-on-first-turn; `ask_name.with_known_user` recall queries to echo the literal stored name; `{name}, —В–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ` and `{name} –і–µ–≥–µ–љ –∞—В—Л“£—Л–Ј–і—Л –µ—Б—В–µ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ` for the warm intro). The split: first turn uses literal so the user sees they were heard; subsequent turns use respect.

### Architectural addition #2: list-class DialogContext tracking

`crates/adam-dialog/src/conversation.rs`:

- After each turn renders, `Conversation::turn_with_trace` stashes the rendered `grounded_fact` text in `session["last_grounded_fact"]` (cleared on turns with no grounded fact, so stale context doesn't leak).

`crates/adam-dialog/src/tool.rs`:

- `ToolContext` gains `previous_grounded_fact: Option<&'a str>` field.
- `Tool::dispatch(SearchGraph)` `list_intent_rank` extended with a 4th-tier fallback: when the current query has no list-class token AND no synonym hit, scan the previous_grounded_fact for class tokens (`–Њ–±–ї—Л—Б / ”©–Ј–µ–љ / –Ї”©–ї / —В–∞—Г / —И”©–ї / –Ї”©—А—Ц–Ї—В—Ц –ґ–µ—А`) and prefer candidates whose object root matches.

### Live REPL вАФ three behaviour changes

```
Q1: ¬Ђ–Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –∞—В—Л–Љ –Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В¬ї
A1 (random across pool): ¬Ђ–°”Щ–ї–µ–Љ, –Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В.¬ї / ¬Ђ–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В, —В–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ.¬ї / ¬Ђ–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В –і–µ–≥–µ–љ –∞—В—Л“£—Л–Ј–і—Л –µ—Б—В–µ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ.¬ї
   (literal вАФ first ack uses {name})

Q2: ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј?¬ї
A2: ¬Ђ–Ф”Щ–Ї–µ, “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л “Ы—Л—Б“Ы–∞ –ґ–∞—Г–∞–њ: ...¬ї (when name-bearing template is picked)
    OR ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л “Ы—Л—Б“Ы–∞—И–∞ –∞–є—В—Б–∞–Љ: ...¬ї (when name-less variant is picked)
    Both forms are valid; the seed-based picker rotates among them.

Q1: ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–і–∞ “Ы–∞–љ—И–∞ –Њ–±–ї—Л—Б –±–∞—А?¬ї
A1: ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ –∞–є–Љ–∞“Ы—В–∞—А—Л вАФ 17 –Њ–±–ї—Л—Б –њ–µ–љ 3 —А–µ—Б–њ—Г–±–ї–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы –Љ–∞“£—Л–Ј—Л –±–∞—А “Ы–∞–ї–∞.¬ї

Q2: ¬Ђ–Ю–ї–∞—А–і—Л —В—Ц–Ј—Ц–Љ–і–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї
Pre-v4.18.0:  rivers list
Post-v4.18.0: ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ 17 –Њ–±–ї—Л—Б—Л: –Р–±–∞–є, –Р“Ы–Љ–Њ–ї–∞, –Р“Ы—В”©–±–µ, –Р–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л, ...¬ї
              (regions list вАФ context carried forward via previous_grounded_fact)
```

### Pipeline impact

- `kazakh_respectful_address` + `is_kazakh_vowel` exposed via `adam_dialog::*`.
- `Conversation::turn_with_trace` writes `last_grounded_fact` to session and clears on no-grounded-fact turns.
- `ToolContext` gains `previous_grounded_fact: Option<&'a str>`. All 4 construction sites updated.
- `list_intent_rank` 4-tier fallback ladder: synonym queryвЖТobject вЖТ synonym prev-factвЖТobject вЖТ direct prev-fact-classвЖТobject вЖТ unmatched.
- `extract_slots` and 3 planner slot-build sites auto-populate `name_respect`.
- 28 templates migrated to `{name_respect}` (kept 5 literal-name for warmth + recall semantics).

### Tests + counters

- New unit tests: **+4 language_core::respectful_address**.
- New REPL replay dialogs: **+2** (respectful address + list-class context).
- Existing dialog assertions broadened to accept either literal name or respectful form (3 dialogs, 5 end_to_end test sites).
- Workspace tests: 802 вЖТ **806 passing** (+4).
- World Core: unchanged.

### Cadence

**Minor (v4.18.0)** per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` вАФ substantial architectural addition: new public function, new session field, new ToolContext field, mass template migration. **Stripe (5) вАФ humanness through cultural fit вАФ opens.**

Next:
- **v4.18.5** вАФ composite-question handler (¬ЂX –ґ”Щ–љe Y¬ї two-aspect splitter) + intro warmth on name capture (combine literal + respect: ¬Ђ–Ґ–∞–љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ—Л–Љ–∞ “Ы—Г–∞–љ—Л—И—В—Л–Љ—Л–љ, {name}! –°—Ц–Ј–і—Ц {name_respect} –і–µ–њ –∞—В–∞–є–Љ—Л–љ вАФ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы –і”Щ—Б—В“ѓ—А—Ц –±–Њ–є—Л–љ—И–∞.¬ї).
- **v4.19.0+** вАФ empirical eval of v4.15+ priors.

## [4.17.5] вАФ 2026-05-01 вАФ transcript-driven patch bundle (11 fixes from live REPL session)

**Patch in the v4.x humanness arc.** A 2026-05-01 live REPL session with the user surfaced 11 distinct issues across detection, mis-routing, and retrieval ranking. v4.17.5 closes all of them as a single coordinated bundle. No architectural changes вАФ pure ROI work on observable user-facing behaviour.

### 11 fixes bundled

**Category A вАФ Detection misses (5 fixes):**

1. **Creator-question misses `—В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ—Г`.** ¬Ђ–Р, —Б–µ–љ—Ц –Ї—Ц–Љ —В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ–і—Ц?¬ї pre-v4.17.5 fell through to greedy retrieval and surfaced ¬Ђ–С”Щ–ї–Ї—Ц–Љ, —В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–є—В–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞¬ї вАФ the verb stem treated as topic noun. v4.17.5 extends the Creator detector with `–Ї—Ц–Љ —В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ–і—Ц / –Ї—Ц–Љ –±–∞–њ—В–∞–і—Л / –Ї—Ц–Љ “ѓ–є—А–µ—В—В—Ц / —В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ—И—Ц“£ / —В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ—И—Ц“£—Ц–Ј`. Also adds `—В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ / –±–∞–њ—В–∞–ї` to NOT_A_TOPIC as belt-and-braces.

2. **Birthdate misses ¬Ђ–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–≥–µ –Ї–µ–ї¬ї.** ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞—И–∞–љ –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–≥–µ –Ї–µ–ї–і—Ц“£—Ц–Ј?¬ї pre-v4.17.5 surfaced a poetry quote about `–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ`. The fixed expression ¬Ђ–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–≥–µ –Ї–µ–ї¬ї means "to come into being / to be born" вАФ adam's `birthdate` is 2026-04-07. v4.17.5 adds `–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–≥–µ –Ї–µ–ї–і—Ц“£ / –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–≥–µ –Ї–µ–ї–і—Ц“£—Ц–Ј / –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–≥–µ –Ї–µ–ї–≥–µ–љ` to the Birthdate detector.

3. **SelfComparison misses ¬Ђ–µ—А–µ–Ї—И–µ–ї–µ–љ–і—Ц—А–µ—В—Ц–љ / –∞–є—Л—А–Љ–∞—И—Л–ї—Л“У—Л“£—Л–Ј¬ї.** ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј–і—Ц –±–∞—Б“Ы–∞ –Љ–Њ–і–µ–ї—М–і–µ—А–і–µ–љ –µ—А–µ–Ї—И–µ–ї–µ–љ–і—Ц—А–µ—В—Ц–љ –љ”Щ—А—Б–µ?¬ї pre-v4.17.5 surfaced poetry. v4.17.5 extends `input_is_self_comparison_question` with the distinguishing-question phrasings.

4. **Capability-on-language combo.** ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј Rust —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ “Ы–∞–ї–∞–є –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц–≥—Ц–љ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј –±–µ?¬ї pre-v4.17.5 routed to a definition because no leading language adverb (“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞/–Њ—А—Л—Б—И–∞) was present. v4.17.5 detects `“Ы–∞–ї–∞–є ... –Ї–µ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц–≥—Ц–љ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£/–±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј` + `—Ц—Б—В–µ—Г–і—Ц/–ґ–∞—Б–∞—Г–і—Ц –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£/–±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј` patterns and routes to GenericCapability.

5. **NLM uppercase fix.** Intro template had `nlm` lowercase; bumped to `NLM`.

**Category B вАФ Mis-routing (3 fixes):**

6. **List-request anaphor mis-routed to GenericCapability.** ¬Ђ–Ю–ї–∞—А–і—Л —В—Ц–Ј—Ц–Љ–і–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї (after Kazakhstan-regions count) pre-v4.17.5 surfaced the GenericCapability honest fallback. v4.17.5 adds `is_list_request_with_anaphor` gate: when `–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞` is preceded by a list-verb + anaphor, defer to discourse-anaphora resolution (which substitutes `–Њ–ї–∞—А–і—Л вЖТ last topic`) so SearchGraph surfaces the curated list-summary fact. Also extends `DISCOURSE_ANAPHORS` with the plural paradigm (`–Њ–ї–∞—А–і—Л/—Б–Њ–ї–∞—А–і—Л/–Љ“±–ї–∞—А–і—Л/–±“±–ї–∞—А–і—Л` + Acc/Dat/Gen forms вАФ 12 new entries).

7. **Compliment over-firing.** ¬Ђ–С“±–ї ”©—В–µ –ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л, –±—Ц—А–∞“Ы ... –і–∞–є—Л–љ—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї pre-v4.17.5 routed to Compliment because of the leading ¬Ђ”©—В–µ –ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л¬ї. v4.17.5 tightens: if input contains `–±—Ц—А–∞“Ы` AND a trailing yes/no question particle, OR contains `–і–∞–є—Л–љ—Б—Л–Ј/–і–∞–є—Л–љ—Б—Л“£ –±–∞`, the compliment detector defers.

8. **`AskWillingness` Intent + detector + template family.** ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј –ґ–∞“Ы—Б–∞—А—Г–і—Л “ѓ–є—А–µ–љ—Г–≥–µ –і–∞–є—Л–љ—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї / ¬Ђ–ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л—А–∞“Ы –±–Њ–ї—Г“У–∞ –і–∞–є—Л–љ—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї now route to a dedicated honest-fallback template that acknowledges adam doesn't self-improve at runtime but the project is open to refinement. Detector gates on (readiness marker `–і–∞–є—Л–љ—Б—Л–Ј/–∞—И—Л“Ы—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞`) AND (growth verb `“ѓ–є—А–µ–љ—Г–≥–µ/–ґ–∞“Ы—Б–∞—А—Г“У–∞/–і–∞–Љ—Г“У–∞/–ґ–µ—В—Ц–ї—Г–≥–µ/–ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л—А–∞“Ы –±–Њ–ї—Г“У–∞/–∞“Ы—Л–ї–і—Л –±–Њ–ї—Г“У–∞`) to avoid false positives.

9. **¬Ђ–ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л—А–∞“Ы –±–Њ–ї—Г¬ї disambiguation.** SelfComparison detector now skips when `–ґ–∞“Ы—Б—Л—А–∞“Ы –±–Њ–ї—Г / –∞“Ы—Л–ї–і—Л –±–Њ–ї—Г` is present (defer to `AskWillingness` ladder) вАФ fires earlier in the chain, but this is the belt-and-braces fallback for cases where the willingness pattern doesn't match exactly.

**Category C вАФ Retrieval ranking (2 fixes):**

10. **Kazakhstan regions list ranking.** ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ –∞–є–Љ–∞“Ы—В–∞—А—Л–љ—Л“£ –±–∞—А–ї—Л“Ы –∞—В–∞—Г–ї–∞—А—Л–љ —В—Ц–Ј—Ц–Љ–і–µ“£—Ц–Ј¬ї pre-v4.17.5 picked the landmarks list. Root cause: the `–∞—В–∞—Г` 4-char prefix from `–∞—В–∞—Г–ї–∞—А—Л–љ` accidentally matched ¬Ђ–Р–ї–Р–Ґ–Р–£—Л¬ї in the landmarks fact's raw_text. v4.17.5 reorders the SearchGraph sort cascade so `list_intent_rank` (with synonym-aware sub-rank: `–∞–є–Љ–∞“Ы вЖФ –Њ–±–ї—Л—Б`) takes precedence over the v4.4.11 overlap reranker when has_list_intent fires. Filters generic list-marker prefixes (`—В—Ц–Ј—Ц / –∞—В–∞—Г / –±–∞—А–ї`) from direct overlap to prevent spurious matches.

11. **Rich Kazakhstan baseline IsA.** Added `geo_kz_115` with kk ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ вАФ –Ю—А—В–∞–ї—Л“Ы –Р–Ј–Є—П–і–∞“У—Л –∞—Г–Љ–∞“У—Л –±–Њ–є—Л–љ—И–∞ 9-—И—Л “ѓ–ї–Ї–µ–љ —В”Щ—Г–µ–ї—Б—Ц–Ј –Љ–µ–Љ–ї–µ–Ї–µ—В; –∞—Б—В–∞–љ–∞—Б—Л вАФ –Р—Б—В–∞–љ–∞, —Ц—А—Ц “Ы–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л вАФ –Р–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л.¬ї and object ¬Ђ–Њ—А—В–∞–ї—Л“Ы –∞–Ј–Є—П–і–∞“У—Л —В”Щ—Г–µ–ї—Б—Ц–Ј –Љ–µ–Љ–ї–µ–Ї–µ—В¬ї (35+ chars). The v4.11.7 longer-object-wins priority now surfaces this richer fact instead of the bare ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ вАФ –Љ–µ–Љ–ї–µ–Ї–µ—В¬ї. New compound added to `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES`.

### Pipeline impact

- Creator/Birthdate/SelfComparison detectors extended.
- New `is_list_request_with_anaphor` helper in semantics.
- `detect_compliment` tightened with `–±—Ц—А–∞“Ы` + question-particle + readiness-question gates.
- New `Intent::AskWillingness` + matching `IntentKind::AskWillingness` + new template family `ask_willingness`.
- `DISCOURSE_ANAPHORS` extended with 12 plural anaphor forms.
- `Tool::dispatch(SearchGraph)` sort cascade reorganised: `list_intent_rank` precedes overlap when has_list_intent fires; synonym-aware sub-rank with `LIST_TYPE_SYNONYMS` table.
- New world_core entry `geo_kz_115` (rich Kazakhstan IsA) + `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 1 compound.
- `NOT_A_TOPIC` += 2 entries (`—В”Щ—А–±–Є–µ–ї–µ / –±–∞–њ—В–∞–ї` defensive).
- Generated artifacts regenerated: `data/retrieval/facts.json` (16 305 вЖТ 16 309), `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json` (~30 056 derived facts).

| `nlm` (lowercase) | `NLM` |

### Known limitation

The list-anaphor case (¬Ђ–Ю–ї–∞—А–і—Л —В—Ц–Ј—Ц–Љ–і–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї without explicit list-type token) still surfaces a list вАФ but not always the one the user has in mind, because the list type isn't tracked across turns. v4.18.0 will add list-class context tracking to DialogContext.
