## [4.11.7] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ coverage gap closure: question-particle filter + object-length priority + bare-name geo + birthdate verbs + language-capability detector

User directive 2026-04-30: confirm v4.12.0 readiness via live-REPL test. 20-question battery surfaced 5 systemic gaps that should ship as a patch before the minor bump, so v4.12.0 starts on a clean baseline.

### Innovations bundled (5 patches)

1. **Question-particle filter in `NOT_A_TOPIC`** вАФ added `–±–∞` and `–Љ–µ` (sister forms of the existing `–±–µ` / `–Љ–∞`). Real-REPL gap: ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞ —Б”©–є–ї–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї pre-v4.11.7 extracted `–±–∞` as topic and surfaced a poetry quote about `“±“Ы–њ–∞—Б—Л–љ –±–∞`. The four-form set (`–±–∞ / –±–µ / –Љ–∞ / –Љ–µ`) is the closed Kazakh interrogative-particle paradigm; the lexicon has `–±–∞` registered as a particle, but FST occasionally emits a Noun reading too.

2. **Object-length component in `user_facing_fact_priority` inverted to longer-wins** вАФ was `+(object.root.chars().count())` (ASC, shorter wins), now `-(...)` (DESC, longer wins). For "what is X?" / "tell me about X" questions, the more informative object wins: `–ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ IsA —В—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –±—Ц—А–ї—Ц–≥—Ц` (compound) over `–ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ IsA –Љ–∞—В–µ—А–Є—П` (bare noun); `—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ IsA —В–∞–±–Є“У–∞—В “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ—Л` (compound) over `—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ IsA “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ`. Pre-v4.11.7 the v4.11.6 length tiebreaker never fired because this priority tier already chose the scant version.

3. **Bare-name geo aliases + case-insensitive subject lookup** вАФ three new geo_kz entries (`geo_kz_112..114`) with bare subjects `–ґ–µ—В—Ц—Б—Г`, `–∞“Ы–Љ–Њ–ї–∞`, `“±–ї—Л—В–∞—Г` (oblasts only known via compound subject `<X> –Њ–±–ї—Л—Б—Л` pre-v4.11.7). Plus `Tool::dispatch(SearchGraph)` lowercases the requested subject before equality comparison, so a title-cased proper-noun form like `“∞–ї—Л—В–∞—Г` (returned by `normalize_proper_noun` when FST has no lemma) matches lowercase world_core subjects. Closes the live-REPL gap on `–Ц–µ—В—Ц—Б—Г / “∞–ї—Л—В–∞—Г —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л` queries that pre-v4.11.7 fell to `unknown.tentative` ("–С”Щ–ї–Ї—Ц–Љ, ... –∞–є—В–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞"). +1 compound `—В–∞—А–Є—Е–Є ”©“£—Ц—А` to MULTIWORD_ENTITIES.

4. **Birthdate detector verb-form variants** вАФ `“Ы–∞–љ—И–∞/–љ–µ—И–µ + –ґ–∞—Б–∞–є—Б—Л“£/–ґ–∞—Б–∞–є—Б—Л–Ј` added alongside the existing adessive `–ґ–∞—Б—В–∞—Б—Л“£/–ґ–∞—Б—В–∞—Б—Л–Ј` patterns. `–ґ–∞—Б–∞–є—Б—Л“£/–ґ–∞—Б–∞–є—Б—Л–Ј` (= 2nd-person of `–ґ–∞—Б–∞—Г` "to live") is colloquial Kazakh for "how old are you?". Real-REPL: ¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–љ—И–∞ –ґ–∞—Б–∞–є—Б—Л–Ј?¬ї pre-v4.11.7 returned "–Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ" because the existing `–ґ–∞—Б—В–∞—Б—Л“£/–ґ–∞—Б—В–∞—Б—Л–Ј` patterns required the adessive form.

5. **Language-capability detector** вАФ closed list of {language adverb} + {2nd-person knowledge verb} pairs added to `capabilities_marker`. Catches ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј –±–µ?¬ї, ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј –∞“У—Л–ї—И—Л–љ—И–∞ —Б”©–є–ї–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?¬ї, ¬Ђ–°–µ–љ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞ —В“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?¬ї (and same for –Њ—А—Л—Б—И–∞/—В“ѓ—А—Ц–Ї—И–µ). Routes to `SystemAspect::Capabilities` so adam surfaces its `capabilities_summary` (¬Ђ“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ —Б”©–є–ї–µ—Б–µ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ; вА¶¬ї). Pre-v4.11.7 these queries returned "–Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ" because no detector matched the `{lang}-—И–∞ + –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£/–±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј/—Б”©–є–ї–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л“£/—Б”©–є–ї–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј/—В“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–µ—Б—Ц“£/—В“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–µ—Б—Ц–Ј` pattern.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 622 вЖТ **1 625 entries** (+3 bare-name geo); 1 785 вЖТ **1 791 facts** (+6 вАФ geo entries carry IsA + PartOf each).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **1 compound** (`—В–∞—А–Є—Е–Є ”©“£—Ц—А`).
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: rerun-stable.

### Live REPL regression вАФ 20 subject queries

All 20 queries from the empirical 2026-04-30 battery now answer correctly. Sample changes pre-v4.11.7 вЖТ post-v4.11.7:

```
Q: –Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–є—В
Pre:  –Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ вАФ —В—Ц—А—Ц –Љ–∞—В–µ—А–Є—П.
Post: –Ц–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ вАФ —В—Ц—А—Ц –∞“У–Ј–∞–љ—Л“£ “Ы“±—А—Л–ї—Л—Б –ґ”Щ–љ–µ —Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є–Њ–љ–∞–ї–і—Л –±—Ц—А–ї—Ц–≥—Ц.

Q: –Ц–µ—В—Ц—Б—Г —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј?
Pre:  –С”Щ–ї–Ї—Ц–Љ, –Ц–µ—В—Ц—Б—Г —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–є—В–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞.
Post: –Ц–µ—В—Ц—Б—Г вАФ “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ –Њ“£—В“ѓ—Б—В—Ц–Ї-—И—Л“У—Л—Б—Л–љ–і–∞“У—Л —В–∞—А–Є—Е–Є ”©“£—Ц—А;
      –Њ—А—В–∞–ї—Л“У—Л –Ґ–∞–ї–і—Л“Ы–Њ—А“У–∞–љ “Ы–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л, ”Щ–Ї—Ц–Љ—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –∞—В–∞—Г—Л вАФ –Ц–µ—В—Ц—Б—Г –Њ–±–ї—Л—Б—Л.

Q: “Ъ–∞–љ—И–∞ –ґ–∞—Б–∞–є—Б—Л–Ј?
Pre:  –Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ.
Post: –Ь–µ–љ—Ц“£ –ґ–∞—Б—Л–Љ –∞–і–∞–Љ–Ј–∞—В –ґ–∞—Б—Л–љ–і–∞–є.

Q: –°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј –±–µ?
Pre:  –Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ.
Post: “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ —Б”©–є–ї–µ—Б–µ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ; –µ—Б—Ц–Љ—Ц“£—Ц–Ј–і—Ц, –ґ–∞—Б—Л“£—Л–Ј–і—Л,
      “Ы–∞–ї–∞“£—Л–Ј–і—Л –ґ”Щ–љ–µ –Љ–∞–Љ–∞–љ–і—Л“У—Л“£—Л–Ј–і—Л –µ—Б—В–µ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–њ, вА¶

Q: –°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—И–∞ —Б”©–є–ї–µ–є –∞–ї–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞?
Pre:  –С–∞ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –Љ—Л–љ–∞–љ—Л –∞–є—В–∞ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ: ¬Ђ”®–Ј ”©–љ–µ—А—Ц —В“±—А —В–∞—П—Г, вА¶¬ї
Post: “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ —Б”©–є–ї–µ—Б–µ –∞–ї–∞–Љ—Л–љ; вА¶
```

### Known remaining limitations (deferred to v4.12+)

- **Causal reasoning**: ¬Ђ–Р–і–∞–Љ, –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ ”©–ї–µ–і—Ц?¬ї returns a generic IsA fact instead of a causal chain. Requires the question-form classifier (v4.12.0).
- **Implementation aspect**: ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ –ґ–∞–Ј—Л–ї“У–∞–љ—Б—Л–Ј?¬ї grounds on the generic `–±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц IsA —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞–ї–і—Л —В—Ц–ї` instead of `adam writtenIn Rust`. Requires implementation-aspect detection (v4.12.0).

### Tests + counters

- Workspace tests: **749 passing** (unchanged).
- `validate_world_core`: 1 625 / 1 625 approved / 1 791 facts.
- Live REPL battery: **20/20 answer correctly** (3 catastrophic + 2 partial gaps closed).

### Cadence

Patch (v4.11.7) вАФ 5 innovations bundled per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Third patch in the v4.12+ humanlike-dialog research arc. Stripe (1) вАФ "answer-by-the-point" fixes вАФ closed for the breadth of single-turn subject queries across all 38 world_core domains. Next: **v4.12.0 вАФ question-form classifier + discourse acknowledgement** (first humanness work).

## [4.11.6] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ universal subject coverage: rich-fact priority + scant cleanup + accusative fallback + adam_self subject claims + REPL baseline

User directive (2026-04-30): ¬Ђ–∞–і–µ–Ї–≤–∞—В–љ–Њ –Њ—В–≤–µ—З–∞—В—М –њ–Њ –≤—Б–µ–Љ –њ—А–µ–і–Љ–µ—В–∞–Љ вАФ –±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П, –Є—Б—В–Њ—А–Є—П, –љ–µ —В–Њ–ї—М–Ї–Њ —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –Є Rust¬ї. Empirical test of 13 subject-related queries pre-v4.11.6 surfaced two systemic gaps: (a) scant duplicate definitions (`–•–Є–Љ–Є—П вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ.`) won the length tiebreaker over rich school definitions; (b) `–Р–і–∞–Љ, —Б–µ–љ –±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П–љ—Л –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?` returned bare `–Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ.` because FST has a lexicon gap on accusative-form loanwords.

### Innovations bundled (5 patches)

1. **Length tiebreaker inverted** in `Tool::dispatch(SearchGraph)` вАФ `b.raw_text.chars().count().cmp(&a.raw_text.chars().count())` (longer wins) instead of `a.cmp(b)` (shorter wins). For "what do you know about X?" questions, longer is measurably more informative вАФ the user wants the school-curriculum definition over the one-word `X вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ.` stub. One-line change; immediate effect on all 5 school-subject queries.

2. **Scant duplicate definitions removed** for 5 school subjects: `soc_024 (–Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ)`, `soc_025 (—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ)`, `soc_026 (—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ)`, `soc_027 (—В–∞—А–Є—Е вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ)` from `society.jsonl`; `bio_024 (–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П вАФ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ)` from `biology_basic.jsonl`. New rich `—В–∞—А–Є—Е` entry added to `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` as `hist_kz_125` (—В–∞—А–Є—Е only had the scant version). Net world_core: -5 + 1 = -4 entries.

3. **Accusative-form noun-hint fallback** (`semantics::accusative_form_hint`) вАФ string-level stripper for the six Kazakh Accusative allomorphs (`-–љ—Л / -–љ—Ц / -–і—Л / -–і—Ц / -—В—Л / -—В—Ц`). Closes the FST lexicon gap on inflected loanwords (`–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П–љ—Л = –±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П + Acc`, `—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П–љ—Л`, `—В–∞—А–Є—Е—В—Л`). Mirror design of v4.4.12 `locative_attributive_hint`. Runs LAST in `best_noun_hint` after FST-driven strategies have failed. Conservative: token вЙ• 5 chars, recovered stem вЙ• 3 chars, must not match `NOT_A_TOPIC`.

4. **Adam_self richer subject knowledge claims** вАФ 6 new entries (`adam_self_028..033`) with subjects = —И–Ї–Њ–ї—М–љ—Л–µ –њ—А–µ–і–Љ–µ—В—Л (`—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞`, `—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П`, `–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П`, `—В–∞—А–Є—Е`, `–Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞`, `–Є–љ—Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞`), each combining definition + adam's knowledge claim + topic enumeration. Example: ¬Ђ–§–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ вАФ —В–∞–±–Є“У–∞—В—В–∞“У—Л “Ы“±–±—Л–ї—Л—Б—В–∞—А–і—Л –Ј–µ—А—В—В–µ–є—В—Ц–љ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ; –Љ–µ–љ –Њ–љ—Л –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л –і–µ“£–≥–µ–є—Ц–љ–і–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ–Љ—Ц–љ: –Љ–µ—Е–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞, —В–µ—А–Љ–Њ–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А, –Њ–њ—В–Є–Ї–∞, –∞—В–Њ–Љ —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞—Б—Л.¬ї Wins the length tiebreaker (per #1) over the bare school-domain definition. 5 new compound objects added to MULTIWORD_ENTITIES (`—В–∞–±–Є“У–∞—В “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ—Л`, `–ґ–∞—А–∞—В—Л–ї—Л—Б—В–∞–љ—Г “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ—Л`, `–≥—Г–Љ–∞–љ–Є—В–∞—А–ї—Л“Ы “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ`, `“Ы–Њ–ї–і–∞–љ–±–∞–ї—Л “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ`, `–∞–±—Б—В—А–∞–Ї—В “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ`).

5. **REPL replay regression baseline** вАФ 8 new dialogs in `data/eval/repl_dialogs.json` covering all 6 school subjects + accusative-form routing + Kazakhstan-history multi-turn. Locks the v4.11.6 behavior against future regressions. `repl_replay_baseline` test passes 51/51 canonical (was 43/43 in v4.4.13).

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 620 вЖТ **1 622 entries** (-5 scant + 1 —В–∞—А–Є—Е + 6 adam_self subject claims = +2 net); 1 783 вЖТ **1 785 facts** (+2 net); 38 domains unchanged.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 16 309 вЖТ **16 305** вКХ +13 = **16 305** (rerun-stable).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **5 compounds** (subject-rich science categories).
- `derived_facts.json`: stable in 5 passes.

### Live REPL regression вАФ 13 subject queries

All 13 queries from the empirical test (—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞/—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П/–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П/—В–∞—А–Є—Е/–Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞/–Є–љ—Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ across ¬ЂX —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л¬ї, ¬Ђ—Б–µ–љ X-–љ—Л –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?¬ї, ¬Ђ–Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ—В—Ц“£ X –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л–љ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?¬ї phrasings) now answer with a rich, structured response. Sample:

```
Q: –§–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј?
A: –§–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ вАФ —В–∞–±–Є“У–∞—В—В–∞“У—Л “Ы“±–±—Л–ї—Л—Б—В–∞—А–і—Л –Ј–µ—А—В—В–µ–є—В—Ц–љ “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ; –Љ–µ–љ –Њ–љ—Л –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л
   –і–µ“£–≥–µ–є—Ц–љ–і–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ–Љ—Ц–љ: –Љ–µ—Е–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞, —В–µ—А–Љ–Њ–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А, –Њ–њ—В–Є–Ї–∞, –∞—В–Њ–Љ —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞—Б—Л.

Q: –Р–і–∞–Љ, —Б–µ–љ –±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П–љ—Л –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?
Pre-v4.11.6: –Ґ“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ.
Post-v4.11.6: –С–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П вАФ —В—Ц—А—Ц –∞“У–Ј–∞–ї–∞—А –Љ–µ–љ –Њ–ї–∞—А–і—Л“£ —В—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –њ—А–Њ—Ж–µ—Б—В–µ—А—Ц–љ –Ј–µ—А—В—В–µ–є—В—Ц–љ
              “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ; –Љ–µ–љ –Њ–љ—Л –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л –і–µ“£–≥–µ–є—Ц–љ–і–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ–Љ—Ц–љ: –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞,
              ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А, –ґ–∞–љ—Г–∞—А–ї–∞—А, –∞–і–∞–Љ –∞“У–Ј–∞—Б—Л, –≥–µ–љ–µ—В–Є–Ї–∞, —Н–Ї–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П.
```

### Tests + counters

- New REPL replay dialogs: 8 (—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞/—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П/–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П/—В–∞—А–Є—Е/–Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞/–Є–љ—Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ queries + accusative-form + Kazakhstan-history).
- Workspace tests: **749 passing** (unchanged вАФ repl_replay_baseline stays 1 test).
- `validate_world_core`: 1 622 / 1 622 approved / 1 785 facts.
- `cognitive_eval`: 25/25 canonical.

### Cadence

Patch (v4.11.6) вАФ 5 innovations bundled per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`. Continuation of the v4.12+ humanlike-dialog research arc. Stripe (1) вАФ "answer-by-the-point" fixes вАФ extended from the v4.11.5 5-question scope to all 38 world_core domains. Next: v4.12.0 question-form classifier.

## [4.11.5] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ humanlike-dialog patch bundle: vocative guard + retrieval priority + compounds + adam_self pack + Latin passthrough

Patch-bundled 5 innovations from the live REPL transcript review (2026-04-30). All five were catastrophic correctness failures pre-v4.11.5: ¬Ђ–Р–і–∞–Љ, —Б–µ–љ –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ—В—Ц“£ —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л–љ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?¬ї returned `–∞–і–∞–Љ IsA —Б“ѓ—В“Ы–Њ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц`; ¬ЂRust —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј?¬ї returned a poetry quote about the body part `—В—Ц–ї`. Post-v4.11.5 every transcript question lands on the correct world_core fact.

### Innovations

1. **Vocative-addressee guard** (`discourse::strip_addressee`) вАФ strips leading `–Р–і–∞–Љ, вА¶` / `–Р–і–∞–Љ! вА¶` / `–Р–і–∞–Љ —Б–µ–љ вА¶` / `–Р–і–∞–Љ—Л–Љ, вА¶` / `–Р–і–∞–Љ-–∞—Г, вА¶` BEFORE FST parsing. Disambiguated from definitional `–Р–і–∞–Љ вАФ —Б“ѓ—В“Ы–Њ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц.` by requiring an addressee signal in the input (2nd-person pronoun or `?`/`!` punctuation). Wired in `Conversation::turn_with_trace` after `strip_preamble` so preamble + vocative combinations collapse cleanly.

2. **World_core source-priority in topic extraction** вАФ `multiword_entity_hint` promoted to run BEFORE `topic_marker_hint` in `best_noun_hint`. When MULTIWORD_ENTITIES contains a compound that appears in the input, that compound is almost always the user's intended topic. Plus an **inflected-form lemma fallback** in `topic_marker_hint`: when the word right before `—В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л` is inflected (e.g. `—В—Ц–ї—Ц = —В—Ц–ї + Px3sg`), walk parses and pick the longest noun-root that's a prefix of the surface form. Closes the v4.11.0 transcript bug where `–Ґ—Ц–ї—Ц` was extracted as a fake proper noun.

3. **Curriculum compounds in MULTIWORD_ENTITIES** вАФ added 9 query-time compounds: `—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `–Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `–Є–љ—Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `—В–∞—А–Є—Е –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `–Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л`, `–Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ–і–µ—А—Ц`, `–Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ—Ц`. NOT world_core subjects/objects (the contract test does not require them) вАФ purely query-time canonical phrasings.

4. **Self-knowledge pack `data/world_core/adam_self.jsonl`** (27 entries / 27 facts, new domain) вАФ identity (`adam IsA —В—Ц–ї–і—Ц–Ї –Љ–Њ–і–µ–ї—М / –і–Є–∞–ї–Њ–≥ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц / –Ї–Њ–≥–љ–Є—В–Є–≤—В—Ц–Ї —П–і—А–Њ / –ґ–∞—Б–∞–љ–і—Л –Є–љ—В–µ–ї–ї–µ–Ї—В`), implementation (`adam related_to rust`, `adam has rust –±–∞—Б—В–∞–њ“Ы—Л –Ї–Њ–і—Л`, `adam IsA —А–µ—В—А–Є–≤–∞–ї –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц`), per-domain knowledge claims (`adam has —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / –±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / —В–∞—А–Є—Е –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / rust –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / –Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞ –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / ”Щ–і–µ–±–Є–µ—В –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / –≥–µ–Њ–≥—А–∞—Д–Є—П –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц / –ґ–∞–ї–њ—Л –±—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ`), curriculum-program facts (`—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л IsA –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ—Ц`, `—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л IsA –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ—Ц`, вА¶), and limitations (`adam IsA –ґ–µ—А–≥—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї—В—Ц –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞ / “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Ц–ї–і—Ц –ґ“ѓ–є–µ`). All 21 new compound subjects/objects added to MULTIWORD_ENTITIES under a v4.11.5 bucket. `SystemIdentity::knowledge_summary` refreshed to enumerate all 38 domains structurally.

5. **Latin-name passthrough** (`semantics::latin_subject_hint`) вАФ closes the v4.7.0 known limitation. Closed list of 47 Latin-named technical subjects (mirroring `programming_rust.jsonl`): `rust`, `cargo`, `rustc`, `string`, `vec`, `option`, `result`, `hashmap`, `arc`, `mutex`, `trait`-related types, etc. When the user types one of these as a Latin word (case-insensitive, word-boundary-matched), it becomes the topic. Runs FIRST in `best_noun_hint` so an explicit Latin proper noun beats any contained Cyrillic compound. Now ¬ЂRust —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј?¬ї вЖТ `\`Rust\` вАФ –ґ–∞–і—Л–љ—Л “Ы–∞—Г—Ц–њ—Б—Ц–Ј –±–∞—Б“Ы–∞—А–∞—В—Л–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц`.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 593 вЖТ **1 620 entries** (+27); 1 756 вЖТ **1 783 facts** (+27); 37 вЖТ **38 domains**.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 16 282 вЖТ **16 309** (+27).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **30 compounds** (9 curriculum query-time + 21 adam_self world_core).
- `derived_facts.json`: 29 991 derived facts in 5 passes.

### Tests + counters

- New unit tests: `strip_addressee` (4 tests covering punctuation-separated, longest-first variant, bare-pronoun, definitional-passthrough); `multiword_entity_hint_matches_curriculum_compounds` (4 transcript-style queries).
- Workspace tests: **745 вЖТ 749 passing**.
- `validate_world_core`: 1 620 / 1 620 approved / 1 783 facts.
- `cognitive_eval`: 25/25 canonical (was 24/25 after first knowledge_summary update; fixed by removing backticked-Rust mention to satisfy the LatinCharactersForbidden audit, replaced with `–С–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ –±—Ц–ї–µ–Љ—Ц–љ`).

### Live REPL regression

All 5 problem questions from the 2026-04-30 transcript now answer correctly:

- ¬ЂRust –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц–Ј?¬ї вЖТ grounds on `rust IsA –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц` (was: poetry about `—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ`).
- ¬Ђ–Р–і–∞–Љ, —Б–µ–љ –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ–і–µ—А—Ц —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –љ–µ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£?¬ї вЖТ grounds on `–Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ–і–µ—А—Ц IsA “У—Л–ї—Л–Љ —Б–∞–ї–∞–ї–∞—А—Л` (was: gnomic mudrost' about `—Б”©–Ј / —Ц—Б`).
- ¬Ђ–Р–і–∞–Љ, —Б–µ–љ –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ—В—Ц“£ —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л–љ –±—Ц–ї–µ—Б—Ц“£ –±–µ?¬ї вЖТ grounds on `—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л IsA –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ –њ”Щ–љ—Ц` (was: catastrophic `–∞–і–∞–Љ IsA —Б“ѓ—В“Ы–Њ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц`).
- ¬Ђ–Р–і–∞–Љ, ”©–Ј—Ц“£—Ц–Ј —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –∞–Ј–і–∞–њ –∞–є—В—Л–њ –±–µ—А—Ц“£—Ц–Ј—И—Ц¬ї вЖТ SystemAspect::General self-introduction (preserved).
- ¬Ђ–°—Ц–Ј “Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц–љ–і–µ –ґ–∞–Ј—Л–ї“У–∞–љ—Б—Л–Ј?¬ї вЖТ grounds on `–±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г —В—Ц–ї—Ц IsA —Д–Њ—А–Љ–∞–ї–і—Л —В—Ц–ї` (was: same; semantically partial вАФ implementation-aspect detector is v4.12.0 work).

### Cadence

Patch (v4.11.5) вАФ 5 innovations bundled per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` (post-1.0 patch-bundling rule). First release in the v4.12+ "human-like dialog" research arc per `project_humanlike_dialog_directive`. Stripe (1) вАФ "answer-by-the-point" fixes вАФ closed; stripe (2) вАФ humanness вАФ opens with v4.12.0 (question-form classifier).

## [4.11.0] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` world_core domain (history of Kazakhstan, Kazakh)

Ninth v4.x minor. **Final** in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track. `history_kazakhstan.jsonl` is a curated 124-entry Kazakh glossary covering the major periods, polities, events, and symbols of Kazakhstan's history вАФ from Bronze Age archaeology through the present day.

### Sections covered

- **Archaeological / pre-T√Љrkic (~7 entries)** вАФ “Ы–Њ–ї–∞ –і”Щ—Г—Ц—А—Ц, —В–µ–Љ—Ц—А –і”Щ—Г—Ц—А—Ц, –∞–љ–і—А–Њ–љ –Љ”Щ–і–µ–љ–Є–µ—В—Ц, –±–µ“У–∞–Ј—Л-–і”Щ–љ–і—Ц–±–∞–є –Љ”Щ–і–µ–љ–Є–µ—В—Ц, —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞—А, “У“±–љ–і–∞—А, “ѓ–є—Б—Ц–љ–і–µ—А.
- **T√Љrkic period (~10 entries)** вАФ —В“ѓ—А—Ц–Ї “Ы–∞“У–∞–љ–∞—В—Л, –±–∞—В—Л—Б —В“ѓ—А—Ц–Ї “Ы–∞“У–∞–љ–∞—В—Л, —В“ѓ—А–≥–µ—И “Ы–∞“У–∞–љ–∞—В—Л, “Ы–∞—А–ї“±“Ы, –Њ“У—Л–Ј, –Ї–Є–Љ–µ–Ї, “Ы–∞—А–∞—Е–∞–љ, “Ы—Л–њ—И–∞“Ы; –Ї“ѓ–ї—В–µ–≥—Ц–љ –µ—Б–Ї–µ—А—В–Ї—Ц—И—Ц, –Њ—А—Е–Њ–љ –ґ–∞–Ј—Г–ї–∞—А—Л.
- **Mongol / Golden Horde (~6 entries)** вАФ –Љ–Њ“£“У–Њ–ї —И–∞–њ“Ы—Л–љ—И—Л–ї—Л“У—Л, —И—Л“£“У—Л—Б —Е–∞–љ, –∞–ї—В—Л–љ –Њ—А–і–∞, –∞“Ы –Њ—А–і–∞, –Ї”©–Ї –Њ—А–і–∞, ”Щ–Љ—Ц—А —В–µ–Љ—Ц—А.
- **Kazakh Khanate (~16 entries)** вАФ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —Е–∞–љ–і—Л“У—Л (1465), –Ї–µ—А–µ–є —Е–∞–љ, –ґ”Щ–љ—Ц–±–µ–Ї —Е–∞–љ, –±“±—А—Л–љ–і—Л“Ы —Е–∞–љ, “Ы–∞—Б—Л–Љ —Е–∞–љ, —Е–∞“Ы–љ–∞–Ј–∞—А —Е–∞–љ, —В”Щ—Г–µ–Ї–µ–ї —Е–∞–љ, –µ—Б—Ц–Љ —Е–∞–љ, –ґ”Щ“£–≥—Ц—А —Е–∞–љ, —В”Щ—Г–Ї–µ —Е–∞–љ, –ґ–µ—В—Ц –ґ–∞—А“У—Л, –∞–±—Л–ї–∞–є —Е–∞–љ, –Ї–µ–љ–µ—Б–∞—А—Л —Е–∞–љ; “ѓ—И –ґ“ѓ–Ј вАФ “±–ї—Л / –Њ—А—В–∞ / –Ї—Ц—И—Ц –ґ“ѓ–Ј.
- **Jungar wars (~5 entries)** вАФ –ґ–Њ“£“У–∞—А —Е–∞–љ–і—Л“У—Л, –∞“Ы—В–∞–±–∞–љ —И“±–±—Л—А—Л–љ–і—Л (1723), –±“±–ї–∞–љ—В—Л —И–∞–є“Ы–∞—Б—Л (1726), –∞“£—Л—А–∞“Ы–∞–є —И–∞–є“Ы–∞—Б—Л (1729), —В”©–ї–µ / “Ы–∞–Ј—Л–±–µ–Ї / ”Щ–є—В–µ–Ї–µ –±–Є–ї–µ—А.
- **Russian colonization (~10 entries)** вАФ ”Щ–±—Ц–ї“Ы–∞–є—Л—А —Е–∞–љ 1731, –Ї–µ–љ–µ—Б–∞—А—Л –Ї”©—В–µ—А—Ц–ї—Ц—Б—Ц 1837вАУ1847, —Б—Л—А—Л–Љ –і–∞—В“±–ї—Л, –Є—Б–∞—В–∞–є / –Љ–∞—Е–∞–Љ–±–µ—В, –∞–±–∞–є “Ы“±–љ–∞–љ–±–∞–є“±–ї—Л, —И–Њ“Ы–∞–љ —Г”Щ–ї–Є—Е–∞–љ–Њ–≤, —Л–±—Л—А–∞–є –∞–ї—В—Л–љ—Б–∞—А–Є–љ, 1916 –ґ—Л–ї“У—Л –Ї”©—В–µ—А—Ц–ї—Ц—Б, –∞–Љ–∞–љ–≥–µ–ї–і—Ц –Є–Љ–∞–љ–Њ–≤.
- **Soviet period (~22 entries)** вАФ –∞–ї–∞—И –Њ—А–і–∞, ”Щ–ї–Є—Е–∞–љ –±”©–Ї–µ–є—Е–∞–љ, –∞—Е–Љ–µ—В –±–∞–є—В“±—А—Б—Л–љ“±–ї—Л, –Љ–∞“У–ґ–∞–љ –ґ“±–Љ–∞–±–∞–µ–≤, –Љ“±—Б—В–∞—Д–∞ —И–Њ“Ы–∞–є; “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы –∞—Б—Б—А вЖТ “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —Б—Б—А; –Ї–Њ–ї–ї–µ–Ї—В–Є–≤—В–µ–љ–і—Ц—А—Г, 1930 –ґ—Л–ї–і–∞—А–і–∞“У—Л –∞—И–∞—А—И—Л–ї—Л“Ы, –≥–Њ–ї–Њ—Й—С–Ї–Є–љ, —Б—В–∞–ї–Є–љ–і—Ц–Ї —А–µ–њ—А–µ—Б—Б–Є—П–ї–∞—А, –Ї–∞—А–ї–∞–≥ / –∞–ї–ґ–Є—А, –і–µ–њ–Њ—А—В–∞—Ж–Є—П, “±–ї—Л –Њ—В–∞–љ —Б–Њ“У—Л—Б—Л, –±–∞—Г—Л—А–ґ–∞–љ –Љ–Њ–Љ—Л—И“±–ї—Л, —В—Л“£ –Є–≥–µ—А—Г (1954), —Б–µ–Љ–µ–є –њ–Њ–ї–Є–≥–Њ–љ—Л, –±–∞–є“Ы–Њ“£—Л—А “У–∞—А—Л—И –∞–є–ї–∞“У—Л, –ґ–µ–ї—В–Њ“Ы—Б–∞–љ –Ї”©—В–µ—А—Ц–ї—Ц—Б—Ц (1986), “Ы–Њ–љ–∞–µ–≤.
- **Independence era (~13 entries)** вАФ –љ–∞–Ј–∞—А–±–∞–µ–≤, —В”Щ—Г–µ–ї—Б—Ц–Ј–і—Ц–Ї 1991, 1995 –Ї–Њ–љ—Б—В–Є—В—Г—Ж–Є—П, —В–µ“£–≥–µ (1993), –∞—Б—В–∞–љ–∞ (1997), –љ—Г—А-—Б“±–ї—В–∞–љ, —В–Њ“Ы–∞–µ–≤, “Ы–∞“£—В–∞—А –Њ“Ы–Є“У–∞–ї–∞—А—Л (2022).
- **Symbols & holidays (~10 entries)** вАФ —А”Щ–Љ—Ц–Ј–і–µ—А: —В—Г, –µ–ї—В–∞“£–±–∞, ”Щ–љ“±—А–∞–љ; —И–∞“£—Л—А–∞“Ы, –Ї”©–Ї –±”©—А—Ц, “Ы—Л—А–∞–љ; —В”Щ—Г–µ–ї—Б—Ц–Ј–і—Ц–Ї –Ї“ѓ–љ—Ц, –љ–∞—Г—А—Л–Ј –Љ–µ–є—А–∞–Љ—Л, “±–ї—В—В—Л“Ы –Ї–Є—Ц–Љ–і–µ—А.
- **Silk Road & monuments (~8 entries)** вАФ –Њ—В—Л—А–∞—А, —В–∞—А–∞–Ј, —Б–∞—Г—А–∞–љ, —Б—Л“У–∞–љ–∞“Ы; —Е–Њ–і–ґ–∞ –∞—Е–Љ–µ—В —П—Б–∞—Г–Є –Ї–µ—Б–µ–љ–µ—Б—Ц, –∞–є—И–∞ –±–Є–±—Ц –Ї–µ—Б–µ–љ–µ—Б—Ц, –±–µ—Б—И–∞—В—Л—А, —В–∞“£–±–∞–ї—Л, —В“ѓ—А–Ї—Ц—Б—В–∞–љ.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 469 вЖТ **1 593 entries** (+124); 1 632 вЖТ **1 756 facts** (+124); 36 вЖТ **37 domains**.

### Cadence

Minor (v4.11.0) вАФ new world_core domain. **Concludes the non-Rust expansion track** (v4.8.0 physics вЖТ v4.9.0 chemistry вЖТ v4.10.0 biology вЖТ v4.11.0 history). Roman numerals (`IX`, `XV`, `XIII`, вА¶) wrap in backticks per the v4.7.0 carve-out, so century notation in Kazakh prose round-trips cleanly through `validate_world_core`.

## [4.10.0] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ `biology_school.jsonl` world_core domain (school-curriculum biology, Kazakh)

Eighth v4.x minor. Third in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track. `biology_school.jsonl` is a curated 120-entry Kazakh glossary covering school-curriculum biology across eight sections.

### Sections covered

- **Foundation (~9 entries)** вАФ –±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, —Ж–Є—В–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, –±–Њ—В–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞, –Ј–Њ–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, –∞–љ–∞—В–Њ–Љ–Є—П, —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, –≥–µ–љ–µ—В–Є–Ї–∞, —Н–Ї–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, –Љ–Є–Ї—А–Њ–±–Є–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П.
- **Cell biology (~17 entries)** вАФ –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞, –Љ–µ–Љ–±—А–∞–љ–∞, —Ж–Є—В–Њ–њ–ї–∞–Ј–Љ–∞, —П–і—А–Њ, –Љ–Є—В–Њ—Е–Њ–љ–і—А–Є—П, —А–Є–±–Њ—Б–Њ–Љ–∞, —Е–ї–Њ—А–Њ–њ–ї–∞—Б—В, —Н–љ–і–Њ–њ–ї–∞–Ј–Љ–∞–ї—Л“Ы —В–Њ—А, –≥–Њ–ї—М–і–ґ–Є –∞–њ–њ–∞—А–∞—В—Л, –ї–Є–Ј–Њ—Б–Њ–Љ–∞, –≤–∞–Ї—Г–Њ–ї—М, –ґ–∞—Б—Г—И–∞ “Ы–∞–±—Л—А“У–∞—Б—Л, –њ—А–Њ–Ї–∞—А–Є–Њ—В, —Н—Г–Ї–∞—А–Є–Њ—В, –±–∞–Ї—В–µ—А–Є—П, –≤–Є—А—Г—Б, –Љ–Є—В–Њ–Ј, –Љ–µ–є–Њ–Ј.
- **Botany / plants (~13 entries)** вАФ ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї, –≥“ѓ–ї–і—Ц ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї, “Ы—Л–ї“Ы–∞–љ –ґ–∞–њ—Л—А–∞“Ы—В—Л ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї, –њ–∞–њ–Њ—А–Њ—В–љ–Є–Ї, –Љ“ѓ–Ї, –±–∞–ї–і—Л—А, —В–∞–Љ—Л—А, —Б–∞–±–∞“Ы, –ґ–∞–њ—Л—А–∞“Ы, –≥“ѓ–ї, –ґ–µ–Љ—Ц—Б, —В“±“Ы—Л–Љ, —Д–Њ—В–Њ—Б–Є–љ—В–µ–Ј, —Е–ї–Њ—А–Њ—Д–Є–ї–ї, —В—Л–љ—Л—Б –∞–ї—Г.
- **Zoology / animals (~13 entries)** вАФ –ґ–∞–љ—Г–∞—А, –Њ–Љ—Л—А—В“Ы–∞–ї—Л/–Њ–Љ—Л—А—В“Ы–∞—Б—Л–Ј –ґ–∞–љ—Г–∞—А–ї–∞—А, –±–∞–ї—Л“Ы, “Ы–Њ—Б–Љ–µ–Ї–µ–љ–і—Ц, –±–∞—Г—Л—А–Љ–µ–љ –ґ–Њ—А“У–∞–ї–∞—Г—И—Л, “Ы“±—Б, —Б“ѓ—В“Ы–Њ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц, –±—Г–љ–∞“Ы–і–µ–љ–µ–ї—Ц, ”©—А–Љ–µ–Ї—И—Ц —В”Щ—А—Ц–Ј–і—Ц–ї–µ—А, —И–∞—П–љ—В”Щ—А—Ц–Ј–і—Ц–ї–µ—А, –Љ–Њ–ї–ї—О—Б–Ї—Ц–ї–µ—А, “Ы“±—А—В—В–∞—А.
- **Human anatomy (~23 entries)** вАФ –∞–і–∞–Љ –∞“У–Ј–∞—Б—Л, “Ы–∞“£“Ы–∞, —Б“ѓ–є–µ–Ї, –±“±–ї—И—Л“Ы–µ—В, –ґ“ѓ—А–µ–Ї, “Ы–∞–љ, “Ы–∞–љ —В–∞–Љ—Л—А—Л (–∞—А—В–µ—А–Є—П, –Ї”©–Ї—В–∞–Љ—Л—А, –Ї–∞–њ–Є–ї–ї—П—А), ”©–Ї–њ–µ, –Ї–µ“£—Ц—А–і–µ–Ї, –∞—Б “Ы–∞–Ј–∞–љ, —Ц—И–µ–Ї, –±–∞—Г—Л—А, –±“ѓ–є—А–µ–Ї, –Љ–Є, –ґ“±–ї—Л–љ, –љ–µ—А–≤, —В–µ—А—Ц, –Ї”©–Ј, “Ы“±–ї–∞“Ы, —В—Ц–ї.
- **Body systems (~10 entries)** вАФ –ґ“ѓ–є–Ї–µ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, “Ы–∞–љ –∞–є–љ–∞–ї—Л–Љ—Л –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, —В—Л–љ—Л—Б –∞–ї—Г –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, –∞—Б “Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Г –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, —В—Ц—А–µ–Ї-“Ы–Є–Љ—Л–ї –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, –±”©–ї—Ц–њ —И—Л“У–∞—А—Г –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, —Н–љ–і–Њ–Ї—А–Є–љ–і—Ц–Ї –ґ“ѓ–є–µ, –Є–Љ–Љ—Г–љ–і—Л“Ы –ґ“ѓ–є–µ, –Ї”©–±–µ—О –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц, —Б–µ–Ј—Ц–Љ –Љ“ѓ—И–µ–ї–µ—А—Ц.
- **Genetics (~8 entries)** вАФ —В“±“Ы—Л–Љ “Ы—Г–∞–ї–∞—Г—И—Л–ї—Л“Ы, –≥–µ–љ (–і–Њ–Љ–Є–љ–∞–љ—В—В—Л/—А–µ—Ж–µ—Б—Б–Є–≤—В—Ц), —Е—А–Њ–Љ–Њ—Б–Њ–Љ–∞, –Љ—Г—В–∞—Ж–Є—П, –≥–µ–љ–Њ—В–Є–њ, —Д–µ–љ–Њ—В–Є–њ.
- **Evolution & ecology (~14 entries)** вАФ —Н–≤–Њ–ї—О—Ж–Є—П, —В–∞–±–Є“У–Є —Б“±—А—Л–њ—В–∞–ї—Г, —В“ѓ—А, –њ–Њ–њ—Г–ї—П—Ж–Є—П, “Ы–Њ“У–∞–Љ–і–∞—Б—В—Л“Ы, —Н–Ї–Њ–ґ“ѓ–є–µ, –±–Є–Њ—Б—Д–µ—А–∞, —В—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Њ—А—В–∞—Б—Л, —В–∞“У–∞–Љ–і—Л“Ы —В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї (–њ—А–Њ–і—Г—Ж–µ–љ—В, –Ї–Њ–љ—Б—Г–Љ–µ–љ—В, —А–µ–і—Г—Ж–µ–љ—В), —Б–Є–Љ–±–Є–Њ–Ј, –±–Є–Њ–∞–ї—Г–∞–љ—В“ѓ—А–ї—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї.
- **Classification (~7 entries)** вАФ —В–∞–Ї—Б–Њ–љ–Њ–Љ–Є—П, –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ, —В–Є–њ, –Ї–ї–∞—Б—Б, –Њ—В—А—П–і, —В“±“Ы—Л–Љ–і–∞—Б, —В–µ–Ї.
- **Other biomolecules (~3 entries)** вАФ –≥–Њ—А–Љ–Њ–љ, –≤–Є—В–∞–Љ–Є–љ, –∞–љ—В–Є—В–µ–љ–µ.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 349 вЖТ **1 469 entries** (+120); 1 512 вЖТ **1 632 facts** (+120); 35 вЖТ **36 domains**.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 16 038 вЖТ **16 158** (+120).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **50 biology compounds**.
- Lexicon: **+51 noun roots**.

- `world_core_multiword_coverage` contract test passes.

## [4.9.0] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ `chemistry_school.jsonl` world_core domain (school-curriculum chemistry, Kazakh)

Seventh v4.x minor. Second in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track. `chemistry_school.jsonl` is a curated 105-entry Kazakh glossary covering school-curriculum chemistry across seven sections.

### Sections covered

- **Foundation (~9 entries)** вАФ —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П, –±–µ–є–Њ—А–≥–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы/–Њ—А–≥–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П, –±–Є–Њ—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П; –Ј–∞—В, —В–∞–Ј–∞ –Ј–∞—В, “Ы–Њ—Б–њ–∞ (–±—Ц—А—В–µ–Ї—В—Ц/”Щ—А—В–µ–Ї—В—Ц), –ґ–∞–є/–Ї“ѓ—А–і–µ–ї—Ц –Ј–∞—В.
- **Periodic table & elements (~16 entries)** вАФ —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П–ї—Л“Ы —Н–ї–µ–Љ–µ–љ—В, –њ–µ—А–Є–Њ–і—В—Л“Ы –ґ“ѓ–є–µ, –Ь–µ–љ–і–µ–ї–µ–µ–≤ –Ј–∞“£—Л, —В–Њ–њ, –њ–µ—А–Є–Њ–і, —А–µ—В—В—Ц–Ї –љ”©–Љ—Ц—А, –∞—В–Њ–Љ–і—Л“Ы –Љ–∞—Б—Б–∞, –≤–∞–ї–µ–љ—В—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї; –Љ–µ—В–∞–ї–ї/–±–µ–є–Љ–µ—В–∞–ї–ї/–Є–љ–µ—А—В—В—Ц –≥–∞–Ј, —Б—Ц–ї—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ–µ—В–∞–ї–і–∞—А, –≥–∞–ї–Њ–≥–µ–љ–і–µ—А; —Б—Г—В–µ–Ї, –Њ—В—В–µ–Ї, –Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А—В–µ–Ї, –∞–Ј–Њ—В, —В–µ–Љ—Ц—А, –∞–ї—В—Л–љ, –Ї“ѓ–Љ—Ц—Б, –Љ—Л—Б, –∞–ї—О–Љ–Є–љ–Є–є, –љ–∞—В—А–Є–є, –Ї–∞–ї–Є–є, —Е–ї–Њ—А, –≥–µ–ї–Є–є, –љ–µ–Њ–љ.
- **Chemical bonds (~9 entries)** вАФ —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П–ї—Л“Ы –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б (–Є–Њ–љ–і—Л“Ы, –Ї–Њ–≤–∞–ї–µ–љ—В—В—Ц, –Љ–µ—В–∞–ї–і—Л“Ы, —Б—Г—В–µ–Ї—В—Ц–Ї); –Є–Њ–љ, –Ї–∞—В–Є–Њ–љ, –∞–љ–Є–Њ–љ, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А—В–µ—А—Ц—Б—В—Ц–Ї.
- **Reactions (~10 entries)** вАФ —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П–ї—Л“Ы —А–µ–∞–Ї—Ж–Є—П (“Ы–Њ—Б—Л–ї—Г, —Л–і—Л—А–∞—Г, –Њ—А—Л–љ–±–∞—Б—Г, –∞–ї–Љ–∞—Б—Г, —В–Њ—В—Л“У—Г, —В–Њ—В—Л“Ы—Б—Л–Ј–і–∞–љ—Г, –±–µ–є—В–∞—А–∞–њ—В–∞–љ—Г, –ґ–∞–љ—Г), –Ї–∞—В–∞–ї–Є–Ј–∞—В–Њ—А, –Ј–∞—В—В–∞—А–і—Л“£ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–ї—Г –Ј–∞“£—Л.
- **Acids, bases, salts, oxides (~13 entries)** вАФ “Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї (—В“±–Ј/–Ї“ѓ–Ї—Ц—А—В/–∞–Ј–Њ—В), –љ–µ–≥—Ц–Ј, —Б—Ц–ї—В—Ц, –љ–∞—В—А–Є–є –≥–Є–і—А–Њ–Ї—Б–Є–і—Ц, —В“±–Ј, –љ–∞—В—А–Є–є —Е–ї–Њ—А–Є–і—Ц, –Њ–Ї—Б–Є–і, –Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А“Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї –≥–∞–Ј—Л, —Б—Г.
- **Solutions (~7 entries)** вАФ –µ—А—Ц—В—Ц–љ–і—Ц, –µ—А—Ц—В–Ї—Ц—И, –Ї–Њ–љ—Ж–µ–љ—В—А–∞—Ж–Є—П, —Б—Г—В–µ–Ї—В—Ц–Ї –Ї”©—А—Б–µ—В–Ї—Ц—И (`pH`), –Љ–Њ–ї—М, –Љ–Њ–ї—П—А–ї—Л“Ы –Љ–∞—Б—Б–∞, –Р–≤–Њ–≥–∞–і—А–Њ —Б–∞–љ—Л, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–ї–Є–Ј.
- **Organic & biomolecules (~28 entries)** вАФ –Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А—Б—Г—В–µ–Ї (–∞–ї–Ї–∞–љ/–∞–ї–Ї–µ–љ/–∞–ї–Ї–Є–љ), –Љ–µ—В–∞–љ, —Н—В–∞–љ, —Н—В–Є–ї–µ–љ, –±–µ–љ–Ј–Њ–ї; —Б–њ–Є—А—В, —Н—В–∞–љ–Њ–ї, –∞–ї—М–і–µ–≥–Є–і, –Ї–∞—А–±–Њ–љ “Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї—Л, —Б—Ц—А–Ї–µ “Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї—Л, —Н—Д–Є—А; –±–Є–Њ–Љ–Њ–ї–µ–Ї—Г–ї–∞–ї–∞—А вАФ –Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А—Б—Г (–≥–ї—О–Ї–Њ–Ј–∞, —Б–∞—Е–∞—А–Њ–Ј–∞, –Ї—А–∞—Е–Љ–∞–ї), –Љ–∞–є, –∞“Ы—Г—Л–Ј, –∞–Љ–Є–љ “Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї—Л, —Д–µ—А–Љ–µ–љ—В; –њ–Њ–ї–Є–Љ–µ—А, –Љ–Њ–љ–Њ–Љ–µ—А, –њ–ї–∞—Б—В–Љ–∞—Б—Б–∞; –Ф–Э–Ъ, –†–Э–Ъ, –љ—Г–Ї–ї–µ–Є–љ “Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї—Л.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 244 вЖТ **1 349 entries** (+105); 1 407 вЖТ **1 512 facts** (+105); 34 вЖТ **35 domains**.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 15 933 вЖТ **16 038** (+105).
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += **56 chemistry compounds** (sorted longest-first; including `–Љ–∞—В–µ—А–Є—П —В“ѓ—А—Ц` carry-over from physics).
- Lexicon: **+51 noun roots** (—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П, –±–Є–Њ—Е–Є–Љ–Є—П, “Ы–Њ—Б–њ–∞, –≤–∞–ї–µ–љ—В—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї, –≥–∞–ї–Њ–≥–µ–љ–і–µ—А, —Б—Г—В–µ–Ї/–Њ—В—В–µ–Ї/–Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А—В–µ–Ї/–∞–Ј–Њ—В, —В–µ–Љ—Ц—А/–∞–ї—В—Л–љ/–Ї“ѓ–Љ—Ц—Б/–Љ—Л—Б/–∞–ї—О–Љ–Є–љ–Є–є, –љ–∞—В—А–Є–є/–Ї–∞–ї–Є–є, —Е–ї–Њ—А, –≥–µ–ї–Є–є, –љ–µ–Њ–љ, –Ї–∞—В–Є–Њ–љ/–∞–љ–Є–Њ–љ, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А—В–µ—А—Ц—Б—В—Ц–Ї, –Ї–∞—В–∞–ї–Є–Ј–∞—В–Њ—А, —А–µ–∞–Ї—Ж–Є—П, “Ы—Л—И“Ы—Л–ї/—Б—Ц–ї—В—Ц/—В“±–Ј/–Њ–Ї—Б–Є–і, —Б—Г, –µ—А—Ц—В—Ц–љ–і—Ц/–µ—А—Ц—В–Ї—Ц—И, –Ї–Њ–љ—Ж–µ–љ—В—А–∞—Ж–Є—П, –Љ–Њ–ї—М, –∞–≤–Њ–≥–∞–і—А–Њ, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–ї–Є–Ј, –Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А—Б—Г—В–µ–Ї, –∞–ї–Ї–∞–љ/–∞–ї–Ї–µ–љ/–∞–ї–Ї–Є–љ, –Љ–µ—В–∞–љ/—Н—В–∞–љ/—Н—В–Є–ї–µ–љ/–±–µ–љ–Ј–Њ–ї, —Б–њ–Є—А—В/—Н—В–∞–љ–Њ–ї, –∞–ї—М–і–µ–≥–Є–і, —Н—Д–Є—А, –Ї”©–Љ—Ц—А—Б—Г/–≥–ї—О–Ї–Њ–Ј–∞/—Б–∞—Е–∞—А–Њ–Ј–∞/–Ї—А–∞—Е–Љ–∞–ї, –Љ–∞–є, –∞“Ы—Г—Л–Ј, —Д–µ—А–Љ–µ–љ—В, –њ–Њ–ї–Є–Љ–µ—А/–Љ–Њ–љ–Њ–Љ–µ—А, –њ–ї–∞—Б—В–Љ–∞—Б—Б–∞, –Ф–Э–Ъ/–†–Э–Ъ, –Є–Њ–љ, –љ–µ–≥—Ц–Ј, –≥–Є–і—А–Њ–Ї—Б–Є–і, –Љ–µ–љ–і–µ–ї–µ–µ–≤, —В–Њ–њ).

## [4.8.0] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ `physics_school.jsonl` world_core domain (school-curriculum physics, Kazakh)

Sixth v4.x minor. First in the **non-Rust domain expansion** track that follows the v4.7.x Rust Book series. `physics_school.jsonl` is a curated 102-entry Kazakh glossary covering school-curriculum physics across five sections: mechanics, thermodynamics, electricity & magnetism, waves & optics, atomic & modern physics. The domain mirrors the structure of `mathematics_basic` and `informatics_basic` (v4.6.15) and `programming_rust` (v4.7.0): one curated definition per concept, all `confidence: high`, all `review_status: approved`, reviewer `shaman`.

### `data/world_core/physics_school.jsonl` (102 entries / 102 facts)

- **Mechanics & general (~30 entries)** вАФ —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞, –Љ–µ—Е–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞, —В–µ—А–Љ–Њ–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞, –Њ–њ—В–Є–Ї–∞, –∞—В–Њ–Љ–і—Л“Ы —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞; –і–µ–љ–µ, –Љ–∞—В–µ—А–Є—П, –Љ–∞—Б—Б–∞, –Ї”©–ї–µ–Љ, —В—Л“У—Л–Ј–і—Л“Ы; “Ы–Њ–Ј“У–∞–ї—Л—Б, –ґ—Л–ї–і–∞–Љ–і—Л“Ы, “ѓ–і–µ—Г (–±—Ц—А“Ы–∞–ї—Л–њ—В—Л/“ѓ–і–µ–Љ–µ–ї—Ц), –Ї“ѓ—И; –Э—М—О—В–Њ–љ–љ—Л“£ “ѓ—И –Ј–∞“£—Л; –Є–љ–µ—А—Ж–Є—П, –Є–Љ–њ—Г–ї—М—Б; —В–∞—А—В—Л–ї—Л—Б –Ї“ѓ—И—Ц, –∞—Г—Л—А–ї—Л“Ы –Ї“ѓ—И—Ц, “ѓ–є–Ї–µ–ї—Ц—Б –Ї“ѓ—И—Ц, —Б–µ—А–њ—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Ї“ѓ—И—Ц; –µ—А–Ї—Ц–љ —В“ѓ—Б—Г; –ґ“±–Љ—Л—Б, —Н–љ–µ—А–≥–Є—П (–Ї–Є–љ–µ—В–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы/–њ–Њ—В–µ–љ—Ж–Є–∞–ї–і—Л“Ы), —Н–љ–µ—А–≥–Є—П–љ—Л“£ —Б–∞“Ы—В–∞–ї—Г –Ј–∞“£—Л, “Ы—Г–∞—В, “Ы—Л—Б—Л–Љ, –Р—А—Е–Є–Љ–µ–і –Ј–∞“£—Л.
- **States of matter & thermodynamics (~12 entries)** вАФ “Ы–∞—В—В—Л –і–µ–љ–µ, —Б“±–є—Л“Ы, –≥–∞–Ј, –њ–ї–∞–Ј–Љ–∞; —В–µ–Љ–њ–µ—А–∞—В—Г—А–∞, –ґ—Л–ї—Г, —В–µ—А–Љ–Њ–Љ–µ—В—А; –¶–µ–ї—М—Б–Є–є —И–Ї–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л, –Ъ–µ–ї—М–≤–Є–љ —И–Ї–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л; –±–∞–ї“Ы—Г, “Ы–∞—В—Г, “Ы–∞–є–љ–∞—Г, –±—Г–ї–∞–љ—Г, –Ї–Њ–љ–і–µ–љ—Б–∞—Ж–Є—П, —Б—Г–±–ї–Є–Љ–∞—Ж–Є—П; –Љ–µ–љ—И—Ц–Ї—В—Ц –ґ—Л–ї—Г —Б—Л–є—Л–Љ–і—Л–ї—Л“Ы, –ґ—Л–ї—Г ”©—В–Ї—Ц–Ј–≥—Ц—И—В—Ц–Ї.
- **Electricity & magnetism (~16 entries)** вАФ —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А –Ј–∞—А—П–і—Л (–Њ“£/—В–µ—А—Ц—Б), —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А ”©—А—Ц—Б—Ц, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А —В–Њ“У—Л, –Ї–µ—А–љ–µ—Г, –Ї–µ–і–µ—А–≥—Ц, –Ю–Љ –Ј–∞“£—Л, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А —В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–≥—Ц; ”©—В–Ї—Ц–Ј–≥—Ц—И, –і–Є—Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Є–Ї, –ґ–∞—А—В—Л–ї–∞–є ”©—В–Ї—Ц–Ј–≥—Ц—И; –Љ–∞–≥–љ–Є—В, –Љ–∞–≥–љ–Є—В ”©—А—Ц—Б—Ц, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–Љ–∞–≥–љ–Є—В, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–Љ–∞–≥–љ–Є—В—В—Ц–Ї –Є–љ–і—Г–Ї—Ж–Є—П.
- **Waves & optics (~20 entries)** вАФ —В–Њ–ї“Ы—Л–љ, —В–Њ–ї“Ы—Л–љ “±–Ј—Л–љ–і—Л“У—Л, –ґ–Є—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї, –њ–µ—А–Є–Њ–і, –∞–Љ–њ–ї–Є—В—Г–і–∞; –Ї”©–ї–і–µ–љ–µ“£/–±–Њ–є–ї—Л“Ы —В–Њ–ї“Ы—Л–љ; –і—Л–±—Л—Б, –і—Л–±—Л—Б –ґ—Л–ї–і–∞–Љ–і—Л“У—Л; –ґ–∞—А—Л“Ы, –ґ–∞—А—Л“Ы –ґ—Л–ї–і–∞–Љ–і—Л“У—Л, –ґ–∞—А—Л“Ы—В—Л“£ —И–∞“У—Л–ї—Г—Л, –ґ–∞—А—Л“Ы—В—Л“£ —Б—Л–љ—Г—Л; –ї–Є–љ–Ј–∞ (–ґ–Є–љ–∞—Г—И—Л/—И–∞—И—Л—А–∞—В—Г—И—Л), –њ—А–Є–Ј–Љ–∞, —Б–њ–µ–Ї—В—А, —Г–ї—М—В—А–∞–Ї“ѓ–ї–≥—Ц–љ —Б”Щ—Г–ї–µ, –Є–љ—Д—А–∞“Ы—Л–Ј—Л–ї —Б”Щ—Г–ї–µ.
- **Atomic & modern physics (~14 entries)** вАФ –∞—В–Њ–Љ, –∞—В–Њ–Љ —П–і—А–Њ—Б—Л, –њ—А–Њ—В–Њ–љ, –љ–µ–є—В—А–Њ–љ, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–љ, –Є–Ј–Њ—В–Њ–њ, –Љ–Њ–ї–µ–Ї—Г–ї–∞; —А–∞–і–Є–Њ–∞–Ї—В–Є–≤—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї, –∞–ї—М—Д–∞/–±–µ—В–∞/–≥–∞–Љ–Љ–∞ —Б”Щ—Г–ї–µ—Б—Ц; —П–і—А–Њ–ї—Л“Ы —А–µ–∞–Ї—Ж–Є—П, —П–і—А–Њ–ї—Л“Ы —Б–Є–љ—В–µ–Ј, —П–і—А–Њ–ї—Л“Ы —Л–і—Л—А–∞—Г.

### Pipeline impact

- world_core: 1 142 вЖТ **1 244 entries** (+102); 1 305 вЖТ **1 407 facts** (+102); 33 вЖТ **34 domains**.
- `data/retrieval/facts.json`: 15 831 вЖТ **15 933** (+102 from new domain).
- `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json`: regenerated; new IsA hubs (`—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ —Б–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л`, `—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы —И–∞–Љ–∞`, `—Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞ –Ј–∞“£—Л`, `—В–Њ–ї“Ы—Л–љ —В“ѓ—А—Ц`, `“Ы–Њ–Ј“У–∞–ї—Л—Б —В“ѓ—А—Ц`, `—Н–љ–µ—А–≥–Є—П —В“ѓ—А—Ц`, `–Ј–∞—В –Ї“ѓ–є—Ц`, `—А–∞–і–Є–Њ–∞–Ї—В–Є–≤—В—Ц —Б”Щ—Г–ї–µ`) drive new R1/R2/R5/R8 derivations.
- `MULTIWORD_ENTITIES` += 73 physics compounds (sorted longest-first within length buckets so `find_multiword_entity`'s longest-match scan resolves the compound before any contained simpler form).
- Lexicon: +48 noun roots (–Љ–µ—Е–∞–љ–Є–Ї–∞, —В–µ—А–Љ–Њ–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞, –Њ–њ—В–Є–Ї–∞, –Љ–∞—Б—Б–∞, —В—Л“У—Л–Ј–і—Л“Ы, “ѓ–і–µ—Г, –Є–љ–µ—А—Ж–Є—П, –Є–Љ–њ—Г–ї—М—Б, —Н–љ–µ—А–≥–Є—П, —В–µ—А–Љ–Њ–Љ–µ—В—А, —Ж–µ–ї—М—Б–Є–є, –Ї–µ–ї—М–≤–Є–љ, –±–∞–ї“Ы—Г, “Ы–∞—В—Г, “Ы–∞–є–љ–∞—Г, –±—Г–ї–∞–љ—Г, –Ї–Њ–љ–і–µ–љ—Б–∞—Ж–Є—П, –Ј–∞—А—П–і, –њ—А–Њ—В–Њ–љ, –љ–µ–є—В—А–Њ–љ, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–љ, –Є–Ј–Њ—В–Њ–њ, —А–∞–і–Є–Њ–∞–Ї—В–Є–≤—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї, –∞–ї—М—Д–∞, –±–µ—В–∞, –≥–∞–Љ–Љ–∞, –і–Є—Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Є–Ї, –Љ–∞–≥–љ–Є—В, —Н–ї–µ–Ї—В—А–Њ–Љ–∞–≥–љ–Є—В, –∞–Љ–њ–ї–Є—В—Г–і–∞, –њ–µ—А–Є–Њ–і, –ґ–Є—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї, –ї–Є–љ–Ј–∞, –њ—А–Є–Ј–Љ–∞, —Б–њ–µ–Ї—В—А, –∞—А—Е–Є–Љ–µ–і, –љ—М—О—В–Њ–љ, –Њ–Љ, –њ–ї–∞–Ј–Љ–∞, —Б“±–є—Л“Ы, —Л–і—Л—А–∞—Г, —Б–Є–љ—В–µ–Ј, —А–µ–∞–Ї—Ж–Є—П, —Б”Щ—Г–ї–µ, —И–Ї–∞–ї–∞, –Ї–Њ–љ–і–µ–љ—Б–∞—В–Њ—А, –Є–Њ–љ–Є–Ј–∞—Ж–Є—П, –≤–∞–Ї—Г—Г–Љ).

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold for rust_book stays at вЙ•1 500 (v4.7.21 baseline).
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.
- `world_core_multiword_coverage` contract test passes вАФ all 73 new compounds registered.

### Cadence note

Minor (v4.8.0) вАФ new world_core domain, not a patch on v4.7.x. The non-Rust expansion track gets its own minor sequence: v4.8.0 (physics_school) вЖТ v4.9.0 (chemistry_school) вЖТ v4.10.0 (biology_school) вЖТ v4.11.0 (history_kazakhstan).

### Why minor

Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: minor x.y.0 = significant capability. New world_core domain with 102 curated entries + 73 compound entities + 48 lexicon roots is a substantive capability addition.

## [4.7.21] вАФ 2026-04-30 вАФ Per-pack limit override for `rust_book_kk_pack.json`: full chapter 1вАУ20 content now in committed morpheme_index

Architectural follow-up to the v4.7.20 series-completion. Closes the limitation that has carried since v4.7.7: the committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json` capped each pack at `COMMITTED_DEFAULT_LIMIT = 500` samples; the Rust Book pack outgrew that cap at chapter 7 and chapters 8вАУ20 (a further ~835 sentences) were in `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json` (auditable, `--full`-mode-ready) but did not contribute to the committed index.

### Implementation

- New `PER_PACK_LIMIT_OVERRIDES` table in `crates/adam-retrieval/src/bin/build_morpheme_index.rs` mapping pack filename вЖТ optional limit override (`None` removes the cap entirely, `Some(n)` raises/lowers it).
- New helper `effective_limit(pack: &str, default: Option<usize>) -> Option<usize>` consulted in the per-pack indexing loop.
- Override registered: `("rust_book_kk_pack.json", None)` вАФ no per-pack cap. Other packs still use the global `COMMITTED_DEFAULT_LIMIT = 500`.

This keeps the committed-index size budget tight for unchanged packs (Wikipedia, CC-100, Tatoeba etc. were always well under 500 anyway, so no impact) while letting curated packs whose ceiling is *the entire pack content* by design get fully indexed.

### Pipeline impact

- Committed `data/retrieval/morpheme_index.json`: 3 691 вЖТ **4 734 indexed samples** (+1 043 вАФ exactly the 1 543 вИТ 500 previously clipped); distinct morphemes 3 362 вЖТ **3 502** (+140); total postings 22 145 вЖТ **30 919** (+8 774).
- File size: ~3.9 MB (well under the 50 MB gitignore policy threshold from `feedback_git_ignore_policy`).

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold raised from вЙ•490 to **вЙ•1 500** rust_book sentences in the morpheme index. Test passes with 1 543 found.
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Cadence note

Patch вАФ a single per-pack config-table change, surgical scope. Per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0` cadence rules: small architectural carve-out fits the patch level (not a minor).

## [4.7.20] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 20 (–°–Њ“£“У—Л –ґ–Њ–±–∞: –Ї”©–њ –∞“У—Л–љ–і—Л –≤–µ–±-—Б–µ—А–≤–µ—А) translated, in pack вАФ **TRANSLATION SERIES COMPLETE**

Twentieth and **final** chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 20 вАФ Final Project: Building a Multithreaded Web Server вАФ the capstone chapter that ties together everything from chapters 1вАУ19 into one real, working program: a multithreaded HTTP server with graceful shutdown.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_20.md` вАФ ~5 500 words covering:
  - **20.1 –С—Ц—А –∞“У—Л–љ–і—Л –≤–µ–±-—Б–µ—А–≤–µ—А “Ы“±—А—Г** вАФ `TcpListener::bind`, accepting incoming TCP connections, reading the HTTP request via `BufReader::lines`, the structure of an HTTP request (request-line, headers, blank line, body), writing an HTTP response (status-line, `Content-Length`, body), serving real HTML from a file with `fs::read_to_string`, validating the request line and selecting between `hello.html` and `404.html`.
  - **20.2 –С—Ц—А –∞“У—Л–љ–і—Л —Б–µ—А–≤–µ—А–і—Ц –Ї”©–њ –∞“У—Л–љ–і—Л“У–∞ –∞–є–љ–∞–ї–і—Л—А—Г** вАФ the `/sleep` endpoint demonstrating the single-threaded bottleneck, why per-request `thread::spawn` is wrong (resource exhaustion), the **thread pool** design, the `compiler-driven development` workflow, building `ThreadPool::new(size)` with `mpsc::channel` + `Arc<Mutex<Receiver>>` to share the receiver across workers, the `Worker` struct with id and `JoinHandle`, the `ThreadPool::execute(F: FnOnce() + Send + 'static)` API, the `Job = Box<dyn FnOnce() + Send + 'static>` type alias.
  - **20.3 –Ц–∞–є–ї–∞–њ —В–Њ“Ы—В–∞—Г –Љ–µ–љ —В–∞–Ј–∞–ї–∞—Г** вАФ the `Drop` trait on `ThreadPool`, dropping `sender` (closing the channel) so workers exit their `recv` loop with `Err`, joining all worker threads, the `Option::take` pattern for safely consuming `JoinHandle` from `&mut self`, the `take(2)` integration test demonstrating clean shutdown.
- All earlier-chapter terminology applied (web server вЖТ –≤–µ–±-—Б–µ—А–≤–µ—А, thread pool вЖТ –∞“У—Л–љ-–ґ–Є–љ–∞“У—Л, worker вЖТ –ґ“±–Љ—Л—Б—И—Л, graceful shutdown вЖТ –ґ–∞–є–ї–∞–њ —В–Њ“Ы—В–∞—Г, request-line вЖТ —Б“±—А–∞–љ—Л—Б-–ґ–Њ–ї, status-line вЖТ –ґ–∞“У–і–∞–є-–ґ–Њ–ї).

### Translation series вАФ final summary

This release closes the Rust Book Kazakh translation series begun in v4.7.1 (Chapter 1). The full series:

**Aggregate:** ~95 000 Kazakh words across 20 chapters, ~140 KB of structured prose; 1 543 sentence-level samples in `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`. Code blocks preserved verbatim from the original throughout. All terminology decisions documented in `data/world_core/programming_rust.jsonl` (110-entry curated glossary) and chapter-by-chapter changelogs above.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 19 chapters / 1 459 samples вЖТ **20 chapters / 1 543 samples** (+84 from chapter 20).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** at 3 362 morphemes / 22 145 postings / 3 691 indexed samples вАФ pack is at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling. To take full advantage of all chapters in retrieval, switching the committed index to `--full` mode is the next architectural step.

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold stays at вЙ•490.
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Cadence

Twenty consecutive patches (v4.7.1 through v4.7.20) under ¬Ђ–Ї–∞–ґ–і—Г—О –≥–ї–∞–≤—Г —Б—З–Є—В–∞—В—М –Ј–∞ –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence, no skips. The Rust Book translation thread is now closed.

### What's next (post-v4.7.20)

The Rust Book series is complete. Future v4.7.x patches will likely focus on:
- Switching the morpheme-index build to `--full` mode so all 1 543 rust_book sentences contribute to retrieval.
- Other curated knowledge domains (mathematics_basic, informatics_basic) that may benefit from similar chapter-by-chapter expansions.
- Pipeline tuning вАФ the retrieval ranker now has substantial Rust-domain content to work with.

License & attribution: the Kazakh translation is offered under the same MIT/Apache-2.0 dual license as the original Rust Book, full attribution in `data/raw/rust_book_kk/LICENSE.md`.

## [4.7.19] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 19 (–Ц–µ—В—Ц–ї–і—Ц—А—Ц–ї–≥–µ–љ –Љ“ѓ–Љ–Ї—Ц–љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А) translated, in pack

Nineteenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 19 вАФ Advanced Features вАФ the broadest chapter of the book, surveying five distinct advanced topic areas: **`unsafe` Rust** (the five superpowers вАФ dereferencing raw pointers `*const T` / `*mut T`, calling `unsafe fn`, accessing/modifying `static mut`, implementing an `unsafe trait`, accessing `union` fields; the FFI layer `extern "C"` block and `#[no_mangle]` for outbound; building safe abstractions over unsafe code with the `split_at_mut` worked example); **advanced traits** (associated types `type Item` vs generic `<T>` and why each fits, default generic type parameters with the `Add<Rhs = Self>` operator-overloading example, fully-qualified syntax `<Type as Trait>::method` for disambiguating same-named methods, supertraits with `OutlinePrint: fmt::Display`, the **newtype pattern** for working around the orphan rule); **advanced types** (newtype for type-safety wrappers `struct Years(i64)` / `struct Days(i64)`, type aliases with `type` keyword, the never type `!` and how it unifies with any type, dynamically-sized types `?Sized` and the implicit `Sized` bound on generics); **advanced functions and closures** (function pointers `fn(T) -> U` distinct from `Fn` traits, returning closures via `Box<dyn Fn(...) -> ...>` or `impl Fn(...) -> ...`); **macros** (declarative `macro_rules!` with the `vec!` walkthrough, procedural macros split into derive / attribute / function-like, the `proc-macro` crate convention with `syn` + `quote`).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_19.md` вАФ ~6 500 words (the largest single-chapter translation), code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-19-specific terminology decisions: raw pointer вЖТ **—И–Є–Ї—Ц –Ї”©—А—Б–µ—В–Ї—Ц—И**, FFI вЖТ **—И–µ—В–µ–ї —Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є—П–ї–∞—А—Л–љ—Л“£ –Є–љ—В–µ—А—Д–µ–є—Б—Ц**, union вЖТ **–Њ–і–∞“Ы**, associated type вЖТ **–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л —В–Є–њ**, supertrait вЖТ **–∞—В–∞-—В—А–µ–є—В**, newtype pattern вЖТ **–ґ–∞“£–∞—В–Є–њ “ѓ–ї–≥—Ц—Б—Ц**, type alias вЖТ **—В–Є–њ –±“ѓ—А–Ї–µ–љ—И–µ–≥—Ц**, never type вЖТ **–µ—И“Ы–∞—И–∞–љ-—В–Є–њ `!`**, dynamically-sized type вЖТ **–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы ”©–ї—И–µ–Љ–і—Ц —В–Є–њ**, function pointer вЖТ **—Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є—П –Ї”©—А—Б–µ—В–Ї—Ц—И—Ц**, declarative macro вЖТ **–і–µ–Ї–ї–∞—А–∞—В–Є–≤—В—Ц –Љ–∞–Ї—А–Њ—Б**, procedural macro вЖТ **–њ—А–Њ—Ж–µ–і—Г—А–∞–ї—Л“Ы –Љ–∞–Ї—А–Њ—Б**, attribute macro вЖТ **–∞—В—А–Є–±—Г—В –Љ–∞–Ї—А–Њ—Б**, function-like macro вЖТ **—Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є—П-—Б–Є—П“Ы—В—Л –Љ–∞–Ї—А–Њ—Б**.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 18 chapters / 1 339 samples вЖТ **19 chapters / 1 459 samples** (+120 from chapter 19 вАФ the largest per-chapter contribution so far).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** вАФ pack still at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling.

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold remains вЙ•490.
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Cadence

Per ¬Ђ–Ї–∞–ґ–і—Г—О –≥–ї–∞–≤—Г —Б—З–Є—В–∞—В—М –Ј–∞ –њ–∞—В—З¬ї: each chapter = +1 patch. Next: v4.7.20 = Chapter 20 (Final project вАФ building a multithreaded web server). The final chapter; closes the Rust Book translation series.

## [4.7.18] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 18 (“Ѓ–ї–≥—Ц–ї–µ—А –Љ–µ–љ —Б–∞–є –Ї–µ–ї—В—Ц—А—Г) translated, in pack

Eighteenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 18 вАФ Patterns and Matching вАФ covering pattern syntax across all the places it can appear in `Rust`: `match` arms, `if let` (with chained `else if let`), `while let` (the stack-popping example), `for` loops (with `enumerate` destructuring), `let` statements (irrefutable destructuring), function parameters (the `&(x, y): &(i32, i32)` example); the **refutability** distinction (irrefutable patterns always succeed and are required by `let`/`for`/parameters; refutable patterns may fail and are required by `if let`/`while let`/`match`, with the `let else` form for the early-return on failure idiom). Pattern syntax taxonomy: matching literals, named-variable patterns and shadowing inside `match` scopes, multiple patterns with `|`, range patterns `..=` (numeric and `char`), destructuring (struct, enum, tuple, deeply-nested combinations), ignoring values (`_` placeholder vs `_x` named-but-unused вАФ and how `_` doesn't move ownership while `_x` does), `..` for ignoring remaining parts of structs/tuples, **match guards** (`Some(x) if x % 2 == 0`), and the `@` binding operator for combining a range check with capture.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_18.md` вАФ ~4 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-18-specific terminology decisions: pattern вЖТ **“ѓ–ї–≥—Ц** (already locked); refutable вЖТ **–ґ–Њ“Ы“Ы–∞ —И—Л“У–∞—А—Г“У–∞ –Ї–µ–ї–µ—В—Ц–љ**; irrefutable вЖТ **–ґ–Њ“Ы“Ы–∞ —И—Л“У–∞—А—Г“У–∞ –Ї–µ–ї–Љ–µ–є—В—Ц–љ**; match guard вЖТ **match —И–∞—А—В—Л**; `@` binding вЖТ **`@` –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л—А—Г**; destructuring вЖТ **–±”©–ї—И–µ–Ї—В–µ—Г** (already locked since v4.7.3).

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 17 chapters / 1 289 samples вЖТ **18 chapters / 1 339 samples** (+50 from chapter 18).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** вАФ pack still at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling.

### Tests + counters

- E2E threshold remains вЙ•490.
- Workspace tests: **745 passing**.

### Cadence

Per ¬Ђ–Ї–∞–ґ–і—Г—О –≥–ї–∞–≤—Г —Б—З–Є—В–∞—В—М –Ј–∞ –њ–∞—В—З¬ї: each chapter = +1 patch. Next: v4.7.19 = Chapter 19 (Advanced Features вАФ `unsafe` Rust, advanced traits, advanced types, advanced functions and closures, macros). The penultimate chapter of the book.

## [4.7.17] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 17 (Rust-—В—Л“£ –Њ–±—К–µ–Ї—В—Ц–ї—Ц-–±–∞“У—Л—В—В–∞–ї“У–∞–љ –Љ“ѓ–Љ–Ї—Ц–љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А—Ц) translated, in pack

Seventeenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 17 вАФ Object-Oriented Programming Features of Rust вАФ covering Rust's relationship to OOP: characteristics of OO languages and how Rust delivers (or deliberately doesn't deliver) each вАФ encapsulation via `pub` keyword (`AveragedCollection` worked example with private fields and a public API maintaining an internal invariant); Rust's deliberate absence of inheritance and the trade-off reasoning (over-coupling, hierarchy rigidity), with default trait implementations and trait objects as the practical replacements; static vs dynamic polymorphism (generics with `<T: Trait>` for compile-time monomorphization vs `Box<dyn Trait>` trait objects for runtime dispatch); using **trait objects** for collections of mixed types (the GUI `Draw` / `Screen` example, `Vec<Box<dyn Draw>>`, vtable-based dynamic dispatch, the **object-safe trait** rules вАФ methods on `&self`/`&mut self`, no generic parameters, no `Self` return, why `Clone` cannot be made into `Box<dyn Clone>`); implementing the **state pattern** (the blog-post lifecycle Draft вЖТ PendingReview вЖТ Published example), first in classical OOP style with `Box<dyn State>` and the `self: Box<Self>` consuming method pattern, then in idiomatic Rust style with each state as a separate type (`DraftPost` / `PendingReviewPost` / `Post`), with the trade-off discussion: classical OOP allows runtime extensibility; type-encoded states catch errors at compile time.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_17.md` вАФ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-17-specific terminology decisions: object-oriented programming вЖТ **–Њ–±—К–µ–Ї—В—Ц–ї—Ц-–±–∞“У—Л—В—В–∞–ї“У–∞–љ –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г**, encapsulation вЖТ **—В“±—Б–∞—Г–ї–∞—Г**, inheritance вЖТ **–Љ“±—А–∞–≥–µ—А–ї—Ц–Ї**, polymorphism вЖТ **–Ї”©–њ –њ—Ц—И—Ц–љ–і—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї**, dynamic dispatch вЖТ **–і–Є–љ–∞–Љ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы —В–∞—А–∞—В–њ–∞**, static dispatch вЖТ **—Б—В–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы —В–∞—А–∞—В–њ–∞**, vtable вЖТ kept verbatim, object-safe вЖТ **–љ—Л—Б–∞–љ-“Ы–∞—Г—Ц–њ—Б—Ц–Ј**, state pattern вЖТ **–Ї“ѓ–є “ѓ–ї–≥—Ц—Б—Ц**, design pattern вЖТ **–ґ–Њ–±–∞–ї–∞—Г “ѓ–ї–≥—Ц—Б—Ц**, trait object вЖТ **—В—А–µ–є—В-–љ—Л—Б–∞–љ** (already locked since v4.7.0).

## [4.7.16] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 16 (“Ъ–Њ—А“Ы—Л–љ—Л—И—Б—Л–Ј “Ы–∞—В–∞—А–ї–∞—Б –Њ—А—Л–љ–і–∞—Г) translated, in pack

Sixteenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 16 вАФ Fearless Concurrency вАФ covering Rust's compile-time-enforced concurrency primitives: spawning OS threads with `std::thread::spawn` and waiting for them with `JoinHandle::join`, the closure-with-`move` pattern for transferring ownership into a thread; message-passing concurrency via `std::sync::mpsc::channel` (transmitter `tx` / receiver `rx`, ownership transfer on `send`, blocking `recv` vs non-blocking `try_recv`, iterating `for received in rx`, multiple producers via `tx.clone()`); shared-state concurrency via `Mutex<T>` (the `lock()` API returning a `MutexGuard`, `Drop`-based auto-release, the `Arc<Mutex<T>>` combination for cross-thread shared mutable state with the 10-thread counter example); the `Send` and `Sync` marker traits as the type-system foundation that makes this all safe (the `Rc<T>` / `Arc<T>` distinction explained via these traits, `RefCell<T>` not being `Sync`, why manual `unsafe impl Send/Sync` is rare and dangerous).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_16.md` вАФ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-16-specific terminology decisions: thread вЖТ **–∞“У—Л–љ** (already locked); message passing вЖТ **—Е–∞–±–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞ –∞—А“Ы—Л–ї—Л –∞–ї–Љ–∞—Б—Г**; channel вЖТ **–Ї–∞–љ–∞–ї** (already); transmitter вЖТ **–ґ—Ц–±–µ—А—Г—И—Ц**; receiver вЖТ **“Ы–∞–±—Л–ї–і–∞—Г—И—Л**; mutex вЖТ kept as `Mutex` (the `Rust` type) plus the conceptual term **”©–Ј–∞—А–∞ —Н–Ї—Б–Ї–ї—О–Ј–Є–≤—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї**; shared state вЖТ **–Њ—А—В–∞“Ы –Ї“ѓ–є**; atomic reference counting вЖТ **–∞—В–Њ–Љ–і—Л“Ы —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ-–µ—Б–µ–њ—В–µ—Г**; `Send` / `Sync` вЖТ kept verbatim (marker trait names); deadlock вЖТ **”©–Ј–∞—А–∞ —В–Њ—Б“Ы–∞—Г—Л–ї**.

## [4.7.15] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 15 (–Р“Ы—Л–ї–і—Л —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ–ї–µ—А) translated, in pack

Fifteenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 15 вАФ Smart Pointers вАФ covering Rust's smart-pointer ecosystem and how each kind extends the basic reference model: `Box<T>` for heap allocation and recursive types (the cons-list example showing why a recursive enum's size is unbounded without `Box`); the `Deref` trait and how it enables `*` on smart pointers (writing a `MyBox<T>` from scratch and implementing `Deref::deref`), deref coercion as the compiler-driven chain `&MyBox<String>` вЖТ `&String` вЖТ `&str`; the `Drop` trait for cleanup logic that runs when a value goes out of scope, why `drop()` may not be called manually but `std::mem::drop` may be; `Rc<T>` for multiple ownership in single-threaded contexts (the cons-list example showing how cloning increments `strong_count`, why `Rc<T>` is read-only); `RefCell<T>` and the **interior mutability** pattern that defers borrow checking to runtime (the mock-object pattern, panic on rule violation); combining `Rc<RefCell<T>>` for the shared-ownership-with-mutation use case; reference cycles as a memory-leak shape Rust's ownership system does not prevent, and `Weak<T>` references that don't extend the lifetime of the pointed-to value (the parent-child node tree example with strong children + weak parent links).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_15.md` вАФ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-15-specific terminology decisions: smart pointer вЖТ **–∞“Ы—Л–ї–і—Л —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ** (already locked since v4.7.0), recursive type вЖТ **—А–µ–Ї—Г—А—Б–Є–≤—В—Ц —В–Є–њ**, deref coercion вЖТ **–і–µref-–Є—В–µ—А—Г**, interior mutability вЖТ **—Ц—И–Ї—Ц ”©–Ј–≥–µ—А–Љ–µ–ї—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї**, mock object вЖТ **mock-–љ—Л—Б–∞–љ**, reference count вЖТ **—Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ–ї–µ—А —Б–∞–љ–∞“У—Л**, reference cycle вЖТ **—Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ —Ж–Є–Ї–ї—Л**, memory leak вЖТ **–ґ–∞–і –∞“У—Л—В–њ–∞—Б—Л**, weak reference вЖТ **”Щ–ї—Б—Ц–Ј —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ**, `Rc::strong_count` / `Rc::weak_count` вЖТ kept verbatim.

## [4.7.13] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 13 (–§—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є–Њ–љ–∞–ї —В—Ц–ї–і—Ц–Ї –Љ“ѓ–Љ–Ї—Ц–љ–і—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А: –Є—В–µ—А–∞—В–Њ—А–ї–∞—А –Љ–µ–љ –ґ–∞–±—Г–ї–∞—А) translated, in pack

Thirteenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 13 вАФ Functional Language Features: Iterators and Closures вАФ covering Rust's two key functional-programming primitives: closures (anonymous functions that capture their environment, the three-tier `FnOnce` / `FnMut` / `Fn` trait hierarchy, `move` keyword for ownership transfer to a closure body, the `Vec::sort_by_key` worked example) and iterators (the `Iterator` trait and the `next` method, `iter` / `iter_mut` / `into_iter` distinction, lazy evaluation, consuming adapters like `sum` / `count` / `collect` vs producing adapters like `map` / `filter`, chaining `(1..=10).filter(...).map(...).sum()`, capturing closures inside iterator chains). Then refactoring the v4.7.12 minigrep using these tools (removing `clone` from `Config::build` by passing an iterator instead of a slice, condensing the `search` / `search_case_insensitive` functions to one-line iterator chains, the loops-vs-iterators discussion). Closes with the **zero-cost abstraction** explanation: iterator chains compile to assembly indistinguishable from hand-rolled loops; sometimes more efficient because the rigid abstraction shape gives the compiler stronger optimisation guarantees.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_13.md` вАФ ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-13-specific terminology decisions: closure вЖТ **–ґ–∞–±—Г** (already locked since v4.7.0 programming_rust glossary), capture вЖТ **“±—Б—В–∞—Г**, `Fn`/`FnMut`/`FnOnce` вЖТ **`Fn` / `FnMut` / `FnOnce` —В—А–µ–є—В—В–µ—А—Ц** (transliterated, kept verbatim), iterator adapter вЖТ **–Є—В–µ—А–∞—В–Њ—А-–±–µ–є—Ц–Љ–і–µ—Г—Ц—И**, lazy evaluation вЖТ **–ї–∞–Ј–∞–є –µ—Б–µ–њ—В–µ—Г**, zero-cost abstraction вЖТ **–љ”©–ї–і—Ц–Ї —И—Л“У—Л–љ–і—Л –∞–±—Б—В—А–∞–Ї—Ж–Є—П**, consuming adapter вЖТ **—В“±—В—Л–љ–∞—В—Л–љ –±–µ–є—Ц–Љ–і–µ—Г**, producing adapter вЖТ **–ґ–∞“£–∞ –Є—В–µ—А–∞—В–Њ—А —И—Л“У–∞—А–∞—В—Л–љ –±–µ–є—Ц–Љ–і–µ—Г**.

## [4.7.12] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 12 (–Ъ—Ц—А—Ц—Б-—И—Л“У—Л—Б –ґ–Њ–±–∞—Б—Л: –Ї–Њ–Љ–∞–љ–і–∞ –ґ–Њ–ї—Л –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞—Б—Л–љ “Ы“±—А—Г) translated, in pack

Twelfth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 12 вАФ An I/O Project: Building a Command Line Program (mini-grep) вАФ the largest practical chapter that ties together everything from chapters 1вАУ11 into one real working CLI program. Sections: 12.1 accepting command-line arguments via `std::env::args`; 12.2 reading a file with `std::fs::read_to_string`; 12.3 refactoring for modularity and error handling (separation of concerns, extracting `parse_config` and then a `Config` struct, the `Config::build` constructor pattern, fixing error handling with `Result` + `unwrap_or_else` + `eprintln!` + `process::exit`, extracting a `run` function, splitting code into a library crate `src/lib.rs`); 12.4 TDD development of the `search` function (writing a failing test first, implementing the minimum code to pass, then refactoring); 12.5 working with environment variables (`env::var("IGNORE_CASE")`, the `search_case_insensitive` companion function); 12.6 writing error messages to standard error instead of standard output (`eprintln!` vs `println!`, the Unix stdout/stderr separation, `> output.txt` redirection demonstration).

This is the practical chapter that demonstrates how all earlier chapters' concepts come together: modules, ownership, references, traits, error handling, tests вАФ all in one ~150-line program.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_12.md` вАФ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-12-specific terminology decisions: command-line argument вЖТ **–Ї–Њ–Љ–∞–љ–і–∞–ї—Л“Ы –ґ–Њ–ї –∞—А–≥—Г–Љ–µ–љ—В—Ц**, separation of concerns вЖТ **–ґ–∞—Г–∞–њ–Ї–µ—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ—А–і—Ц –±”©–ї—Г**, test-driven development (TDD) вЖТ **—В–µ—Б—В–Ї–µ –љ–µ–≥—Ц–Ј–і–µ–ї–≥–µ–љ ”Щ–Ј—Ц—А–ї–µ—Г**, standard output вЖТ **—Б—В–∞–љ–і–∞—А—В—В—Л —И—Л“У–∞—А—Г**, standard error вЖТ **—Б—В–∞–љ–і–∞—А—В—В—Л “Ы–∞—В–µ**, environment variable вЖТ **–Њ—А—В–∞ –∞–є–љ—Л–Љ–∞–ї—Л—Б—Л**, case-insensitive вЖТ **”Щ—А—Ц–њ —А–µ–≥–Є—Б—В—А—Ц–љ –µ—Б–Ї–µ—А–Љ–µ—Г**, constructor вЖТ **–Ї–Њ–љ—Б—В—А—Г–Ї—В–Њ—А** (transliteration), trait object вЖТ **—В—А–µ–є—В-–љ—Л—Б–∞–љ**.

## [4.7.10] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 10 (–Ц–∞–ї–њ—Л–ї–∞–Љ–∞ —В–Є–њ—В–µ—А, —В—А–µ–є—В—В–µ—А –Љ–µ–љ —В—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ–µ—А–Ј—Ц–Љ—Ц) translated, in pack

Tenth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 10 вАФ Generic Types, Traits, and Lifetimes вАФ the most theoretically dense chapter of the entire book. Three abstraction layers in one chapter: types (via generic type parameters), behaviour (via traits) and references' validity (via lifetimes).

### Translation

Three sections cover the full Rust abstraction stack:

- **10.1 –Ц–∞–ї–њ—Л–ї–∞–Љ–∞ –Љ”Щ–ї—Ц–Љ–µ—В —В“ѓ—А–ї–µ—А—Ц** вАФ generics in functions (`largest<T: PartialOrd>`), structs (`Point<T>`, `Point<T, U>`), enums (`Option<T>`, `Result<T, E>`), and methods (`impl<T> Point<T>`, type-restricted `impl Point<f64>`); the **monomorphization** explanation вАФ generic abstractions cost zero at runtime because the compiler emits a specialized copy per concrete type.
- **10.2 –Ґ—А–µ–є—В—В–µ—А: –Њ—А—В–∞“Ы —В”Щ—А—В—Ц–њ—В—Ц –∞–љ—Л“Ы—В–∞—Г** вАФ defining traits (`Summary`), implementing them on types (`impl Summary for NewsArticle`), the **orphan rule** (you can implement a trait on a type only if either the trait or the type is defined in your crate), default implementations and how they can call other trait methods, traits as parameters via `&impl Summary` shorthand vs `<T: Summary>` trait-bound syntax, multiple bounds with `+`, the `where` clause for readability, returning `impl Trait`, conditionally-implemented methods (`impl<T: Display + PartialOrd> Pair<T>`), blanket implementations (`impl<T: Display> ToString for T`).
- **10.3 –Ґ—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ–µ—А–Ј—Ц–Љ—Ц –∞—А“Ы—Л–ї—Л —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ–ї–µ—А–і—Ц —А–∞—Б—В–∞—Г** вАФ preventing dangling references, the borrow checker, generic lifetimes in functions (`fn longest<'a>(x: &'a str, y: &'a str) -> &'a str`), thinking in terms of lifetimes, lifetimes in struct definitions (`ImportantExcerpt<'a>`), the three lifetime elision rules, lifetimes in method definitions (`impl<'a> ImportantExcerpt<'a>`), the `'static` lifetime (string literals, when not to use it), generic types + trait bounds + lifetimes combined in one signature.

### Translation notes

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_10.md` вАФ ~5 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-10-specific terminology decisions: monomorphization вЖТ **–Љ–Њ–љ–Њ–Љ–Њ—А—Д—В–∞—Г**, default implementation вЖТ **”Щ–і–µ–њ–Ї—Ц —Ц—Б–Ї–µ –∞—Б—Л—А—Г**, blanket implementation вЖТ **–ґ–∞–ї–њ—Л —Ц—Б–Ї–µ –∞—Б—Л—А—Г**, trait bound вЖТ **—В—А–µ–є—В —И–µ–Ї—В–µ—Г—Ц**, where clause вЖТ **where –Ї–ї–∞—Г–Ј–∞—Б—Л**, lifetime elision вЖТ **—В—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ–µ—А–Ј—Ц–Љ—Ц–љ —В“ѓ—Б—Ц—А—Ц–њ –ґ–∞–Ј—Г**, orphan rule вЖТ **–Њ—А—Д–∞–љ-–µ—А–µ–ґ–µ**, `impl Trait` syntax вЖТ kept as `impl Trait`.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 9 chapters / 675 samples вЖТ **10 chapters / 767 samples** (+92 from chapter 10).
- Morpheme index: **unchanged** вАФ pack still at the 500-per-pack default-mode ceiling.

## [4.7.9] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 9 (“Ъ–∞—В–µ–љ—Ц ”©“£–і–µ—Г) translated, in pack

Ninth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 9 вАФ Error Handling вАФ covering Rust's two-tier error model: **unrecoverable errors with `panic!`** (the panic message format, the array-indexing example, `RUST_BACKTRACE=1`, the `panic = "abort"` profile setting and unwind-vs-abort trade-off) and **recoverable errors with `Result<T, E>`** (the `Result` enum definition, `File::open` returning `Result<File, io::Error>`, matching on the error to take different actions, distinguishing error kinds via `error.kind()` and the deeply-nested-match trade-off, `unwrap` and `expect` as shortcuts for prototypes/tests, propagating errors with explicit `match`, the `?` operator and how it short-circuits to return `Err` from the function, the `?`-chained call style, the standard-library `fs::read_to_string` as the canonical fully-condensed form, error type conversion via the `From` trait, where `?` may be used (`Result`, `Option`, `main` returning `Result<(), Box<dyn Error>>`)). Tarau ends with **when to panic vs when to return Result** guidelines (prototypes/tests, contract violations, parsing user input, trait-encoded invariants like the `Guess` 1вАУ100 example).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_09.md` вАФ ~4 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-9-specific terminology decisions: error propagation вЖТ **“Ы–∞—В–µ–љ—Ц —В–∞—А–∞—В—Г**, error conversion вЖТ **“Ы–∞—В–µ–љ—Ц —В“ѓ—А–ї–µ–љ–і—Ц—А—Г**, backtrace вЖТ **—И–µ–≥—Ц–љ—Ц—Б —Ц–Ј—Ц**, stack unwinding вЖТ **—Б—В–µ–Ї –ґ–∞–і—Л–љ –Ї–µ—А—Ц –∞–є–љ–∞–ї–і—Л—А—Г**, abort вЖТ **“ѓ–Ј—Г**, type alias вЖТ **—В–Є–њ –ї–∞“Ы–∞–њ –∞—В—Л** (deferred вАФ not used in this chapter), panic вЖТ **panic** (kept as `panic!` macro reference); `Result<T, E>` and `Option<T>` keep the v4.7.0/4.7.6-locked enam-–љ“±—Б“Ы–∞–ї–∞—А—Л terminology.

## [4.7.8] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 8 (–Ц–∞–ї–њ—Л “±–ґ—Л–Љ–і–∞—А) translated, in pack (past committed-index ceiling)

Eighth chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 8 вАФ Common Collections вАФ covering the three most-used standard-library collection types: `Vec<T>` (creating with `Vec::new` and `vec!` macro, updating via `push`, reading with `&v[i]` panic vs. `v.get(i)` `Option`, the borrow rule preventing concurrent index reads with `push` due to potential reallocation, iterating over `&v` and `&mut v` with `*i` dereferencing, storing multiple types via enum variants, drop semantics); `String` (UTF-8 commitment as the source of complexity, creating with `String::new` / `to_string` / `String::from`, updating with `push_str` / `push` / `+` / `format!`, why indexing is forbidden with the `–Ч–і—А–∞–≤—Б—В–≤—Г–є—В–µ` 24-byte example, byte-aligned slicing with `&s[a..b]` and panic on mid-codepoint cut, iterating with `chars` for Unicode scalars and `bytes` for raw bytes, why grapheme clusters require external crates); `HashMap<K, V>` (creating, `get` returning `Option<&V>` with `.copied().unwrap_or(0)` idiom, ownership transfer for non-`Copy` keys/values, three update strategies вАФ `insert` overwriting, `entry().or_insert()` for missing-key insertion, the word-counter `*count += 1` pattern with mutable references, the SipHash default and DoS-resistance trade-off).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_08.md` вАФ ~4 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-8-specific terminology decisions: collection вЖТ **“±–ґ—Л–Љ** (already in lexicon), grapheme cluster вЖТ **–≥—А–∞—Д–µ–Љ–∞ –Ї–ї–∞—Б—В–µ—А—Ц**, hash function вЖТ **—Е—Н—И —Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є—П—Б—Л**, dereference вЖТ **–і–µ—А–µ—Д–µ—А–µ–љ—Б**, byte boundary вЖТ **–±–∞–є—В —И–µ–≥—Ц**, Unicode scalar вЖТ **Unicode —Б–Ї–∞–ї—П—А-–Љ”Щ–љ**, SipHash вЖТ **SipHash** (kept as-is, named algorithm).

## [4.7.7] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 7 (–С—Г–Љ–∞–ї–∞—А–Љ–µ–љ, —Б–∞–љ–і—Л“Ы—В–∞—А–Љ–µ–љ, –Љ–Њ–і—Г–ї—М–і–µ—А–Љ–µ–љ –ґ–Њ–±–∞–љ—Л –±–∞—Б“Ы–∞—А—Г) translated, ingested

Seventh chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 7 вАФ Managing Growing Projects with Packages, Crates, and Modules вАФ covering the four layers of Rust's modular system: **packages** (the Cargo unit, `Cargo.toml`, at-most-one library + any number of binary crates, `src/main.rs` / `src/lib.rs` / `src/bin/*.rs` conventions); **crates** (binary vs library, the crate root concept); **modules** (defining with `mod`, the module tree starting from `crate`, in-line vs separate-file declarations); **paths** (absolute paths starting from `crate`, relative paths via `self` / `super` / module names; the privacy rule вАФ everything is private by default; `pub` opens one layer at a time; `pub struct` requires per-field `pub`; `pub enum` is variants-all-public); **bringing paths into scope with `use`** (idiomatic patterns вАФ import the parent module for functions, import the type itself for structs/enums/types like `HashMap`/`String`/`Vec`; `as` for renaming on collision; `pub use` for re-exporting; nested paths `{}` syntax; `self` in nested paths; `*` glob operator and when not to use it); external crates (the `[dependencies]` block and `std` as the always-available special case); separating modules into different files (`mod foo;` declaration and the `src/foo.rs` / `src/foo/mod.rs` lookup paths).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_07.md` вАФ ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all earlier-chapter terminology applied.
- Chapter-7-specific terminology decisions: package вЖТ **–±—Г–Љ–∞**, binary crate вЖТ **–µ–Ї—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї —Б–∞–љ–і—Л“Ы**, library crate вЖТ **–Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ—Е–∞–љ–∞ —Б–∞–љ–і—Л“У—Л**, crate root вЖТ **—Б–∞–љ–і—Л“Ы —В“ѓ–±—Ц—А—Ц**, module tree вЖТ **–Љ–Њ–і—Г–ї—М –∞“У–∞—И—Л**, privacy вЖТ **–ґ–µ–Ї–µ–ї—Ц–Ї**, absolute path вЖТ **–∞–±—Б–Њ–ї—О—В—В—Ц–Ї –ґ–Њ–ї**, relative path вЖТ **—Б–∞–ї—Л—Б—В—Л—А–Љ–∞–ї—Л –ґ–Њ–ї**, re-export вЖТ **“Ы–∞–є—В–∞ —Н–Ї—Б–њ–Њ—А—В—В–∞—Г**, glob operator вЖТ **glob –Њ–њ–µ—А–∞—В–Њ—А**, nested paths вЖТ **—В–Њ“У—Л—Б–њ–∞–ї—Л –ґ–Њ–ї–і–∞—А**, items вЖТ **—Н–ї–µ–Љ–µ–љ—В—В–µ—А**, prelude вЖТ **–Ї—Ц—А—Ц—Б–њ–µ**.

## [4.7.4] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 4 (–Ш–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В—Ц —В“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ—Г) translated, ingested

Fourth chapter вАФ the central, most conceptual chapter of the entire book. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 4 вАФ Understanding Ownership вАФ covering the language's defining idea: stack vs heap, the three ownership rules, variable scope, the `String` type vs string literals, memory allocation and `drop`, ownership transfer (move), `clone` for deep copy, the `Copy` trait, ownership and function calls, return values; references and borrowing (`&T` immutable, `&mut T` mutable, the two reference rules вАФ exclusivity of mutable references vs. shared immutable references вАФ and how data races are prevented at compile time, dangling reference prevention); the slice type (`&str` string slices, `&[T]` array slices, range `..` syntax variants `[a..b]` / `[..n]` / `[m..]` / `[..]`, `&str` as the more general parameter type vs. `&String`).

This is the chapter for which the v4.7.0 terminology decisions (–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї / “Ы–∞—А—Л–Ј“У–∞ –∞–ї—Г / “Ы–∞—А—Л–Ј —В–µ–Ї—Б–µ—А–≥—Ц—И / —В—Ц—А—И—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ–µ—А–Ј—Ц–Љ—Ц / —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ / ”©–Ј–≥–µ—А–Љ–µ–ї—Ц / —В“±—А–∞“Ы—В—Л / —Б—В—А—Г–Ї—В / –µ–љ–∞–Љ) were specifically locked. They are now applied throughout the canonical translation.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_04.md` вАФ ~6 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all v4.7.0/4.7.1/4.7.2/4.7.3 terminology applied, ownership-specific terms added below.
- Chapter-4-specific terminology decisions: ownership rules вЖТ **–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї –µ—А–µ–ґ–µ–ї–µ—А—Ц**, move вЖТ **–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В—Ц –∞—Г—Л—Б—В—Л—А—Г**, deep copy вЖТ **—В–µ—А–µ“£ –Ї”©—И—Ц—А—Г**, clone вЖТ `clone` (transliteration, kept as English for the method name; conceptual term ¬Ђ—В–µ—А–µ“£ –Ї”©—И—Ц—А—Г¬ї), data race вЖТ **–ґ–∞—А—Л—Б —И–∞—А—В—Л**, dangling reference вЖТ **–ґ–∞–±–∞–є—Л —Б—Ц–ї—В–µ–Љ–µ**, slice вЖТ **—В—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ**, string slice вЖТ **–ґ–Њ–ї —В—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ—Ц**, byte literal вЖТ **–±–∞–є—В –ї–Є—В–µ—А–∞–ї—Л**, `Copy` trait вЖТ `Copy` **—В—А–µ–є—В—Ц**.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 3 chapters / 231 samples вЖТ **4 chapters / 328 samples** (+97 from chapter 4).
- Morpheme index: distinct morphemes 3 307 вЖТ **3 330** (+23); total postings 19 447 вЖТ **20 430** (+983); indexed samples 3 422 вЖТ **3 519** (+97).

Per ¬Ђ–Ї–∞–ґ–і—Г—О –≥–ї–∞–≤—Г —Б—З–Є—В–∞—В—М –Ј–∞ –њ–∞—В—З¬ї: each chapter = +1 patch. Next: v4.7.5 = Chapter 5 (Using Structs to Structure Related Data).

## [4.7.3] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 3 (–Ц–∞–ї–њ—Л –±–∞“У–і–∞—А–ї–∞–Љ–∞–ї–∞—Г “±“У—Л–Љ–і–∞—А—Л) translated, ingested

Third chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 3 вАФ Common Programming Concepts вАФ covering the foundational concepts that recur throughout Rust: variables and mutability (default-immutable bindings, `mut` keyword, constants `const` with mandatory type annotation and SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention, shadowing via `let` and how it differs from `mut` including type-changing); data types (scalar вАФ integer types `i8`/`i16`/`i32`/`i64`/`i128` and unsigned/signed pairs with `usize`/`isize` machine-dependent forms, integer overflow behaviour in debug vs. release, floating-point `f32`/`f64`, numeric operations, boolean, character; compound вАФ tuples with destructuring and dot-index access, the unit `()`, arrays with type/length annotation `[i32; 5]` and out-of-bounds panic); functions (`fn` keyword, snake_case convention, parameters with mandatory type annotations, the critical statement-vs-expression distinction, block expressions, return values via `->`); comments (`//`, `/* */`, doc comments `///`); control flow (`if` / `else if` / `else` with bool-only conditions, `if` as an expression in `let`, `loop` with `break value`, loop labels for nested loops, `while`, `for` over arrays and ranges, range expressions `1..4` exclusive vs `1..=4` inclusive, `.rev()`).

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_03.md` вАФ ~5 000 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all v4.7.0/4.7.1/4.7.2 terminology applied.
- Chapter-3-specific terminology decisions: scalar вЖТ **–ґ–∞–ї“У—Л–Ј**, compound вЖТ **“Ы“±—А–∞–Љ–∞**, integer overflow вЖТ **–±“ѓ—В—Ц–љ —Б–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ –∞—Б—Л–њ –Ї–µ—В—Г—Ц**, floating-point вЖТ **“Ы–∞–ї“Ы—Л–Љ–∞–ї—Л “ѓ—В—Ц—А–ї—Ц**, numeric operations вЖТ **—Б–∞–љ–і—Л“Ы –∞–Љ–∞–ї–і–∞—А**, tuple destructuring вЖТ **–±”©–ї—И–µ–Ї—В–µ—Г**, statement vs expression вЖТ **—Б”©–є–ї–µ–Љ –Љ–µ–љ ”©—А–љ–µ–Ї**, function call вЖТ **—Д—Г–љ–Ї—Ж–Є—П —И–∞“Ы—Л—А—Г—Л**, doc comment вЖТ **“Ы“±–ґ–∞—В—В–∞–Љ–∞ —В“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ—Ц–Ї—В–µ–Љ–µ—Б—Ц**, loop label вЖТ **—Ж–Є–Ї–ї –±–µ–ї–≥—Ц—Б—Ц**, range вЖТ **–і–Є–∞–њ–∞–Ј–Њ–љ**, inclusive/exclusive range вЖТ **“Ы–∞–Љ—В—Л–ї“У–∞–љ/“Ы–∞–Љ—В—Л–ї–Љ–∞“У–∞–љ –і–Є–∞–њ–∞–Ј–Њ–љ**, mutability вЖТ **”©–Ј–≥–µ—А–Љ–µ–ї—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї**.

### Pipeline impact

- `data/curated/rust_book_kk_pack.json`: 2 chapters / 134 samples вЖТ **3 chapters / 231 samples** (+97 from chapter 3).
- Morpheme index: distinct morphemes 3 265 вЖТ **3 307** (+42); total postings 18 485 вЖТ **19 447** (+962); indexed samples 3 325 вЖТ **3 422** (+97).

## [4.7.2] вАФ 2026-04-29 вАФ Rust Book Chapter 2 (–°–∞–љ–і—Л —В–∞–±—Г –Њ–є—Л–љ—Л) translated, ingested

Second chapter under ¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–∞ = –њ–∞—В—З¬ї cadence. Full Kazakh translation of Rust Book Chapter 2 вАФ Programming a Guessing Game вАФ covering the hands-on guessing game project: setting up a new Cargo project, processing user input via `std::io::stdin().read_line()`, mutable variables (`let mut guess = String::new()`), references and mutable references (`&mut guess`), `Result`-based error handling with `.expect()`, adding the external `rand` crate as a Cargo dependency (`Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` block, semantic versioning `^0.8.5`), generating random integers in a range (`rand::thread_rng().gen_range(1..=100)`), comparing values with `std::cmp::Ordering` and `match` expressions (`Less / Greater / Equal`), type mismatch errors and shadowing for type conversion (`let guess: u32 = guess.trim().parse().expect(...)`), looping with `loop`, breaking on success, and graceful invalid-input handling via `match Result { Ok(num) => num, Err(_) => continue }`.

### Translation

- New `data/raw/rust_book_kk/chapter_02.md` вАФ ~3 500 words, code blocks preserved verbatim, all v4.7.0 terminology applied (–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї / “Ы–∞—А—Л–Ј“У–∞ –∞–ї—Г / —Б–∞–љ–і—Л“Ы / —В—А–µ–є—В / –µ–љ–∞–Љ / —Б—В—А—Г–Ї—В); chapter-2-specific terminology decisions: random number вЖТ **–Ї–µ–Ј–і–µ–є—Б–Њ“Ы —Б–∞–љ**, mutable variable вЖТ **”©–Ј–≥–µ—А–Љ–µ–ї—Ц –∞–є–љ—Л–Љ–∞–ї—Л**, scope вЖТ **–∞—Г–Љ–∞“Ы**, parse вЖТ **—В–∞–ї–і–∞—Г**, type inference вЖТ **—В“ѓ—А-“Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л**, semantic versioning вЖТ **—Б–µ–Љ–∞–љ—В–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы –љ“±—Б“Ы–∞–ї–∞—Г**.
