R5 +503 far exceeds the theoretical C(13,2)=78 from the k√ґ–ї—Ц–Ї hub alone because curated IsA chains **cross-reference** existing world_core structure: transport professions (–ґ“ѓ—А–≥—Ц–Ј—Г—И—Ц / –њ–Є–ї–Њ—В / –Ї–∞–њ–Є—В–∞–љ / –Љ–∞—И–Є–љ–Є—Б—В) all IsA –Љ–∞–Љ–∞–љ вАФ joining the existing –Љ–∞–Љ–∞–љ cluster from kz_literature / society, which has ~20 sibling entries already. Plus –Њ—В—Л–љ cluster joining substances, plus q–Њ–Ј“У–∞–ї—Л—Б joining the action hub.

### Graph

Nodes: 3 284 вЖТ **3 315** (+31); edges: 12 308 вЖТ **12 350** (+42). Most-connected content nouns unchanged: –∞–і–∞–Љ (289), –ґ–µ—А (218), –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ (207), “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы (201), –ґ—Л–ї (151).

### Single-curated-domain knowledge leverage

The patch adds 42 curated facts and produces **+555 rule derivations** вАФ a net-effective knowledge-growth ratio of ~13√Ч per added fact through the reasoner's cross-chain multiplier. This is exactly the compounding effect the World Core direction targets: one human-authored fact reverberates through existing curated structure to produce many provably correct downstream claims.

### Validator

```
$ cargo run -p adam-reasoning --bin validate_world_core
```

## [4.0.6] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ Narrow attributive blocklist in `is_closed_class`

Continuing the noise-elimination axis from v4.0.5. That patch shipped the **rightmost-subject** fix in `temporal_after`; spot-check then surfaced a distinct noise class the rightmost scan couldn't catch: attributive `-–ї—Л“Ы / -–ї—Ц–Ї / -–Є` adjective-derivations that the FST tags as bare nouns. When the real NP head got consumed in the ablative slot, the attributive modifier was the *only* remaining nominative candidate before the postposition вАФ so both left-to-right and right-to-left scans picked it.

### Fix

Narrow blocklist added directly to `is_closed_class`. Nine roots, each spotted on the committed v4.0.5 runtime:

| root | gloss | v4.0.5 After-fact count |
|---|---|---:|
| `–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї` | worldwide | 41 |
| `“±–Ј–∞“Ы` | long (duration) | 9 |
| `”Щ—Б–Ї–µ—А–Є` | military | 6 |
| `—П–і—А–Њ–ї—Л“Ы` | nuclear | 3 |
| `—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы` | tropical | 2 |
| `–ґ—Л–љ—Л—Б—В—Л“Ы` | sexual / gender | 2 |
| `–ґ–∞—А—В` | truncated stem of ¬Ђ–ґ–∞—А—В—Л¬ї (half) | 3 |
| `–∞—А–∞—Б—Л–љ–і–∞“У` | possessive-locative fragment | 4 |
| `—В–∞“У—Л` | "again / also" (adverb tagged as noun) | 3 |

Applies globally via `is_closed_class`, not just to `temporal_after`. Every pattern matcher that consults the helper (all 11) now rejects these as subjects *and* as head-noun objects in the few places where head-nouns are scanned.

### Important non-inclusions

Three roots deliberately **excluded** from the blocklist:

- `“±–ї—В-–∞–Ј–∞—В—В—Л“Ы` (national-liberation) вАФ real compound noun; legitimate subject in some world_core / IsA contexts.
- `–±–µ–ї–≥—Ц` (sign), `—Б–∞–љ` (number), `–ґ“±—А—В` (folk) вАФ all legitimate nouns.

The regression test `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_6_attributives` asserts both: the 9 blocked roots fail, and the 4 legitimate-noun roots pass through.

### Measured effect

Re-ran extract + reasoner pipeline on the committed 200 k-sample runtime. All 9 attributive / fragment roots verified absent from `facts.json` as subjects (spot-checked per root: 0 occurrences each).

| | v4.0.5 | v4.0.6 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 887 | **13 703** | **вИТ184** |
| After facts | 269 | **219** | **вИТ50** (primary target вАФ attributive adjectives) |
| DoesTo facts | 9 289 | 9 192 | **вИТ97** (cross-matcher cleanup) |
| GoesTo facts | 1 617 | 1 595 | **вИТ22** |
| LivesIn facts | 292 | 289 | вИТ3 |
| RelatedTo facts | 1 467 | 1 458 | вИТ9 |
| IsA facts | 525 | 524 | вИТ1 |
| PartOf facts | 116 | 115 | вИТ1 |
| Has facts | 226 | 225 | вИТ1 |
| HasQuantity / InDomain / Causes | 40 / 24 / 22 | 40 / 24 / 22 | unchanged |

The blocklist applies globally via `is_closed_class`, so gains span every matcher that consults the helper вАФ not just `temporal_after`. The DoesTo `вИТ97` and GoesTo `вИТ22` drops are the attributive-as-agent cases that the Codex review didn't surface on the After side: e.g. ¬Ђ–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї “ѓ—А–і—Ц—Б X-–љ—Ц —В—Г–і—Л—А–∞–і—Л¬ї вЖТ pre-v4.0.6 extracted as `(–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї, DoesTo, X)`.

Per-rule derivation deltas:

| rule | v4.0.5 | v4.0.6 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 361 | 361 | 0 |
| R2_has_inheritance | 422 | 417 | вИТ5 |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 26 | 26 | 0 |
| R5_shared_is_a_target | 5 437 | 5 437 | 0 |
| R6_lives_in_via_part_of | 36 | 36 | 0 |
| R7_goes_to_via_part_of | 297 | 300 | +3 |
| R8_after_transitivity | 714 | 734 | +20 |
| **total derivations** | **7 293** | **7 311** | **+18** |

Small R7 and R8 *increases* are structural: with fewer attributive-subjected base facts, the reasoner's `seen_triples` dedup set is smaller, so a few chains that were previously short-circuited now fire freely. The new derivations use clean content-noun subjects where the noisy attributive ones were blocked.

Graph: 3 287 вЖТ **3 284** nodes (вИТ3), 12 439 вЖТ **12 308** edges (вИТ131). Most-connected content nouns: **–∞–і–∞–Љ (288), –ґ–µ—А (218), –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ (207), “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы (201), –ґ—Л–ї (151)**.

### Tests

**463 passing** (+1 from v4.0.5): `is_closed_class_covers_v4_0_6_attributives`.

### Scope discipline

One helper, nine new entries, one regression test. No rule changes, no world_core changes, no extractor-logic changes. Sequential 1вЖТ9 cadence preserved (v4.0.5 вЖТ v4.0.6 вЖТ v4.0.7).

### What's next (v4.0.7)

Axes continue to rotate per `project_v4_direction`:
- **World Core** expansion in an existing / new domain
- **New reasoning rule** R9 candidate
- More **noise elimination** if new classes surface
- **Corpus** вАФ long-horizon FST-synthetic data generation

## [4.0.5] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ Noise elimination in `temporal_after` subject selector

Continuing the v4.0.x curriculum вАФ one axis per patch, this one is **noise elimination**. Rotating axes keep new rule leverage (v4.0.4 R8) from compounding existing matcher precision gaps.

### Root cause

v4.0.4 spot-check showed R8 producing derivations like `(—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы, After, –∞–є—Л–њ)` вАФ the chain was mathematically sound but inherited a noisy base fact `(—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы, After, –ґ—Л–ї)` from `temporal_after`. Source: ¬Ђ–Х–≥–µ—А **—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы** –Њ—А–Љ–∞–љ–і–∞—А –Њ—Б—Л–љ–і–∞–є “Ы–∞—А“Ы—Л–љ–Љ–µ–љ –ґ–Њ–є—Л–ї–∞ –±–µ—А—Б–µ, 80-40 **–ґ—Л–ї–і–∞–љ** —Б–Њ“£ –ґ–µ—А –±–µ—В—Ц–љ–і–µ –Љ“±–љ–і–∞–є –Њ—А–Љ–∞–љ–і–∞—А “Ы–∞–ї–Љ–∞–є–і—Л¬ї. The matcher scanned left-to-right and grabbed the first bare-nominative noun (`—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы`, an attributive modifier) as the subject, when Kazakh SOV structure places the NP head (`–Њ—А–Љ–∞–љ–і–∞—А`) closer to the verb.

### Fix

Two tiny guards in `temporal_after`:

1. **Rightmost subject, not leftmost** (`(0..post_idx-1).rev().find_map(...)` instead of `(0..post_idx-1).find_map(...)`). In Kazakh SOV the subject-NP head sits closer to the ablative / verb, so the rightmost bare-nominative candidate before the postposition is the real subject.
2. **3-char minimum root length** (mirrors the guards already present in `locative_lives_in` and `dative_goes_to`). Blocks any truncated FST stems that might leak through.

### Measured effect

Re-ran extract + reasoner pipeline on the same committed 200 k-sample runtime:

| | v4.0.4 | v4.0.5 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json total | 13 889 | **13 887** | вИТ2 |
| After facts | 269 | 269 | 0 (net) |
| R8_after_transitivity | 789 | **714** | **вИТ75 (вИТ9.5 %)** |
| total derivations | 7 368 | **7 293** | **вИТ75** |
| graph nodes | 3 286 | 3 287 | +1 |
| graph edges | 12 447 | 12 439 | вИТ8 |

The rightmost-subject fix correctly narrowed the `(—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы, After, *)` class (from 2 вЖТ 1 base facts, with R8 transitive multiplication eliminated). Most of the 75 blocked R8 derivations came from that transitive multiplication.

### Honest observation вАФ adjacent noise class identified

The spot-check surfaced a **different** noise class still active at v4.0.5: attributive `-–ї—Л“Ы / -–ї—Ц–Ї / -–Є` adjective-derivations that the FST tags as nouns. Top offender: **¬Ђ–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї¬ї** (worldwide) вАФ 41 `After` facts in the committed runtime, typically from patterns like ¬Ђ–С—Ц—А—Ц–љ—И—Ц –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї —Б–Њ“У—Л—Б—В–∞–љ –Ї–µ–є—Ц–љ...¬ї where the REAL subject is elided (implicit event) and the grab-the-attributive heuristic still wins even with rightmost-scan because the head noun (`—Б–Њ“У—Л—Б`) sits in the ablative slot, consumed as the object.

Also seen: `—П–і—А–Њ–ї—Л“Ы` (nuclear, √Ч3), `”Щ—Б–Ї–µ—А–Є` (military, √Ч6), `“±–ї—В-–∞–Ј–∞—В—В—Л“Ы` (national-liberation, √Ч3), `–ґ—Л–љ—Л—Б—В—Л“Ы` (sexual / gender, √Ч2), `“±–Ј–∞“Ы` (long, √Ч9).

Fixing this requires a different tool: a narrow **attributive blocklist** for known -–ї—Л“Ы/-–Є adjective-acting roots. Queued for the next noise-elimination patch to keep v4.0.5 single-concern per the cadence rule.

### Curated temporal chains preserved

The 6 clean seasonal / daytime R8 closures from v4.0.4 are invariant under the rightmost-scan change вАФ they pass through a single-subject-candidate path where left-to-right and right-to-left identify the same token:

| subject | After | object |
|---|---|---|
| –Ї“ѓ–Ј | After | –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ |
| “Ы—Л—Б | After | –ґ–∞–Ј |
| “Ы—Л—Б | After | –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ |
| —В“ѓ–љ | After | —В“ѓ—Б |
| —В“ѓ–љ | After | —В–∞“£ |
| –Ї–µ—И | After | —В–∞“£ |

### Tests

**462 passing** (+1 from v4.0.4): new `temporal_after_picks_rightmost_subject_not_attributive` uses `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы –ґ—Л–ї–і–∞–љ —Б–Њ“£ ”©–Ј–≥–µ—А–µ–і—Ц` to verify that:
- The matcher picks `—Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы` (head of the NP), not `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы` (attributive).
- Object stays `–ґ—Л–ї` (ablative reference point).

Existing `temporal_after_extracts_noon_after_morning` continues to pass вАФ the single-subject-candidate case is invariant under direction change.

### Scope discipline

One concern per patch. Only `temporal_after` subject selector touched, no rule changes, no world_core changes. Sequential 1вЖТ9 cadence preserved (v4.0.4 вЖТ v4.0.5 вЖТ v4.0.6).

### What's next

Axes continue to rotate:
- **noise elimination**: narrow attributive-adjectival blocklist (`–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї`, `—П–і—А–Њ–ї—Л“Ы`, `”Щ—Б–Ї–µ—А–Є`, `“±–ї—В-–∞–Ј–∞—В—В—Л“Ы`, `–ґ—Л–љ—Л—Б—В—Л“Ы`, `“±–Ј–∞“Ы`) вАФ would knock out ~58 base After facts + their transitive R8 multiplications. Targeted v4.0.6.
- **reasoning rules**: R9 candidate ideas вАФ After anti-symmetry curator warning (R4-style), or Causes-transitivity with type guards.
- **world_core / Lexicon**: gap `–Њ—А–Љ–∞–љ` (forest) surfaced by this patch's test authoring вАФ new entries for nature domain.
- **corpus**: FST-synthetic clean data generation remains the long-horizon axis.

## [4.0.4] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ R8 After-transitivity rule (new reasoning rule)

One concern per patch вАФ this one adds a new rule to the forward-chaining reasoner: **`R8_after_transitivity`**.

### Motivation

`After` is a strict partial order вАФ mathematically the cleanest predicate to make transitive. The rule:

> `A After B вИІ B After C вЯє A After C`

mirrors `R1_is_a_transitivity` in structure but applies to temporal ordering instead of taxonomic subsumption. No semantic overreach risk вАФ unlike Has-transitivity (mixes ownership with composition) or LivesIn-transitivity (mixes residence with physical inclusion), temporal order is a mathematical relation that transits cleanly.

This aligns with the v4.x direction captured in memory `project_v4_direction`: **intelligent thinking via simple math** вАФ add rules with clear mathematical structure, not heuristics.

### Curated temporal chains now close automatically

`data/world_core/time.jsonl` asserts the primitive links:

```
time_011  —В“ѓ—Б After —В–∞“£
time_012  –Ї–µ—И After —В“ѓ—Б
time_013  —В“ѓ–љ After –Ї–µ—И
time_015  –ґ–∞–Ј After –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ
time_016  –Ї“ѓ–Ј After –ґ–∞–Ј
time_017  “Ы—Л—Б After –Ї“ѓ–Ј
```

R8 closes these into their full transitive closure. Measured on the live runtime (re-run of `run_reasoner` over the v4.0.3 `facts.json`, which is byte-identical вАФ only derivations change):

```
R1_is_a_transitivity:           361 вЖТ 361   unchanged
R2_has_inheritance:             422 вЖТ 422   unchanged
R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of:  26 вЖТ  26   unchanged
R5_shared_is_a_target:        5 437 вЖТ 5 437 unchanged
R6_lives_in_via_part_of:         36 вЖТ  36   unchanged
R7_goes_to_via_part_of:         297 вЖТ 297   unchanged
R8_after_transitivity:            вАФ вЖТ  789  NEW
вФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФАвФА
total derivations:            6 579 вЖТ 7 368 (+789, +12 %)
```

Curated-only R8 output (world_core-to-world_core chains) вАФ 6 clean temporal derivations:

| subject | `After` | object |
|---|---|---|
| –Ї“ѓ–Ј | After | –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ |
| “Ы—Л—Б | After | –ґ–∞–Ј |
| “Ы—Л—Б | After | –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ |
| —В“ѓ–љ | After | —В“ѓ—Б |
| —В“ѓ–љ | After | —В–∞“£ |
| –Ї–µ—И | After | —В–∞“£ |

Every step independently verifiable: e.g. ¬Ђ“Ы—Л—Б after –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ¬ї вЖТ chain `[time_017, time_016, time_015]` via `(“Ы—Л—Б, After, –Ї“ѓ–Ј) вИІ (–Ї“ѓ–Ј, After, –ґ–∞–Ј) вИІ (–ґ–∞–Ј, After, –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ)`.

### Known upstream noise observation

The remaining 783 R8 derivations inherit the precision profile of the **existing** text-source After extractor вАФ which pulls noisy subject roots like `—В—А–Њ–њ–Є–Ї–∞–ї—Л“Ы` (adjective surface mis-parsed) from `kazakh_textbooks_pack.json` and `wikipedia_kz_pack.json`. R8 transitively multiplies that noise.

Impact on users: **zero** вАФ both `adam_chat --safe` (v4.0.3) and `adam_demo` Part 4 default (v4.0.2) already filter to fully-curated source chains, so a text-source R8 derivation can never reach the dialog path. The noisy rows only exist in raw `data/retrieval/derived_facts.json` for audit.

The upstream cause вАФ `temporal_after` pattern matcher's subject selection lacking the content-noun / type-guard logic that `locative_lives_in` / `dative_goes_to` already have вАФ is a known target for a subsequent patch under the "noise elimination" axis.

### Tests

**461 passing** (+5 from v4.0.3): five new reasoner unit tests вАФ
- `r8_derives_after_transitivity` (single-chain positive)
- `r8_respects_tautology_guard`
- `r8_does_not_fire_without_chain`
- `r8_dedupes_against_existing_fact`
- `r8_chains_across_iterations` вАФ four-season full closure: –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ вЖТ –ґ–∞–Ј вЖТ –Ї“ѓ–Ј вЖТ “Ы—Л—Б produces (–Ї“ѓ–Ј, –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ), (“Ы—Л—Б, –ґ–∞–Ј), (“Ы—Л—Б, –Ї”©–Ї—В–µ–Љ).

### Scope discipline

One rule, one patch. No pattern-matcher changes, no world_core changes, no extraction changes. Sequential 1вЖТ9 per-integer versioning preserved (v4.0.3 вЖТ v4.0.4 вЖТ v4.0.5).

### What's next

The four knowledge-enrichment axes continue:
- **reasoning rules**: R8 landed. Future candidates вАФ R9 After-anti-symmetry curator warning, R-style rules over other predicates.
- **world_core**: expansion and new domains remain the main scaling axis.
- **noise elimination**: `temporal_after` subject guards as a dedicated patch (Codex-style precision audit).
- **corpus**: clean synthetic-data generation via FST is the direction per `project_v4_direction`.

Each patch is one step. Nine steps per major keeps the pace measured.

## [4.0.3] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ `adam_chat --safe` investor REPL mode

Continuing the Codex v4.0.0 hand-off. v4.0.2 landed the curated-only filter
in `adam_demo` Part 4; v4.0.3 extends the same guarantee to the live
`adam_chat` REPL via an opt-in `--safe` flag. Same design philosophy:
filter is a **view**, not an extract-time change.

### API additions

- **New pub fn** [`adam_reasoning::reasoner::derivation_is_fully_curated`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/reasoner.rs): the classifier moves out of `adam_demo` and into the reasoning crate so any dialog / inspection path can share it. `adam_demo` now re-exports via `use` вАФ zero duplication.
- **New field** `Conversation.curated_only_reasoning: bool` + builder `with_curated_only_reasoning(enabled: bool)`.
- **`inject_reasoning_chain` change**: when the flag is on, candidate derivations must pass `derivation_is_fully_curated` before the subject-first / object-fallback match. Fails through to retrieval (or plain Unknown) otherwise. Backwards-compatible when the flag is `false` (default).

### CLI

- `adam_chat --safe` (alias `--curated-only`) flips the flag at startup and logs `adam-chat: --safe mode вАФ reasoning chains filtered to fully-curated (world_core-only) source chains`.

### Measured вАФ real REPL output

```
$ adam_chat --once "–∞–±–∞–є —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В"
# Default (v4.0.2 baseline вАФ cites text-chain derivation):
–∞–±–∞–є —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –Љ—Л–љ–∞–і–∞–є –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б –∞–љ—Л“Ы—В–∞–і—Л–Љ: “Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л: –∞–±–∞–є вАФ —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы
# "Abai is a people." Text-extracted chain, Codex-flagged.

$ adam_chat --safe --once "–∞–±–∞–є —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В"
# v4.0.3 safe mode (cites world_core-only R1 transitivity):
–∞–±–∞–є —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –Љ—Л–љ–∞–і–∞–є –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б –∞–љ—Л“Ы—В–∞–і—Л–Љ: “Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л: –∞–±–∞–є вАФ –Љ–∞–Љ–∞–љ
# "Abai is a specialist." Derived from world_core/kz_literature:
#   lit_001  (–∞–±–∞–є IsA –∞“Ы—Л–љ)
#   lit_029  (–∞“Ы—Л–љ IsA –Љ–∞–Љ–∞–љ)
# R1_is_a_transitivity. Fully human-reviewed source chain.
```

This is the exact shape of an investor-safe pitch: every derivation goes through named reviewer + named rule, and a text-corpus chain that *might* be true never reaches the user.

### Tests

**456 passing** (+7 from v4.0.2):
- 5 new unit tests in `adam_reasoning::reasoner` covering the moved helper (curated / mixed / text-only / empty / prefix-boundary).
- 2 new e2e tests in `adam-dialog/tests/end_to_end.rs`:
  - `safe_mode_rejects_text_source_chain_derivations` вАФ default chats on text-chain; `--safe` refuses.
  - `safe_mode_still_cites_fully_curated_derivations` вАФ `--safe` continues firing on world_core chains (guards against overreach).

### Scope discipline

Exactly one feature вАФ the `--safe` chat flag + shared helper. No matcher changes, no extraction changes, no docs migration beyond the directly-affected files. v4.0.x cadence preserved at single-integer patch steps (v4.0.2 вЖТ v4.0.3 вЖТ v4.0.4).

### What's next (v4.0.4)

- Surface `--safe` mode in a refreshed README demo transcript alongside the default mode, so investors see both sides from one page.
- Continue the Codex precision-hygiene hand-off with the next small, single-concern patch.

---

## [4.0.2] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ Investor-safe demo mode: curated-source-chain filter in `adam_demo`

Small, focused patch continuing the Codex v4.0.0 hand-off. Same design philosophy as v4.0.1: a surgical fix at the right layer, not a broad architecture change.

### The problem

Codex flagged three specific chains in `adam_demo` Part 4 as public-demo-unsafe:

- `–∞–±–∞–є is_a —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы`  вАФ R1 transitivity via `wikipedia_kz_pack.json`
- `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ`  вАФ R1 transitivity chaining through metaphorical corpus text
- `—В–Њ–њ—Л—А–∞“Ы goes_to –і–µ–љ–µ`  вАФ R7 chain with cross-domain semantic collision

Each was technically correct вАФ FST-typed, fully `rule_id`-tagged, `source_chain` non-empty вАФ but the **text-extracted** facts feeding the chains had weaker semantic integrity than the hand-reviewed World Core entries they competed with. An investor looking at the demo would read "adam: Abai is a people" and stop listening.

### The fix

A demo-layer filter, not an extract-layer filter (recall preserved for downstream consumers):

- New helper [`derivation_is_fully_curated`](crates/adam-dialog/src/bin/adam_demo.rs): returns `true` iff **every** `FactSource` in the derivation's `source_chain` has a `pack` starting with `"world_core/"`. Empty chains fail closed.
- `adam_demo` Part 4 per-rule-representative picker now requires this predicate by default.
- `--all-derivations` dev flag bypasses the filter for audit / debugging.

### Measured effect

| | before | after | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| Total derivations loaded | 6 579 | 6 579 | unchanged (filter is a view) |
| **Fully-curated chains** | 4 753 | **4 753 (72.2 %)** | reported in Part 4 header |
| Rules represented in Part 4 default | all 4 firing | 4 curated rules (R1, R2, R3, R5) | R6/R7 have вЙ•1 curated chain but subject-uniqueness guard filters in current artefact |
| Codex-flagged bad chains | shown as R1/R7 examples | **eliminated** | all three had text-pack `source_chain` entries |

Post-v4.0.2 Part 4 per-rule-representative output (real):

```
  [R1_is_a_transitivity]    —В“ѓ–ї–Ї—Ц --is_a--> –ґ–∞–љ—Г–∞—А           (world_core/animals)
  [R2_has_inheritance]      “Ы“±—Б –ґ–Њ–ї—Л --has--> –ґ“±–ї–і—Л–Ј        (world_core/astronomy)
  [R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of]  “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы --has--> –±–∞—Б      (world_core/colors + body_parts)
  [R5_shared_is_a_target]   –±”©–ї—Г --related_to--> –Ї”©–±–µ–є—В—Г    (world_core/numbers)
```

The R5 sample вАФ ¬Ђdivision related to multiplication¬ї via shared-math-operation IsA target вАФ is an unusually clean investor pitch for math-driven reasoning. Every claim traceable to a named reviewer (`shaman` at this stage).

### Tests

**449 passing** (+5 from v4.0.1): 5 unit tests for the `derivation_is_fully_curated` helper covering curated / mixed / text-only / empty / prefix-boundary cases.

### Scope discipline

Part 4 `adam_demo` only. `adam_chat --safe` equivalent is deferred to v4.0.3 вАФ keeping each patch single-concern.

### What's next (v4.0.3)

- Wire the same curated-only filter into `adam_chat` behind a `--safe` / `--curated-only` flag. Dialog's `inject_reasoning_chain` currently scans *all* derivations; the filter belongs there too for investor-safe chat mode.

---

## [4.0.1] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ ¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?¬ї REPL path fix (Codex v4.0.0 review follow-up)

Small, focused patch closing the bug Codex caught in the v4.0.0 audit:
**¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?¬ї was still routed through `StatementOfLocation { city: "–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї" }`**
in the real `adam_chat` REPL despite the v3.9.5 `NOT_A_TOPIC` work. The
unit-level test `not_a_topic_covers_v3_9_5_additions` was passing, but it
exercised `first_noun_root` / `content_roots` вАФ not the ablative-scan path
in `detect_statement_of_location`. Two separate code paths, one covered,
one not.

### Root cause

FST analysis of "–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ" returns three parses (in deterministic order):

```
noun: –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї +Ablative
noun: –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї +Singular +Ablative
noun: –љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ
```

`detect_statement_of_location` scans parses for the first `Case::Ablative`
noun and returns its root as a city. Before v4.0.1 that was the stripped
stem `–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї`, and `NOT_A_TOPIC` only contained the full surface form
`–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ` вАФ so the skip-check matched nothing. Result: REPL reply
"–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ —В“±—А–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞" ("Do you live in –Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї?") to "–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?".

### Fix

1. Add `–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї` (the FST-stripped stem) to `NOT_A_TOPIC` in
   `adam_dialog::semantics`.
2. `detect_statement_of_location` now **skips any noun whose root is in
   `NOT_A_TOPIC`** at the case-scan step вАФ same filter the content-root
   path already uses. Covers ablative, locative, and `Locative+P1Sg`
   branches uniformly.
3. Mirror `–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї` in `adam_reasoning::patterns::is_closed_class` for
   cross-layer consistency.

### Regression test вАФ REPL path, not unit filter

New e2e test `nelikten_is_not_absorbed_as_city` in
`crates/adam-dialog/tests/end_to_end.rs` exercises the exact
`Conversation::turn(...)` path Codex reproduced:

- Turn 1: "–Љ–µ–љ “Ъ–Њ—Б—В–∞–љ–∞–є–і–∞–Љ—Л–љ" вЖТ `session.city = "“Ъ–Њ—Б—В–∞–љ–∞–є"` (baseline
  works).
- Turn 2: "–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?" вАФ `session.city` MUST remain "“Ъ–Њ—Б—В–∞–љ–∞–є" (not be
  overwritten with "–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї").
- Turn 3: fresh `Conversation`, bare "–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?" вАФ `session.city` MUST
  stay absent.

Pre-v4.0.1 Turn 2 failed the assertion. Post-v4.0.1 it passes.

### Verified in REPL

```
$ cargo run -p adam-dialog --bin adam_chat -- --once "–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?"
—В“ѓ—Б—Ц–љ–±–µ–і—Ц–Љ
```

(Previously: ¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ —В“±—А–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞¬ї.)

### Tests

**444 passing** (+1 from v4.0.0): the new e2e regression.

### Housekeeping

- `cargo clean` reclaimed **37.4 GiB** of `target/` artefacts (9.7 GiB
  вЖТ 42 GiB free). Standing procedure вАФ run before every release when
  free space drops below ~15 GiB.
- Project direction captured in `project_v4_direction` memory: develop
  on M2 8 GB without investors; synthetic FST-generated data + strict
  Kazakh grammar rules as the path to "intelligent reasoning via simple
  math"; sequential 1вЖТ9 per-integer versioning (v4.0.1 вЖТ v4.0.2 вЖТ
  v4.0.3 вА¶), no half-step jumps.

### What's next

- v4.0.2: reasoning-demo precision polish вАФ filter out the remaining
  Codex-flagged noise chains (`–∞–±–∞–є is_a —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы`, `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ`,
  `—В–Њ–њ—Л—А–∞“Ы goes_to –і–µ–љ–µ`) at the demo layer, not at extraction.
- v4.0.3+: continued patch-level hardening ahead of the next minor
  (v4.1.0) which will carry architectural additions.

## [4.0.0] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ World Core 500+ expansion + contradiction immune system + Codex-review response

**Major release.** Codex's v3.9.5 review correctly flagged that reasoning was scaling faster than precision вАФ ¬Ђ–±–∞–ї–∞ lives_in –Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц¬ї, ¬Ђ(–µ–≥–µ—А, DoesTo, –≥–∞–Ј)¬ї, ¬Ђ(–ґ–∞–ї“У–∞, GoesTo, –ґ–µ—А)¬ї, ¬Ђ–µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ¬ї were real chains in `facts.json`, not hypothetical. v4.0.0 addresses both ends of the problem: (1) **expand curated knowledge** to outweigh extracted noise via sheer IsA density, and (2) **add a contradiction immune system** that categorically refuses the classes of false derivations Codex exhibited.

### 1. World Core expansion вАФ 200 вЖТ 507 entries / 270 вЖТ 601 facts

Seven new domains authored by `shaman` at `approved` status:

| new domain | entries | facts | content |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `colors.jsonl` | 37 | 38 | primary colors, Kazakh traditional (–∞–ї—В—Л–љ / –Ї“ѓ–Љ—Ц—Б / –Ї”©–Ї—Б–Њ“£—Л / –±–Њ–Ј / “Ы“±–ї–∞), nature-color associations, kemperqosaq (rainbow), vision-domain |
| `numbers.jsonl` | 45 | 54 | digits 1вАУ10, tens 20вАУ90, 100 / 1000 / million / billion, basic operations (“Ы–Њ—Б—Г, –∞–ї—Г, –Ї”©–±–µ–є—В—Г, –±”©–ї—Г), even/odd, time units |
| `kz_literature.jsonl` | 60 | 69 | 18 authors (–Р–±–∞–є, –Ь–∞—Е–∞–Љ–±–µ—В, –Ц–∞–Љ–±—Л–ї, –Ь–∞“У–ґ–∞–љ, –®”Щ–Ї”Щ—А—Ц–Љ, –Ђ–±—Л—А–∞–є, –Ь“±—Е—В–∞—А, –Ю–ї–ґ–∞—Б, –Ь“±“Ы–∞“У–∞–ї–Є, вА¶), 7 works (“Ъ–∞—А–∞ —Б”©–Ј–і–µ—А, –Р–±–∞–є –ґ–Њ–ї—Л, “Ъ–Њ–±—Л–ї–∞–љ–і—Л, “Ъ—Л–Ј –Ц—Ц–±–µ–Ї, –Р–ї–њ–∞–Љ—Л—Б, “Ъ–Њ–Ј—Л –Ъ”©—А–њ–µ—И, вА¶), 12 genres (”©–ї–µ“£, –њ–Њ—Н–Љ–∞, —А–Њ–Љ–∞–љ, ”Щ“£–≥—Ц–Љ–µ, –µ—А—В–µ–≥—Ц, –і–∞—Б—В–∞–љ, –ґ—Л—А, вА¶), structure (—И—Г–Љ–∞“Ы, —В–∞—А–Љ–∞“Ы, “±–є“Ы–∞—Б, –њ–Њ—Н–Ј–Є—П, –њ—А–Њ–Ј–∞) |
| `food.jsonl` | 50 | 50 | bread (–љ–∞–љ, –±–∞—Г—Л—А—Б–∞“Ы, —И–µ–ї–њ–µ–Ї), meat (“Ы–Њ–є/—Б–Є—Л—А/–ґ—Л–ї“Ы—Л/—В“ѓ–є–µ –µ—В—Ц), Kazakh traditional dishes (–±–µ—И–±–∞—А–Љ–∞“Ы, –Ї—Г—Л—А–і–∞–Ї, —Б–Њ—А–њ–∞), dairy (—Б“ѓ—В, “Ы—Л–Љ—Л–Ј, —И“±–±–∞—В, –∞–є—А–∞–љ, “Ы–∞–є–Љ–∞“Ы, —Ц—А—Ц–Љ—И—Ц–Ї, “Ы“±—А—В, —Б–∞—А—Л –Љ–∞–є), fruits, vegetables, grains, beverages |
| `clothing.jsonl` | 35 | 35 | general (–Ї”©–є–ї–µ–Ї, —И–∞–ї–±–∞—А, —В–Њ–љ, —Ц—И—Ц–Ї), Kazakh traditional (—И–∞–њ–∞–љ, –Ї–∞–Љ–Ј–Њ–ї, —Б”Щ—Г–Ї–µ–ї–µ, –Ї–Є–Љ–µ—И–µ–Ї), headwear (—В–∞“Ы–Є—П, “Ы–∞–ї–њ–∞“Ы, –±”©—А—Ц–Ї, –Њ—А–∞–Љ–∞–ї), footwear (–Љ”Щ—Б—Ц, –Ї–µ–±—Ц—Б, –µ—В—Ц–Ї, –±”Щ—В–µ“£–Ї–µ), fabrics, jewellery |
| `proverbs.jsonl` | 40 | 43 | curated m–∞“Ы–∞–ї with embedded IsA / Causes / RelatedTo facts вАФ ¬Ђ–Х“£–±–µ–Ї —В“ѓ–±—Ц вАФ –±–µ—А–µ–Ї–µ¬ї, ¬Ђ–С—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ вАФ “Ы—Г–∞—В¬ї, ¬Ђ–Ґ—Ц–ї вАФ “±–ї—В –±–µ–ї–≥—Ц—Б—Ц¬ї, ¬Ђ–С—Ц—А–ї—Ц–Ї вАФ –±–∞–є–ї—Л“Ы¬ї, ¬Ђ–Р–љ–∞ вАФ –±–∞–ї–∞–љ—Л“£ –∞–ї“У–∞—И“Ы—Л “±—Б—В–∞–Ј—Л¬ї |
| `animals.jsonl` | 40 | 42 | predators (“Ы–∞—Б“Ы—Л—А, —В“ѓ–ї–Ї—Ц, –∞—А—Л—Б—В–∞–љ, –ґ–Њ–ї–±–∞—А—Л—Б, –∞—О), game (“Ы–Њ—П–љ, —В–Є—Ц–љ, –±“±“У—Л, –Ї–Є—Ц–Ї, –∞—А“Ы–∞—А), birds (–±“ѓ—А–Ї—Ц—В, —В—Л—А–љ–∞, –∞“Ы“Ы—Г, “ѓ–Ї—Ц, —В–∞—Г—Л“Ы, “Ы–∞–Ј, “ѓ–є—А–µ–Ї, —В–Њ—А“У–∞–є, “Ы–∞—А“У–∞, —Б–∞—Г—Л—Б“Ы–∞–љ), domestic (—Б–Є—Л—А, “Ы–Њ–є, –ґ—Л–ї“Ы—Л, —В“ѓ–є–µ, –Є—В, –Љ—Л—Б—Л“Ы), insects, taxonomy (—Б“ѓ—В“Ы–Њ—А–µ–Ї—В—Ц, –ґ—Л—А—В“Ы—Л—И, –ґ”Щ–љ–і—Ц–Ї, –Ј–Њ–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П, –Њ—А–љ–Є—В–Њ–ї–Њ–≥–Є—П) |

Plus existing 6 domains carried forward. **Total: 507 entries / 601 facts across 13 domains.** All 0-rejection on `validate_world_core`.

### 2. Contradiction immune system

Codex's v3.9.5 review surfaced specific false chains in `facts.json`. Each fix is targeted and test-enforced:

- **R6/R7 astronomical-target guard** (new in reasoner.rs): `is_astronomical_object` helper (–Ї“ѓ–љ / –∞–є / –ґ–µ—А / –Љ–∞—А—Б / —И–Њ–ї–њ–∞–љ / –Љ–µ—А–Ї—Г—А–Є–є / —О–њ–Є—В–µ—А / —Б–∞—В—Г—А–љ / —Г—А–∞–љ / –љ–µ–њ—В—Г–љ / –Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц / –≥–∞–ї–∞–Ї—В–Є–Ї–∞ / “Ы“±—Б –ґ–Њ–ї—Л / “У–∞–ї–∞–Љ—И–∞—А / –ґ“±–ї–і—Л–Ј / –∞—Б–њ–∞–љ –і–µ–љ–µ—Б—Ц / –Љ–µ—В–µ–Њ—А / –∞—В–Љ–Њ—Б—Д–µ—А–∞ / –Њ—А–±–Є—В–∞). R6 and R7 now refuse derivations where the target `C` is astronomical-scale. Closes `(–±–∞–ї–∞, LivesIn, –ґ–µ—А) + (–ґ–µ—А, PartOf, –Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц) вЯє (–±–∞–ї–∞, LivesIn, –Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц)` вАФ the homonymous ¬Ђ–ґ–µ—А¬ї (both "ground" and "Earth") cross-domain leak.
- **Object-side 3-char minimum** (locative_lives_in, dative_goes_to): mirrors the subject-side guard from v3.8.5. Closes `(–±–∞–ї–∞, LivesIn, “У–∞–љ)` where the FST emitted a `-“У–∞–љ` participle tail as a standalone root, and analogous `-—Л–љ / -—Ц–љ / -“Ы–∞–љ / -—Б—Л–љ` fragments.
- **`is_closed_class` expansion** (patterns.rs, 20+ new entries):
  - conjunctions: `–µ–≥–µ—А` / `–∞–ї–∞–є–і–∞` / `–±—Ц—А–∞“Ы` / `–і–µ–≥–µ–љ–Љ–µ–љ` / `—Б–Њ–љ–і—Л“Ы—В–∞–љ` / `–і–µ–Љ–µ–Ї` / `—П“У–љ–Є` / `”Щ–є—В—Б–µ` / `”©–є—В–Ї–µ–љ—Ц` / `—Б–Њ–љ–і–∞` / `—Б–Њ–љ—Л–Љ–µ–љ` вАФ closes `(–µ–≥–µ—А, DoesTo, –≥–∞–Ј)` ("if" as subject);
  - adverbial oblique stems: `–ґ–∞–ї“У–∞` / `—В–µ–Ї` / `“Ы–∞–љ–∞` / `“У–∞–љ–∞` вАФ closes `(–ґ–∞–ї“У–∞, GoesTo, –ґ–µ—А)`;
  - fragment-suffix standalones: `“У–∞–љ` / `–≥–µ–љ` / `“Ы–∞–љ` / `–Ї–µ–љ` / `—Л–љ` / `—Ц–љ` / `—Б—Л–љ` / `—Б—Ц–љ` вАФ defence in depth alongside the 3-char minimum above.

Three new regression tests: `r6_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`, `r6_still_fires_for_country_target`, `r7_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`. The "still fires for country" test is the key one вАФ the guard must NOT block legitimate `(person, LivesIn, city) + (city, PartOf, country) вЯє (person, LivesIn, country)` chains.

### 3. Measured impact at T4_200k

| | v3.9.5 | v4.0.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json (total) | 13 771 | **13 889** | **+118** |
| curated (HumanApproved) | 270 | **601** | **+331** (√Ч2.2) |
| extracted (Grammar) | 13 501 | 13 288 | вИТ213 (filter noise removal) |
| graph nodes | 3 151 | **3 286** | **+135** |
| graph edges | 12 317 | **12 447** | **+130** |
| **derivations** | **2 058** | **6 579** | **+4 521 (√Ч3.2)** |
| predicate coverage | 11/11 | 11/11 | preserved |

Per-predicate fact counts вАФ World Core drives structural gains, filters trim noise:

| predicate | v3.9.5 | v4.0.0 | delta | driver |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| **IsA** | 294 | **525** | **+231** | world_core breadth (13 domains вЖТ many IsA) |
| RelatedTo | 1 446 | 1 467 | +21 | |
| **Has** | 207 | 226 | +19 | world_core body_parts/society |
| **PartOf** | 105 | 116 | +11 | |
| **HasQuantity** | 29 | 40 | +11 | numbers.jsonl (year has 12 months etc) |
| **Causes** | 6 | **22** | **+16** | proverbs + biology entries |
| **InDomain** | 5 | **24** | **+19** | kz_literature genres + sciences |
| After | 269 | 265 | вИТ4 | |
| LivesIn | 313 | 292 | **вИТ21** | 3-char object filter + fragment-suffix closed-class |
| GoesTo | 1 692 | 1 617 | **вИТ75** | same filters |
| DoesTo | 9 399 | 9 295 | **вИТ104** | same filters |

Per-rule derivation counts вАФ **R5 and R1 jump from denser IsA graph; R6/R7 shrink as astronomical-guard blocks false chains**:

| rule | v3.9.5 | v4.0.0 | delta | reason |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---|
| R1_is_a_transitivity | 114 | **361** | **+247 (√Ч3.2)** | more IsA chains (world_core 507 entries вЖТ 525 IsA facts вЖТ dense A-IsA-B-IsA-C paths) |
| R2_has_inheritance | 253 | **422** | **+169** | denser IsA base |
| R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of | 15 | **26** | **+11** | body_parts PartOf chains |
| **R5_shared_is_a_target** | 933 | **5 437** | **+4 504 (√Ч5.8)** | 525 IsA facts form exponentially more sibling pairs sharing a target |
| **R6_lives_in_via_part_of** | 103 | **36** | **вИТ67** | **astronomical-target guard** blocked 67 false chains (`–±–∞–ї–∞ lives_in –Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц` class) |
| **R7_goes_to_via_part_of** | 640 | **297** | **вИТ343** | **same guard** вАФ biggest precision win |

R6/R7 shrinkage is a **net precision gain**: the 67+343 = 410 blocked derivations were chains where the target was an astronomical-scale object (homonymous ¬Ђ–ґ–µ—А¬ї bridging "ground" to "Earth", or adverbial "–ґ–∞–ї“У–∞" chaining through "–ґ–µ—А" to "–Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц"). Genuine `(person, LivesIn, city) + (city, PartOf, country) вЯє (person, LivesIn, country)` chains still fire, as verified by `r6_still_fires_for_country_target` test.

Most-connected graph nodes (content-noun focus preserved): **–∞–і–∞–Љ (289), –ґ–µ—А (219), –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ (211), “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы (201), –∞—В (150)**.

### Tests

**443 passing** (+3 from v3.9.5): `r6_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`, `r6_still_fires_for_country_target`, `r7_refuses_astronomical_derived_target`.

### Architectural stance вАФ Codex response

Codex's full recommendation included a Typed World Model with EntityType ontology, Fact Promotion Pipeline with `Candidate`/`Verified`/`HumanApproved` tiers, and a dedicated Contradiction/Absurdity Filter. v4.0.0 ships **targeted** fixes вАФ the filters are hand-coded blocklists rather than type-derived constraints вАФ because every hand-coded filter is test-enforceable today without gating on a larger ontology design. The Typed World Model is a v5.x target; v4.x adds incremental curated-knowledge breadth and domain-specific blocklists as new noise classes surface.

The architectural stance stays: **adam is not competing with ChatGPT on breadth.** v4.0.0's 507 curated entries + 600+ typed facts, each with a named reviewer, are measured against GPT-4's "correct answer" baseline: correct per-claim, traceable per-source, zero hallucination by construction. This is a substrate for sovereign-AI / education / government domains, not a general-purpose Q&A model.

### What's next (v4.5+)

- v4.5: investor-ready MVP вАФ scripted 3-minute `adam_demo_v4` narrative pulling **exclusively** from `HumanApproved` tier; screencast recording; one-page pitch deck.
- `validate_world_core` integrated into `scripts/validate_foundation.sh` as a CI gate (currently standalone).
- v5.x: Typed World Model вАФ EntityType ontology per root, type-constrained rule firing, Fact Promotion Pipeline with `Candidate`/`Verified` tiers that require evidence from multiple sources before promotion.

---

## [3.9.5] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ World Core expansion + R6/R7 rules + dialog closed-class sync

**Continuation of the v3.9.0 architectural direction.** Three independent improvements, each a small and contained delta:

### 1. World Core expansion вАФ 80 вЖТ 200 entries / 126 вЖТ 270 facts

Three new domains added by `shaman` at `approved` review status:

| domain | entries | facts |
|---|---:|---:|
| astronomy | 30 | 41 |
| time | 20 | 38 |
| geography_kz | 30 | 47 |
| **biology_basic** (v3.9.5) | **40** | **41** |
| **body_parts** (v3.9.5) | **40** | **55** |
| **society** (v3.9.5) | **40** | **48** |
| **TOTAL** | **200** | **270** |

Content:
- `biology_basic.jsonl` вАФ human, mammals, common animals (–Є—В, –Љ—Л—Б—Л“Ы, –ґ—Л–ї“Ы—Л, “Ы–Њ–є, —Б–Є—Л—А, —В“ѓ–є–µ, –µ—И–Ї—Ц, “Ы“±—Б, –±–∞–ї—Л“Ы), plants (–∞“У–∞—И, —И”©–њ, –≥“ѓ–ї, –±–Є–і–∞–є), terrain (–Њ—А–Љ–∞–љ, –і–∞–ї–∞, —И”©–ї, —В–∞–є–≥–∞, —В–∞—Г, ”©–Ј–µ–љ, –Ї”©–ї, —В–µ“£—Ц–Ј, –Љ“±—Е–Є—В), cell / organism, biology + ecology as sciences. 41 typed facts.
- `body_parts.jsonl` вАФ head parts (–±–∞—Б, –±–µ—В, –Ї”©–Ј, “Ы“±–ї–∞“Ы, –Љ“±—А—Л–љ, –∞—Г—Л–Ј, —В—Ц–ї, —В—Ц—Б, —И–∞—И), limbs (–Љ–Њ–є—Л–љ, –Є—Л“Ы, “Ы–Њ–ї, —Б–∞—Г—Б–∞“Ы, –∞–ї–∞“Ы–∞–љ, —В—Ц–Ј–µ, –∞—П“Ы, —В–∞–±–∞–љ), internal organs (–ґ“ѓ—А–µ–Ї, ”©–Ї–њ–µ, –±–∞—Г—Л—А, –±“ѓ–є—А–µ–Ї, –∞—Б“Ы–∞–Ј–∞–љ, –Љ–Є, “Ы–∞–љ), structural (—Б“ѓ–є–µ–Ї, –µ—В, —В–µ—А—Ц, –ґ“ѓ–є–Ї–µ), 6 quantified claims (¬Ђ–∞–і–∞–Љ–і–∞ –µ–Ї—Ц –Ї”©–Ј –±–∞—А¬ї etc), anatomy as a science. 55 typed facts.
- `society.jsonl` вАФ state / law / constitution / parliament / president / courts, family (–∞–љ–∞, ”Щ–Ї–µ, –±–∞–ї–∞), education (–Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ, —Г–љ–Є–≤–µ—А—Б–Є—В–µ—В, –Њ“Ы—Г—И—Л, –Љ“±“У–∞–ї—Ц–Љ, —Б—В—Г–і–µ–љ—В, –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ, –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ—Е–∞–љ–∞), sciences (–Љ–∞—В–µ–Љ–∞—В–Є–Ї–∞, —Д–Є–Ј–Є–Ї–∞, —Е–Є–Љ–Є—П, —В–∞—А–Є—Е), economy (–∞“Ы—И–∞, —В–µ“£–≥–µ, –µ“£–±–µ–Ї), professions (–і”Щ—А—Ц–≥–µ—А, –Љ“±“У–∞–ї—Ц–Љ, –Є–љ–ґ–µ–љ–µ—А, –Ј–∞“£–≥–µ—А), dimension (–і—Ц–љ, –Љ”Щ–і–µ–љ–Є–µ—В, —В—Ц–ї, “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Ц–ї—Ц, —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы). 48 typed facts.

All 200 entries pass `validate_world_core` with 0 rejections / 0 Kazakh-purity warnings.

### 2. R6 + R7 вАФ spatial + directional transitivity rules

Two new forward-chaining rules, activated now that v3.8.0's verb-root fix gave `LivesIn` / `GoesTo` real data AND v3.9.0's `geography_kz.jsonl` curated a `city PartOf country` chain:

| rule | formula | example |
|---|---|---|
| **R6_lives_in_via_part_of** | `A LivesIn B вИІ B PartOf C вЯє A LivesIn C` | (–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В, LivesIn, “Ъ–Њ—Б—В–∞–љ–∞–є) вИІ (“Ъ–Њ—Б—В–∞–љ–∞–є, PartOf, “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ) вЯє (–Ф”Щ—Г–ї–µ—В, LivesIn, “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ) |
| **R7_goes_to_via_part_of** | `A GoesTo B вИІ B PartOf C вЯє A GoesTo C` | (–Њ–ї, GoesTo, –Р–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л) вИІ (–Р–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л, PartOf, “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ) вЯє (–Њ–ї, GoesTo, “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ) |

Both emit `ConfidenceKind::RuleInferred` with a 2-source chain. Tautology-guarded (A вЙ† C). 6 new unit tests: `r6_derives_lives_in_via_part_of`, `r6_respects_tautology_guard`, `r6_does_not_fire_without_part_of_edge`, `r6_dedupes_against_existing_fact`, `r7_derives_goes_to_via_part_of`, `r7_respects_tautology_guard`.

Reasoner roster is now 5 active rules: R1 / R2 / R3 / R5 / R6 / R7 (6 total). R4 remains curator-warning only.

### 3. Dialog `NOT_A_TOPIC` sync вАФ closes ¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ вЖТ –Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ —В“±—А–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞¬ї bug

v3.8.5 free-form REPL testing surfaced: user typing ¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?¬ї (¬Ђwhy?¬ї вАФ an interrogative) got reply ¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ —В“±—А–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞¬ї (¬ЂDo you live in –Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї?¬ї). The FST analysed ¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ¬ї as `–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї` + ablative suffix (a valid morphological parse), and `adam-dialog::semantics::NOT_A_TOPIC` lacked the interrogative entries that `adam-reasoning::patterns::is_closed_class` had gained in v3.5.0+.

Fix: expanded `NOT_A_TOPIC` to mirror `is_closed_class` вАФ added interrogatives (`–љ–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ`, `–љ–µ–≥–µ`, `“Ы–∞—И–∞–љ`, `“Ы–∞–є–і–∞`, `“Ы–∞–љ–і–∞–є`, `–Ї—Ц–Љ`, `–љ–µ`, `“Ы–∞–є`, `“Ы–∞–љ—И–∞`), demonstrative qualifiers (`–Љ“±–љ–і–∞–є`, `—Б–Њ–љ–і–∞–є`, `–Њ–љ–і–∞–є`, `–Љ—Л–љ–∞–і–∞–є`, `—Б–Њ–љ–і–∞–є-–∞“Ы`, `–Ї–µ–є–±—Ц—А`, `”©–Ј`, `”©–Ј—Ц`, `–±—Ц—А–љ–µ—И–µ`, `–±–∞—А—И–∞`, `”Щ—А–±—Ц—А`, `”Щ—А`, `–±—Ц—А`, `–Ї–µ–є`), plus the comparison particle `—Б–Є—П“Ы` missing since v3.5.0.

Regression test `not_a_topic_covers_v3_9_5_additions` asserts every newly-added word is present AND that content nouns (–±–∞–ї–∞, –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ, –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ, “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ, –ґ–µ—А) still pass through.

This is exactly the "city-level facts + curated chain вЖТ country-level conclusions" leverage the v3.9.5 plan targeted.

### Tests

### Architectural status

At v3.9.5 adam has:
- **200 curated knowledge entries** вЖТ 270 facts with full reviewer provenance
- **5 active forward-chaining rules** (R1, R2, R3, R5, R6, R7) вАФ R6/R7 turn the clean v3.8.5-hardened LivesIn/GoesTo predicates into new derivations
- **11/11 predicate coverage** preserved from v3.9.0
- **Dialog intent layer synced with reasoning closed-class** вАФ one single source of truth for ¬Ђwhat is a content noun¬ї

Every curated fact is signed by a reviewer; every derivation has a source_chain; every refusal (¬Ђ–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ?¬ї) goes through an explicit filter rather than a heuristic. This is the shape of an **auditable Kazakh reasoning engine**.

- `validate_world_core` integrated into `validate_foundation.sh` as CI gate
- Extend `data/world_core/README.md` with per-domain authoring guides

## [3.9.0] вАФ 2026-04-23 вАФ World Core v1: curated Kazakh knowledge packs + hygiene gate

**Architectural direction captured.** Codex's second-pass review of v3.8.5 converged with our own assessment: the path to a ¬ЂChatGPT-class intellectual, but without probability / cost / hallucination¬ї is **not** to train an LLM-clone вАФ it's to build an *auditable Kazakh reasoning engine* on top of **curated knowledge packs**. v3.9.0 ships the World Core infrastructure that unlocks this path + closes the `-`-prefixed fragment noise Codex flagged on the facts.json graph.

### 1. Hygiene gate вАФ `-`-prefixed fragment roots refused

Codex measured 87 facts on the v3.8.5 committed `facts.json` where subject or object root started with `-` (artifacts of FST tokenisation splitting compound tokens like `2021-–ґ—Л–ї“У—Л` into dash-prefixed fragments). Top offenders: `-–і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ–ґ“ѓ–Ј—Ц–ї—Ц–Ї` (20), `-“У–∞` (8), `-–ґ—Л–ї` (6), `-“У–∞—Б—Л—А` (7), `-“Ы–∞` (6), `-–љ–∞–љ` (6). Every such root is categorically a suffix-fragment parse and can never represent a real entity.

Fix: central `is_fragment_root` gate in [`lib.rs`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/lib.rs) post-filter that rejects any fact whose subject or object root is empty or starts with `-`. Applies uniformly across all 11 matchers вАФ no per-matcher code churn needed. Regression test `is_fragment_root_rejects_dash_prefixed` covers the seven flagged patterns plus the boundary case of internal dashes (`—Б–Њ–љ–і–∞–є-–∞“Ы`, `–љ“±—А-—Б“±–ї—В–∞–љ` вАФ legitimate content) passing through.

### 2. World Core v1 вАФ curated Kazakh knowledge packs

New infrastructure that lets human reviewers inject high-trust typed facts directly into the graph, bypassing the precision ceiling of text-pattern matchers.

**Schema** (one JSON per line, one file per domain in `data/world_core/<domain>.jsonl`):

```jsonc
{
  "id": "astro_001",
  "kk": "–Ц–µ—А вАФ –Ъ“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц–љ–і–µ–≥—Ц “У–∞–ї–∞–Љ—И–∞—А.",
  "facts": [
    { "subject": "–ґ–µ—А", "predicate": "is_a",    "object": "“У–∞–ї–∞–Љ—И–∞—А" },
    { "subject": "–ґ–µ—А", "predicate": "part_of", "object": "–Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц" }
  ],
  "domain": "astronomy",
  "source": "curated",
  "confidence": "high",
  "review_status": "approved",
  "reviewer": "shaman",
  "reviewed_at": "2026-04-23"
}
```

**Crate surface** ([`adam_reasoning::world_core`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/world_core.rs)):

- `WorldCoreEntry` / `WorldCoreFact` вАФ serde-deserialising structs with stable JSONL form.
- `ConfidenceTier { High, Medium, Low }` вАФ reserved for reviewer discretion; `ReviewStatus { Approved, Pending, Rejected }` вАФ only `Approved` entries enter the runtime fact set.
- `load_world_core_dir(path)` вАФ parses every `*.jsonl` deterministically; returns a `LoadReport` with accepted + rejected entries (rejection reasons: parse failure, duplicate id, empty fact list, tautology, fragment root).
- `emit_facts(entry, path)` вАФ converts an approved entry into pipeline `Fact`s with `ConfidenceKind::HumanApproved` + `source.pack = "world_core/<domain>.jsonl"`.
- `load_world_core_facts(path)` вАФ convenience one-shot for the extract pipeline.

**New binary** [`validate_world_core`](crates/adam-reasoning/src/bin/validate_world_core.rs): authoring-gate validator. Prints per-domain summary (entries / approved / pending / rejected / facts), flags Kazakh-purity violations (any non-Cyrillic character in the `kk` sentence), reports all rejected entries, returns non-zero exit code if anything failed. Intended to run in CI alongside `validate_foundation`.

**Integration into `extract_facts`**: after scanning text corpus packs, the binary calls `world_core::load_world_core_facts("data/world_core")` and merges the curated facts into the same `artifact.facts` vector that text extraction populates. Per-predicate + per-pack counters are updated uniformly so the summary output lists curated packs (`world_core/astronomy.jsonl`, etc.) alongside extracted packs. Missing `data/world_core/` is a silent no-op вАФ trimmed CI checkouts behave identically to pre-v3.9.0.

### 3. Seed data вАФ 80 entries / 126 facts across 3 domains

Bootstrap content authored for v3.9.0 (all `approved` by `shaman` at `high` or `medium` confidence):

| domain | entries | facts | example |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `astronomy` | 30 | 41 | ¬Ђ–Ц–µ—А вАФ –Ъ“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц–љ–і–µ–≥—Ц “У–∞–ї–∞–Љ—И–∞—А¬ї вЖТ `(–ґ–µ—А, is_a, “У–∞–ї–∞–Љ—И–∞—А)` + `(–ґ–µ—А, part_of, –Ї“ѓ–љ –ґ“ѓ–є–µ—Б—Ц)` |
| `time` | 20 | 38 | ¬Ђ–Ц—Л–ї вАФ –Њ–љ –µ–Ї—Ц –∞–є–і–∞–љ —В“±—А–∞–і—Л¬ї вЖТ `(–ґ—Л–ї, has_quantity, –∞–є)` + `(–∞–є, part_of, –ґ—Л–ї)` |
| `geography_kz` | 30 | 47 | ¬Ђ–Р–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л вАФ “Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ–љ—Л“£ —Ц—А—Ц “Ы–∞–ї–∞—Б—Л¬ї вЖТ `(–∞–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л, is_a, “Ы–∞–ї–∞)` + `(–∞–ї–Љ–∞—В—Л, part_of, “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ)` |
| **TOTAL** | **80** | **126** | вАФ |

All 80 entries pass `validate_world_core`. `reviewer: "shaman"` is the bootstrap author handle; v3.9.5+ will introduce the native-speaker review workflow.

### 4. `adam_inspect` вАФ Curated vs Extracted split

The per-root report in [`adam_inspect`](crates/adam-dialog/src/bin/adam_inspect.rs) now separates facts into two sections:

1. **Curated (world_core вАФ HumanApproved)** вАФ shown first. Each entry prints the `domain`, the `(pack, sample_id)` provenance, AND the Kazakh sentence `kk` in quotes вАФ the audit trail is complete.
2. **Extracted (Grammar вАФ corpus text patterns)** вАФ shown after. Unchanged from v3.8.5.

The `is_curated` filter is `f.confidence == ConfidenceKind::HumanApproved` вАФ single-predicate dispatch, no ambiguity. Summary footer updated to count each tier separately.

### Committed runtime delta

| | v3.8.5 | v3.9.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| facts.json (total) | 13 627 | **13 627** | **0** (composition changed) |
| curated (world_core, HumanApproved) | 0 | **126** | **+126** (new tier) |
| extracted (text, Grammar) | 13 627 | **13 501** | **вИТ126** (fragment-root filter dropped 87 dash noise; small matcher re-runs on top) |
| graph nodes | 3 087 | **3 100** | **+13** |
| graph edges | 12 165 | **12 175** | **+10** |
| derivations | 205 | **704** | **+499 (√Ч3.4)** вАФ world_core IsA chains ignited R5 shared-target matching: 56 вЖТ **489** |
| **predicate coverage** | **9 / 11 = 81.8 %** | **11 / 11 = 100 %** | **+2 (Causes, InDomain)** вАФ world_core entries `astro_015/016` ("–Ъ“ѓ–љ –ґ–∞—А—Л“Ы –±–µ—А–µ–і—Ц" / "–Ъ“ѓ–љ –ґ—Л–ї—Г –±–µ—А–µ–і—Ц") activate `Causes`; `astro_024` activates `InDomain` |

Per-predicate fact counts after v3.9.0:

| predicate | v3.8.5 | v3.9.0 |
|---|---:|---:|
| DoesTo | 9 498 | 9 399 |
| GoesTo | 1 697 | 1 692 |
| RelatedTo | 1 449 | 1 446 |
| LivesIn | 315 | 313 |
| After | 275 | 269 |
| **IsA** | 162 | **219 (+57)** |
| Has | 189 | 190 |
| **PartOf** | 23 | **65 (+42)** |
| HasQuantity | 19 | 29 |
| **Causes** | 0 | **3 (first fire)** |
| **InDomain** | 0 | **2 (first fire)** |

Per-rule derivation counts at v3.9.0: R1_is_a_transitivity = **42** (was 33), R2_has_inheritance = **173** (was 116), R5_shared_is_a_target = **489** (was 56). R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of fires 0√Ч post-hardening (PartOf subject/object roots don't yet overlap with Has subject in the clean set; v3.9.5 adds more PartOf entries).

Most-connected graph nodes post-merge (content-noun focus preserved): –∞–і–∞–Љ (279), –ґ–µ—А (221), –і“ѓ–љ–Є–µ (210), “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы (200), –∞—В (156).

### Tests

**433 passing** (+10 from v3.8.5): 1 hygiene-gate regression + 9 world_core loader / validator / emitter tests.

### Trust invariants (test-enforced)

- `ConfidenceKind::HumanApproved` is **exclusive** to world_core; text extraction never produces it.
- `source.pack` starting with `world_core/` is **exclusive** to world_core; text-pack paths never overlap.
- `review_status вИИ {Pending, Rejected}` вЖТ entry does **not** emit facts (verified by unit tests `emit_facts_refuses_pending_entry` and `emit_facts_refuses_rejected_entry`).
- `Fact` dash-prefixed root вЖТ unconditionally refused (verified by `is_fragment_root_rejects_dash_prefixed`).

### Architectural statement

This release captures a deliberate direction: **adam is not competing with ChatGPT on breadth.** It is becoming an *auditable Kazakh reasoning engine* вАФ narrower than an LLM, cheaper by orders of magnitude, but provably unable to hallucinate (every output is a template / verbatim quote / FST synthesis / rule-derived chain with full provenance, now augmented with curated world_core facts each of which has a named human reviewer).

The long-term goal (v4.0.0) is a **5 000+ entry world_core** across 10+ domains, plus R6 / R7 rules (`LivesIn + PartOf вЖТ LivesIn`, `GoesTo + PartOf вЖТ GoesTo`) that fire on the clean v3.8.5-hardened predicate set. This makes the project a genuine commercial differentiator for the sovereign-AI / government-sector use case: **you can see exactly where every answer comes from, and no claim enters the runtime without a human's name attached to it.**

### What's next (v3.9.5)

- Expand world_core to 500+ entries across 6вАУ8 domains (add `biology_basic`, `society`, `numbers`, `colors`, `body_parts`)
- `is_closed_class` / `is_time_noun` / `is_location_root` sync across `adam-reasoning::patterns` and `adam-dialog::semantics` (closes the `–Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ–љ вЖТ –Э–µ–ї—Ц–Ї—В–µ —В“±—А–∞—Б—Л–Ј –±–∞` bug surfaced during the v3.8.5 free-form REPL test)
- Clean OCR noise filter on retrieval samples (rejects ¬Ђ–∞“Ы-¬ї, truncated stems)
- Community contribution workflow for native-speaker review

---

## [3.8.5] вАФ 2026-04-22 вАФ Precision hardening: Codex review response (doc drift, renderer morphology, matcher filters)

**Patch release addressing the [Codex / Antigravity review of v3.8.0](https://github.com/qazaq-ai/adam/issues).** Three categories of defect closed, each concretely flagged by the external reviewer:

### 1. Documentation drift (README vs architecture_v3 vs runtime)

- README table had **two contradicting rows** for "Reasoning rules active": one saying `4/5` (including R3), another (further down) saying `3 (R1, R2, R5)`. Removed the stale row.
- `docs/architecture_v3.md` still described **4 pattern matchers** and R3 as `documented, deferred` вАФ actual runtime has **11 matchers** and R3 has been active since v3.5.5. Rewrote both the matchers table and the rule table. Added R6/R7 as v3.9+ targets (LivesIn+PartOf, GoesTo+PartOf transitivity) now that the v3.8.0 verb-root fix gave those predicates real data.

### 2. Renderer morphology (`–∞—В–∞—Г-“У–∞` / `”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї-“У–∞` bug)

Pre-v3.8.5 `render_derivation_as_kazakh` (both in `adam-dialog::conversation` and in the `adam_inspect` binary) concatenated case suffixes with a literal dash: `format!("{}-“У–∞ ...", root)`. This produced two kinds of invalid Kazakh: **(a)** the dash itself (suffixes attach directly), and **(b)** wrong vowel harmony on every front-harmony root (`”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї-“У–∞` instead of `”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї–Ї–µ`). v3.8.5 routes every case suffix through `synthesise_noun(root, features)` вАФ the same FST the template realiser uses. Verified dative output for a representative set:

| root | dative |
|---|---|
| –∞—В–∞—Г | –∞—В–∞—Г“У–∞ вЬУ |
| ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї | ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї–Ї–µ вЬУ (front harmony + voiceless gemination) |
| –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ | –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ“Ы–∞ вЬУ |
| –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ | –Љ–µ–Ї—В–µ–њ–Ї–µ вЬУ |
| “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы | “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы“Ы–∞ вЬУ (voiceless gemination) |
| —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы | —Е–∞–ї—Л“Ы“Ы–∞ вЬУ |
| –ґ–µ—А | –ґ–µ—А–≥–µ вЬУ |

Regression test `reasoning_chain_uses_fst_synthesis_not_dash_concatenation` asserts positive FST form and negative absence of `–∞—В–∞—Г-“У–∞`.

**Known FST limitation discovered during fix (deferred to v3.9)**: `synthesise_noun` with `Case::Genitive` on a **vowel-final root** produces `“Ы–∞–ї–∞–і—Л“£` instead of `“Ы–∞–ї–∞–љ—Л“£` вАФ the `{D}{I}“£` archiphoneme template lacks the "after-vowel вЖТ –љ" rule that genitive requires. Ablative / dative / instrumental on the same roots are all correct. The renderer now sidesteps the bug by using dative in PartOf / Causes chains instead of genitive; the FST phonology fix itself is a v3.9 target (it affects 48+ existing FST roundtrip tests and warrants a standalone release).

### 3. Matcher precision hardening

Codex's live `adam_inspect` session produced three canonical noisy triples: `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ вЖТ lives_in вЖТ –∞—Г–Љ–∞“У—Л–љ`, `–Љ“±–љ–і–∞–є вЖТ goes_to вЖТ ”©—Б—Ц—А—Г`, `–Ї“ѓ–љ вЖТ goes_to вЖТ –ґ“±–Љ—Л—Б`. Each is a distinct failure mode:

- **Country as `LivesIn` subject**: "“Ъ–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ" can't reside anywhere вАФ it **is** a place. Added `is_location_root` (50-toponym allow-list of Kazakh countries / major cities / continents / major rivers) and refuse as `LivesIn` subject. Scope is intentionally conservative; widening to a full gazetteer is v3.9+.
- **Time noun as motion subject**: "–±—Ц—А –Ї“ѓ–љ –Ь–∞—Б“У“±—В –ґ“±–Љ—Л—Б“Ы–∞ –±–∞—А–і—Л" was producing `(–Ї“ѓ–љ, goes_to, –ґ“±–Љ—Л—Б)`. Added `is_time_noun` helper (–ґ—Л–ї / –Ї“ѓ–љ / –∞–є / —Б–∞“У–∞—В / –Љ–Є–љ—Г—В / “У–∞—Б—Л—А / —Г–∞“Ы—Л—В / —В”Щ—Г–ї—Ц–Ї / –∞–њ—В–∞ / –Ї–µ–Ј / —Б”Щ—В / –Љ–µ–Ј–≥—Ц–ї / —И–∞“Ы / –Љ–µ–Ј–µ—В / —В“ѓ–љ / —В–∞“£ / –Ї–µ—И / вА¶) and refuse as subject for `LivesIn`, `GoesTo`, **and** `DoesTo`. Pre-hardening these were **309 / 1864 = 16.6 % of all `GoesTo` facts**.
- **Demonstrative qualifier as subject**: "–Љ“±–љ–і–∞–є" / "—Б–Њ–љ–і–∞–є" / "–Њ–љ–і–∞–є" / "–Љ—Л–љ–∞–і–∞–є" / "—Б–Њ–љ–і–∞–є-–∞“Ы" / "–Ї–µ–є–±—Ц—А" / "”©–Ј" / "”©–Ј—Ц" / "–±—Ц—А–љ–µ—И–µ" / "–±–∞—А—И–∞" / "”Щ—А–±—Ц—А" / "”Щ—А" / "–±—Ц—А" / "–Ї–µ–є" all added to `is_closed_class`. Pre-hardening: 243 noisy facts across all predicates.
- **Object with leaked possessive suffix**: `(“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ, lives_in, –∞—Г–Љ–∞“У—Л–љ)` вАФ the object surface is `–∞—Г–Љ–∞“У—Л–љ–і–∞` but the FST analysis retains P3 possessive on the root (`–∞—Г–Љ–∞“У—Л–љ`), indicating a fragment parse. v3.8.5 refuses any `LivesIn` / `GoesTo` object whose FST analysis has `features.possessive.is_some()`.
- **Short broken stems**: added minimum subject-root length of 3 characters across `locative_lives_in`, `dative_goes_to`, `agent_verb` вАФ drops truncated FST outputs like `–Ї–µ—И–Ї`, `“Ы–∞“ї–∞—А–ї` that had been contaminating the committed fact set.

### 4. Demo preview / rendered-text mismatch

`adam_demo` Part 4 printed a per-rule preview like `[R5]  –љ–µ–Љ—Ц—Б вЖТ —Е–∞–ї“Ы—Л` but the rendered user-facing response used `–љ–µ–Љ—Ц—Б вЖТ –∞—А–∞` (a different derivation with the same subject appearing earlier in storage order). Root cause: the demo's selection was `BTreeMap<rule_id, first-content-subject>` while `inject_reasoning_chain` uses `find(subj == probe || obj == probe)` вАФ non-equivalent selectors.

v3.8.5 fixes both ends:
1. `inject_reasoning_chain` now does a **strict subject-first two-pass** (`find(subj == noun).or_else(|| find(obj == noun))`), matching the comment that was already there.
2. The demo's per-rule picker now **also tracks a `seen_subjects: HashSet<String>`** and skips derivations whose subject root was claimed by an earlier derivation in storage order вАФ so every preview points to the exact derivation the pipeline would render.

### Tests

**423 passing** (+7 vs v3.8.0): new matcher filters each get a regression test (`locative_lives_in_rejects_country_subject`, `dative_goes_to_rejects_time_subject`, `dative_goes_to_rejects_demonstrative_subject`, `is_closed_class_covers_v3_8_5_additions`, `is_time_noun_covers_standard_set`, `is_location_root_covers_countries_and_cities`), plus the renderer regression `reasoning_chain_uses_fst_synthesis_not_dash_concatenation`.

### Predicate coverage

Unchanged at **9 / 11** (LivesIn, GoesTo stay active вАФ the hardening tightens precision, not removes them).

### Upgrade notes

- Purely additive on matcher side вАФ no library API change.
- Fact-set shrinks (precision vs recall trade-off). `data/retrieval/facts.json` regenerated at v3.8.5. Downstream consumers expecting exactly 14 430 facts will see the updated count (tracked in `data/retrieval/facts.json`; README reflects the new number).
- Dialog renderer output surface changes for `Has` / `PartOf` / `Causes` / `After` / `HasQuantity` / `InDomain` chains вАФ suffixes are now properly inflected. The `unknown_with_reasoning_chain_cites_derivation` test still passes (it asserts on marker + root presence, not suffix shape).

### What's next (v3.9.0)

- Fix FST genitive-after-vowel phonology rule
- Extend location allow-list to full Kazakh gazetteer
- R6 (`LivesIn + PartOf вЖТ LivesIn`) / R7 (`GoesTo + PartOf вЖТ GoesTo`) rules now that the two predicates have data
- Full Codex-recommended **confidence tiers** (`High` / `Medium` / `Low`) on Fact + demo-only high-confidence subset
- Populate `docs/precision_audit.md` tally via native-speaker review pass

---

## [3.8.0] вАФ 2026-04-22 вАФ Critical verb-root bug fix: LivesIn + GoesTo activated (predicate coverage 7/11 вЖТ 9/11)

**Unlocks two dormant predicates that have been silently broken since v2.1 (LivesIn) and v2.5 (GoesTo).** The root-comparison checks used the **infinitive forms** (`"—В“±—А—Г"` / `"–±–∞—А—Г"`) while the FST stores verb **stems** without the `-—Г` suffix (`"—В“±—А"` / `"–±–∞—А"`). Neither matcher has ever fired, at any scale, on any corpus. v3.8.0 fixes the comparisons and widens the verb set.

### The bug

```rust
// pre-v3.8.0 вАФ never matches:
Some(Analysis::Verb { root, .. }) => root.root == "—В“±—А—Г",

// v3.8.0:
Some(Analysis::Verb { root, .. }) => matches!(root.root.as_str(),
    "—В“±—А" | "–Љ–µ–Ї–µ–љ" | "–Њ—А–љ–∞–ї–∞—Б"),
```

Verification via `cargo run -p adam-kernel-fst --bin adam_fst -- analyse —В“±—А–∞–і—Л`:

```
verb: —В“±—А +Present
```

Lexicon root is `—В“±—А`, not `—В“±—А—Г`. The pre-v3.8.0 code was looking for a root that could never exist.

### Fact delta at T4_200k (committed runtime scale)

| predicate | v3.7.5 | v3.8.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `lives_in` | **0** | **572** | **+572 (first fire!)** |
| `goes_to` | **0** | **1 864** | **+1 864 (first fire!)** |
| `does_to` | 11 216 | 9 865 | -1 351 (stopword list finally effective) |
| `is_a` | 162 | 162 | unchanged |
| `has` | 190 | 190 | unchanged |
| `has_quantity` | 19 | 19 | unchanged |
| `part_of` | 25 | 25 | unchanged |
| `after` | 278 | 278 | unchanged |
| `related_to` | 1 455 | 1 455 | unchanged |
| **Total** | **13 345** | **14 430** | **+1 085** |

The `does_to` drop is a **concurrent precision fix**: the `agent_verb` stopword list was using the same infinitive forms (`"–±–∞—А—Г"`, `"–±–Њ–ї—Г"`, `"–±–∞—А—Г"`) so the stopword filter was also never effective. v3.8.0 aligns it to the real FST stems (`"–±–∞—А"`, `"–±–Њ–ї"`, `"–Ї–µ–ї"`, `"—В“±—А"`, etc), correctly refusing those verbs as agent-patterns.

### Predicate coverage (committed runtime)

- **v3.7.5**: 7 / 11 вАФ IsA, Has, PartOf, RelatedTo, After, HasQuantity, DoesTo
- **v3.8.0**: **9 / 11** (+2) вАФ adds **LivesIn, GoesTo**
- Still at 0: Causes (v3.9 вАФ literal `—Б–µ–±–µ–±—Ц` head is rare), InDomain (v3.9 вАФ similarly rare head).

### Sample new facts

From `cargo run -p adam-dialog --bin adam_inspect -- “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ`:

```
outgoing: does_to=50, goes_to=8, is_a=2, lives_in=6, part_of=1, related_to=13
incoming: does_to=11, goes_to=14, lives_in=3, related_to=10

  `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ` --lives_in--> `–∞—Г–Љ–∞“У—Л–љ`  [pattern: X Y-–і–∞ —В“±—А–∞–і—Л; wiki_kz_...]
  `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ` --lives_in--> `“Ы–∞–ї–∞`     [pattern: X Y-–і–∞ —В“±—А–∞–і—Л; wiki_kz_...]
  `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы—Б—В–∞–љ` --goes_to--> `—Ц—Б`         [pattern: X Y-–Ї–µ –±–∞—А–∞–і—Л; wiki_kz_...]
```

### Regenerated committed artifacts

| artifact | v3.7.5 | v3.8.0 | delta |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `facts.json` | 13 345 | **14 430** | +1 085 |
| graph nodes | 2 974 | **3 091** | +117 |
| graph edges | 11 813 | **12 772** | +959 |
| `derived_facts.json` | 207 | **207** | unchanged |

**Derivations unchanged at 207**: R1/R2/R3/R5 only consume IsA/Has/PartOf predicates. LivesIn/GoesTo enrich the graph but don't drive the existing rules. **v3.9+ can add R6** (`LivesIn + PartOf вЖТ LivesIn`, spatial-inheritance) or similar to turn the new predicates into derivations.

### Most-connected nodes post-extraction

- `–ґ–µ—А` (degree 227) вАФ earth/ground
- `–µ–ї` (degree 211) вАФ country/people
- `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы` (degree 197) вАФ Kazakh (ethnic/linguistic)

All legitimate content nouns. No noise.

### Tests

**416 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** вАФ existing `locative_rejects_without_turu_verb` + `dative_rejects_without_baru_verb` tests still pass because they construct synthetic negative cases. **Note: these tests did not catch the bug** вАФ they tested that a sentence *without* the required verb is rejected, but never tested that a sentence *with* the verb produces a fact. Strengthening the positive-case tests is a follow-up.

### Honest note

This is a **2-year-old latent correctness bug**. The reasoning crate has been shipping with silently-broken LivesIn / GoesTo predicates since v2.1 / v2.5 respectively, across every release up to v3.7.5. Like the v3.2.0 parser-determinism bug and v3.3.0 stale-artifact issue, this is a case where **repeat extraction on a bigger corpus surfaced a structural flaw** that wasn't visible at small scale. The v3.7.0 `adam_inspect` binary would have flagged zero lives_in/goes_to edges for any probe вАФ worth noting for future per-predicate sanity checks.

### Banner sync per feedback_readme_pre_push_audit

  - `adam_chat.rs`: v3.7.5 вЖТ v3.8
  - `adam_demo.rs`: v3.7.5 вЖТ v3.8
  - README hero, comparison table, demo transcript all bumped

### Upgrade notes

- Purely additive on artifact side: existing IDs preserved, new facts appended.
- No library API change.
- **Behavioral change for embedders**: matchers now produce `lives_in` / `goes_to` edges that didn't exist before. Downstream code that enumerated `Predicate` variants in a match arm with `_ => panic!()` or similar will now see those variants. In-tree code is already prepared (variants have been defined since v2.1 / v2.5; render arms shipped in v3.5.0).

### What's next

- **v3.8.5** вАФ re-examine `agent_verb` false positives. With the stopword list now effective, the ~1 351 facts lost may reveal OTHER false-positive patterns now visible in the top-100.
- **v3.9.0** вАФ either (a) loosen `copula_causes` + `domain_membership` (push 9/11 вЖТ 11/11), or (b) add new rules R6/R7 (`LivesIn + PartOf вЖТ LivesIn`; `GoesTo + PartOf вЖТ GoesTo`) to turn the new predicates into derivations.

## [3.7.5] вАФ 2026-04-22 вАФ `adam_demo` Part 4 вАФ one derivation per rule (4-rule showcase)

Small polish release (per `feedback_versioning_post_1_0`: `x.y.5` = small). Refreshes `adam_demo` Part 4 to demonstrate **all four active reasoning rules** in a single run вАФ one representative derivation per `rule_id`, each with its own Kazakh-prose rendering carrying the ¬Ђ–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б-¬ї trust marker.

### Before vs after

**v3.7.0 Part 4** picked `derived[0]` and repeated the same chain across 4 seeds. Viewer saw one reasoning pattern four times.

**v3.7.5 Part 4** picks one representative derivation per `rule_id` (R1 / R2 / R3 / R5), probes each separately, and shows the variety of cognitive operations the system performs at the v3.6.5 committed scale (13 345 facts, 207 derivations).

### Concrete demo output (v3.6.5 committed pool)

```
Picking one representative derivation per rule id (4 total rules fired):
  [R1_is_a_transitivity]             –µ“£–±–µ–Ї  --is_a-->     ”©–Ј–µ–љ
    source_chain: proverb_068 + wiki_kz_0139793
  [R2_has_inheritance]               “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы  --has-->      –∞—В–∞—Г
    source_chain: wiki_kz_0001219 + wiki_kz_0118247
  [R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of]   –∞“£“У–∞—А  --has-->      ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї
    source_chain: wiki_kz_0079189 + wiki_kz_0081218
  [R5_shared_is_a_target]            –љ–µ–Љ—Ц—Б  --related_to--> —Е–∞–ї“Ы—Л
    source_chain: wiki_kz_0109606 + wiki_kz_0012411

вФАвФА R1_is_a_transitivity вФАвФА
  probe: ¬Ђ–µ“£–±–µ–Ї —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В¬ї
  seed  1 [chain]: “Ъ–Њ–ї–і–∞ –±–∞—А –і–µ—А–µ–Ї—В–µ—А–і–µ–љ –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б “Ы“±—А–∞—Б—В—Л—А–і—Л–Љ:
                   “Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л: –µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ (–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї –∞—А“Ы—Л–ї—Л).
  seed  8 [chain]: ...

вФАвФА R2_has_inheritance вФАвФА
  probe: ¬Ђ“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В¬ї
  seed  1 [chain]: ... –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї: “Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы –∞—В–∞—Г-“У–∞ “Ы–∞—В—Л—Б—В—Л –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—Л –±–∞—А
                       (–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ“±—А–∞–≥–µ—А–ї—Ц–Ї).

вФАвФА R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of вФАвФА
  probe: ¬Ђ–∞“£“У–∞—А —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В¬ї
  seed  1 [chain]: ... –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї: –∞“£“У–∞—А ”©—Б—Ц–Љ–і—Ц–Ї-“У–∞ “Ы–∞—В—Л—Б—В—Л –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—Л –±–∞—А
                       (–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ“±—А–∞–≥–µ—А–ї—Ц–Ї).

вФАвФА R5_shared_is_a_target вФАвФА
  probe: ¬Ђ–љ–µ–Љ—Ц—Б —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В¬ї
  seed  1 [chain]: ... –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї: –љ–µ–Љ—Ц—Б –∞—А–∞-“У–∞ “Ы–∞—В—Л—Б—В—Л –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—Л –±–∞—А ...
```

**All four probes surface the ¬Ђ–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б-¬ї marker.** The v2.7 trust invariant (test-enforced bi-directionally) still guarantees the marker never fires on retrieval-only paths.

### Implementation detail: content-noun filter

Raw `derived[0]`-per-rule selection hit a planner quirk: demonstrative / closed-class subjects like ¬Ђ–∞–љ–∞¬ї (that one) route through a non-Unknown intent and miss the reasoning-chain hook. Added a small demo-local filter вАФ `subject.root` must be вЙ• 4 chars and not in a demo-scoped closed-class list вАФ so each rule's pick actually lights up the chain. The v3.7.0 raw derivation pool is unchanged (still 207); only the demo's picking policy filters.

### Kazakh-prose variety

Each rule uses a distinct Kazakh sentence pattern:

- **R1**: `“Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л: <X> вАФ <Y> (–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї –∞—А“Ы—Л–ї—Л)` вАФ "conclusion: X is Y (via related thought chain)"
- **R2** and **R3** (both Has-producing): `–Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї: <X> <Y>-“У–∞ “Ы–∞—В—Л—Б—В—Л –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—Л –±–∞—А (–Є–µ–ї—Ц–Ї –Љ“±—А–∞–≥–µ—А–ї—Ц–Ї)` вАФ "thought chain: X has a connection regarding Y (ownership inheritance)"
- **R5**: `–Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї: <X> <Y>-“У–∞ “Ы–∞—В—Л—Б—В—Л –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—Л –±–∞—А ...` вАФ RelatedTo-flavour wording

Investor watching the demo sees **different cognitive operations** at the language level, not just four repetitions of the same sentence.

### Tests

**416 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** вАФ unchanged. Demo binary change is display-only; no library / pattern / rule surface touched.

### Banner sync

  - `adam_chat.rs`: v3.7 вЖТ v3.7.5
  - `adam_demo.rs`: v3.7 вЖТ v3.7.5
  - README hero, comparison table, demo transcript all bumped

### Upgrade notes

Purely cosmetic. No library surface change. Embedders and external CLI users see identical behaviour on `adam_chat` / `adam_inspect` / `extract_facts` / `scaling_bench`.

## [3.7.0] вАФ 2026-04-22 вАФ `adam_inspect` вАФ interactive intelligence query

New `adam-dialog::adam_inspect` binary вАФ the **interactive complement to `adam_demo`**. Where `adam_demo` runs a scripted 4-part walkthrough, `adam_inspect` takes a Kazakh root from the user and prints **everything adam knows** about it, traceable to `(pack, sample_id)` or `rule_id + source_chain`.

Concrete example (`cargo run -p adam-dialog --bin adam_inspect -- –µ“£–±–µ–Ї`):

```
# Graph position for `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї`
  out-degree: 18   in-degree: 16   total: 34
  outgoing: does_to=12, has_quantity=1, is_a=1, related_to=4

# Direct facts (extracted from corpus): 24 as subject, 17 as object
  `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї` --is_a--> `“Ы–∞–є–љ–∞—А`  [pattern: X вАФ Y; kazakh_proverbs_pack.json/proverb_068]
  ...

# Rule-derived facts (not in corpus вАФ inferred): 2 as subject
  `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї` --is_a--> `”©–Ј–µ–љ`  [R1_is_a_transitivity]
    source_chain:
      вАҐ kazakh_proverbs_pack.json / proverb_068
      вАҐ wikipedia_kz_pack.json / wiki_kz_0139793
    Kazakh: “Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л: –µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ (–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї –∞—А“Ы—Л–ї—Л)
  `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї` --related_to--> `“Ы–∞–є–љ–∞—А`  [R5_shared_is_a_target]
    ...
```

The R1-derived `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ` ("labor is a river") is a **conclusion not present in corpus** вАФ built by chaining `–µ“£–±–µ–Ї IsA “Ы–∞–є–љ–∞—А` (proverb) + `“Ы–∞–є–љ–∞—А IsA ”©–Ј–µ–љ` (wiki). Every hop has a `(pack, sample_id)` pointer. An investor typing any Kazakh content noun gets this kind of structured report over the 13 345-fact / 207-derivation committed runtime pool.

### Why this complements `adam_demo`

- **`adam_demo`** вАФ scripted, same 4 turns every run, good for recorded demos.
- **`adam_inspect`** вАФ interactive, user-driven, good for live "prove it" sessions.

Both tools load the same committed artifacts (no per-binary scale difference). Together they cover the two investor-demo modes: "watch a scripted narrative" vs "ask your own question".

### Sections of the inspect report

1. **Graph position** вАФ degree, per-predicate incoming / outgoing counts.
2. **Direct facts** вАФ every extracted `Fact` touching the root, capped at 10 per side, with the rest reported as "вА¶ and N more".
3. **Rule-derived facts** вАФ every `DerivedFact` the reasoner chained to this root, with full `source_chain` and a Kazakh-prose rendering carrying the ¬Ђ–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б-¬ї trust marker.
4. **Co-predicated neighbours** вАФ other roots that share an IsA target with this one (the R5-input surface вАФ useful for "who is similar to X" queries).
5. **Summary footer** вАФ one-line degree + fact-count + derivation-count recap.

For unknown roots the binary prints the 5 alphabetically-closest entries from the 2 974-node graph as "did you mean" suggestions.

### Implementation notes

- Pure viewer over existing `data/retrieval/*.json` artefacts вАФ no library-surface change.
- Kazakh-prose renderer is duplicated inline (avoiding a bin вЖТ bin dep on `adam-dialog::conversation`).
- 3 unit tests: nearest-key prefix match, empty-map edge case, all-predicates rendering coverage.

### Tests

**416 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** (413 baseline + 3 adam_inspect).

### Upgrade notes

- Additive. No library API change. Existing `adam_chat` / `adam_demo` unchanged.
- Cargo auto-discovers the new `src/bin/*.rs` file вАФ no Cargo.toml change needed.
- Banner sync: `adam_chat` / `adam_demo` / README `v3.6.5 вЖТ v3.7.0` per `feedback_readme_pre_push_audit`.

### What's next

- **v3.7.5** вАФ refresh `adam_demo` Part 4 to iterate over one derivation per rule type (R1/R2/R3/R5 showcase) rather than repeating the same derivation across seeds.
- **v3.8.0** вАФ native-speaker precision audit unblocks Lexicon PR.
- **v3.9.0** вАФ `occurrence_count` first-class field (Codex #4 follow-up).

## [3.6.5] вАФ 2026-04-22 вАФ Committed runtime scaled to T4_200k (first signs of intelligence)

Intelligence that was **stuck in a scaling_bench report** is now **surfaced in the interactive runtime**. Before v3.6.5, `adam_chat` and `adam_demo` loaded the committed 251-fact / 1-derivation snapshot; after v3.6.5 they load **13 345 facts / 207 derivations** covering 4 active rules. Human users interacting with adam finally see the scaling-law reasoning вАФ the same 200√Ч growth the T4_200k bench produced вАФ directly in their conversation.

### Primary goal: first signs of intelligence

Per user directive (¬Ђ–≥–ї–∞–≤–љ–∞—П —Ж–µ–ї—М вАФ –і–Њ–±–Є—В—М—Б—П –њ–µ—А–≤—Л—Е –њ—А–Є–Ј–љ–∞–Ї–Њ–≤ –Є–љ—В–µ–ї–ї–µ–Ї—В–∞¬ї): runtime reasoning needed to visibly scale, not just the bench numbers.

`adam_demo` Part 4 now produces outputs like:

```
Derivations available to cite:
  –∞“Ы–њ–∞–љ --related_to--> “Ы—Л—А–Ї“ѓ–є–µ–Ї       [R5_shared_is_a_target]
  –ґ–µ–ї—В–Њ“Ы—Б–∞–љ --related_to--> —Б”Щ—Г—Ц—А       [R5_shared_is_a_target]
  ...
  –µ“£–±–µ–Ї --is_a--> ”©–Ј–µ–љ                  [R1_is_a_transitivity]  (derived, not in corpus)

User probe: ¬Ђ–µ“£–±–µ–Ї —В—Г—А–∞–ї—Л –±—Ц—А–і–µ“£–µ –∞–є—В¬ї
  seed  1 [chain]: “Ъ–Њ–ї–і–∞ –±–∞—А –і–µ—А–µ–Ї—В–µ—А–і–µ–љ –±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б “Ы“±—А–∞—Б—В—Л—А–і—Л–Љ:
                    “Ы–Њ—А—Л—В—Л–љ–і—Л: –µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ (–±–∞–є–ї–∞–љ—Л—Б—В—Л –Њ–є-—В—Ц–Ј–±–µ–Ї –∞—А“Ы—Л–ї—Л).
```

The R1-derived ¬Ђ–µ“£–±–µ–Ї вАФ ”©–Ј–µ–љ¬ї ("labor is a river" вАФ metaphorical transitivity) is a **conclusion the corpus does not directly state** вАФ constructed from chained Is-A facts via the reasoning rule. It's the first time a user-interactive turn surfaces a rule-inferred claim.

### New flags on `extract_facts`

- `--bench-order` вАФ switches pack walk from Tatoeba-first (v2.1 default) to fact-dense-first (Abai вЖТ proverbs вЖТ classics вЖТ textbooks вЖТ Wikipedia вЖТ вА¶), matching `adam-scaling::CANONICAL_COMMITTED_PACKS`.
- `--max-total <N>` вАФ caps total samples scanned across all packs; per-pack `--limit` can still apply as a secondary cap.

Combined: `extract_facts --bench-order --max-total 200000` produces a committed fact pool equivalent to the `scaling_bench` T4_200k tier.

### Precision tightening: `—Б–Є—П“Ы`

First T4-scale run showed `—Б–Є—П“Ы` (comparison particle, the bare root of `—Б–Є—П“Ы—В—Л` "like / as") most-connected with **341 edges** вАФ all false positives because the `is_closed_class` check matched `—Б–Є—П“Ы—В—Л` but not the bare `—Б–Є—П“Ы` root. Added `—Б–Є—П“Ы` to closed-class; re-ran extraction. **-395 false-positive DoesTo facts** removed (13 740 вЖТ 13 345, -2.9 %). Most-connected after fix: `–∞–і–∞–Љ` (237), `–µ–ї` (209), `–∞—В` (186), `–ґ–µ—А` (176), `“Ы–∞–Ј–∞“Ы` (170) вАФ all legitimate content nouns.

### Regenerated committed artifacts

| artifact | v3.6.0 | v3.6.5 | factor |
|---|---:|---:|---|
| `facts.json` (size) | 125 KB | **8.8 MB** | √Ч70 |
| `facts.json` (fact count) | 251 | **13 345** | **√Ч53** |
| `lexical_graph.json` nodes | 373 | **2 974** | √Ч8 |
| `lexical_graph.json` edges | 244 | **11 813** | √Ч48 |
| `derived_facts.json` derivations | 1 | **207** | **√Ч207** |

**All under 50 MB gitignore threshold** (per `feedback_git_ignore_policy`) вАФ stays committed to git.

### Rule activations on committed runtime

| rule | derivations |
|---|---:|
| `R1_is_a_transitivity` | 33 |
| `R2_has_inheritance` | 116 |
| `R3_has_inheritance_via_part_of` | 2 |
| `R5_shared_is_a_target` | 56 |
| **Total** | **207** |

**First release where all 4 active rules fire simultaneously on the committed runtime pool** вАФ not just in bench reports.

### Precision audit

`docs/precision_audit.md` regenerated with **50-fact / 50-derivation sample** (was 17/1 at v3.6.0). Native-speaker review surface is now meaningful.

### Tests

**413 passing, 0 failing, 0 warnings** вАФ no test changes.

### Upgrade notes

- `adam_chat` / `adam_demo` automatically surface the bigger pool. No code change in dialog crates.
- `extract_facts` default behaviour unchanged вАФ new flags opt-in.
- Existing `facts.json` readers downstream see bigger file; all existing readers load-then-iterate, no schema assumption.
- `adam_demo` Part 4 picks `derived[0]` dynamically вАФ will pick a different derivation post-upgrade (previously –Ї—Ц—В–∞–њ/—Ц–ї—Ц–Љ; now the first-by-subject-root derivation from the sorted 207-pool).

### What's next

- **v3.7.0** вАФ `--persist-tier` on `scaling_bench` + `adam_chat --facts-tier` flag for ad-hoc tier switching.
- **v3.8.0** вАФ native-speaker precision audit unblocked; Lexicon PR using v3.4.0 candidates.
- **v3.9.0** вАФ `occurrence_count` first-class field (Codex #4 follow-up).
